M3你能给我解释一下这个计划吗？是说只要我加入了这个计划，你们就能帮我找到工作吗？它需要我去做什么呢？Can you explain this plan to me? Are you saying that you will find a job for me if I join this plan? What does it require me to do?
E4An Employment Pathway Plan explains what you need to do to get a job or improve your employment prospects and what assistance you will be offered. The plan may contain activities such as job searching, vocational education and participation in a labour market program.44
M4所以，这个计划是由你们制定的，然后要求我严格遵行吗？如果我觉得某些活动不适合我怎么办？这样听起来似乎是你们想控制我的生活。So is it true that this plan is made by you, and then I need to follow it strictly? What if I think some activities are not suitable for me? It sounds like you will control my life.
E5That’s not how it works. We’ll negotiate with you to identify your individual needs but once we have both agreed on the plan you’ll have to participate in the activities. Otherwise, there will be a ‘No Show, No Pay’ penalty.40
M5既然是根据我的个人需求制定的计划，我肯定会欣然执行。我会珍惜一切机会来提高我找到工作的可能性。As long as it’s based on my personal needs, then I will definitely be happy to abide by it. I would appreciate every opportunity to improve my chances of getting employed.
E6Excellent. Please be aware that you need to regularly send us evidence that you are actively looking for suitable paid work and attend all job interviews.26
M6好的，我知道了。你能跟我说一下津贴的数额有多少？我如果偶尔做一些临时工，会不会影响我领取福利金？Alright, I see. Can you tell me how much the allowance is? If I occasionally do some casual work, will it affect my payment?

entry.title: 盗版光盘
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr Philip, an officer from Customs and Border Protection and Mr. Lin, an Australian Citizen who just came back from a recent trip to China and has now arrived at Sydney International Airport.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Excuse me, sir. Can I see your passport and your Incoming Passenger Card please?
M1怎么了? 我没带什么不合法的东西，缉毒犬也已经过来闻过一遍了，没有任何特殊的反应。你们为什么把我拦下？What happened? I don’t have anything illegal with me and a sniffer dog has already checked around and it did not show any particular reactions. Why have you you stopped me?
E2The X-ray shows there may be some undeclared prohibited items in your baggage. If you are caught carrying undeclared, prohibited or restricted goods, you could be fined or prosecuted. Do you understand?
M2我既没有走私，也没带任何的违禁品。不过，您看我的行李都捆绑好了，要是把东西都拿出来再放回去是很费劲的。你们海关就是小题大做，专找我们亚洲人的麻烦。I’m neither smuggling, nor carrying any contraband. You see my luggage has been strapped up. It will be very troublesome if I have to take everything out and put it back in again. You Customs officers are just making a fuss about everything and picking on us Asians.
E3Do you know there are certain restricted goods you need to declare on arrival, such as firearms, weapons and ammunition, and currency of AUD$10,000 or over? Do any of those apply to you? Besides, if you are carrying certain kinds of medicines and substances, food, biological goods, or wildlife products etc, you must declare them.
M3这样啊。我就带了一些土特产给我父亲，他说他很想家，很怀念家乡的味道。 另外还有一些光碟是给我母亲的。你把我拦下来，是因为怀疑这些光碟是盗版的吗？I see. I only brought some local specialties for my father, because he said he was homesick and missing the local flavours of our hometown. Apart from that, there are some DVDs for my mother. Are you suspecting the DVDs are pirated? Is that why you stopped me here?
E4It is a normal practice for customs to run random luggage checks. Those DVDs have to be forfeited as they appear to be counterfeit. So does the local special produce that you have, since they are meat products. What is in those paper bags? Can you please open them?
M4那好吧，反正也不值什么钱。纸袋里没什么违禁的东西，就是一点草药。我妈 有慢性支气管炎，西药不管用，只治标不治本。所以我就在国内的中药店抓了一点草药给她。Alright. Those DVDs are not worth much. There is nothing illicit in those bags, just some herbs. My mom has chronic bronchitis, but western medicine doesn’t seem to be working for her. It only relieves the symptoms but does not cure the disease completely. So I bought some herbs for her at a pharmacy when I was in China.
E5You should have declared that traditional medicine on your Incoming Passenger Card, because they may contain endangered plant or animal products. I’m sorry, but that medicine also needs to be forfeited.
M5请不要这样。她需要这些药来治病的。您这不是要她的命吗？如果我交罚款, 你是不是就不会没收这些药了？Please don’t do that. They are very important for her treatment. Only if you want to kill her. Can I keep the medicine if I choose to pay a fine?
E6I don’t think so. It is advisable that you take her prescriptions to an Australian medical practitioner. I’m sure Chinese medicine practitioners here can help her with this matter.
M6随便你吧，看来也只能自认倒霉了。Whatever… it seems I have to accept this as bad luck.

entry.title: 就业赞助
entry.content:
A lady is talking to a shop manager, asking about the possibility of employment sponsorship.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，你是这家按摩店的经理吗？我是 Lisa.Hello,areyouthemanagerofthismassageshop?MynameisLisa.
M1Yes I am. How are you? How can I help you, Lisa?
E2我是一名按摩师，在中国有将近 10 年的工作经验。听说你这儿在招聘，所以我过来试一试运气。I’m a masseuse with nearly 10 years work experience in China. I heard that you are hiring, so I’m here to try my luck.
M2Yes, currently we are short of experienced staff. I believe you understand that one requirement for this position is being able to communicate with clients.
E3当然了。我正在上英语课，也达到了一定的水平。但为了保证今天和你沟通的质量，我觉得还是通过口译员比较好。Of course. I’m taking an English course and have made some progress. But I thought I’d better speak to you via an interpreter to maintain smooth communication.
M3That’s fine. You will have to be familiar with the terminology related to remedial massage and aware of the specific needs of our clients. They’ll be more willing to come back if you can go the extra mile.
E4我明白。我之前一直在做按摩，所以我深知客人的不同需求。我现在非常想在这里定居。我真的不适合呆在大城市，我喜欢小镇的宁静与安逸。I understand. I’ve been doing this for a while so I know about the varying demands of customers. Now, I’d like to settle down here. I’m really not a big city person. I prefer peaceful and cozy small towns
M4Well, I suppose you’re interested in the Regional Sponsored Migration Scheme. I heard of this at a massage association meeting. They said under this scheme, qualified massage therapists can migrate here permanently.
E5是的，我之前也去中介咨询过。他们说我必须有这里的学历，所以我就去一家学院学了一个证书，并通过了这里的技术评估。Yes, I consulted an agent before, who said I had to have a local qualification. So I got a certificate at an institute and passed the skill assessment.
M5If we are to sponsor you, it will involve a financial commitment on our part as well as the time needed to prepare the application. We really need some time to consider it.
E6我完全理解。这样，我把我的简历给你，上面有我的联系方式，你们随时可以联系我。我也可以先工作，担保的是我们慢慢来。I totally understand. Let me give you my resume first, which has my contact details. You can contact me anytime. I can also start working first and discuss the issue of sponsorship later.
M6That would be perfect. Thank you very much. I’ll be in touch with you.

entry.title: 亲戚移民
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Li and Herbert, a migration agent. Mr. Li is seeking advice on sponsoring an overseas relative to come to Australia to work.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello Mr. Li, you said to me last time that you wanted to sponsor overseas workers to come here, right?
M1是的。我在悉尼拥有一家食品加工厂，生产各种中式点心。我希望我的侄子能来澳洲帮我做事。Yes. I own a food processing factory in Sydney, producing various kinds of Chinese dim sims. I hope my nephew can come here and work for me.
E2Both the employer and the employee need to meet certain requirements to be eligible for the employer nomination scheme. As the employer, you must be actively and lawfully operating a business in Australia.
M2这个肯定没问题，我这个厂已经开了快 10 年了。我的产品非常受欢迎，你在各个华人超市都能买到。我的年营业额有四百多万呢。This is not a problem at all. I’ve been operating the factory for almost 10 years. My products are very popular and you can find them in every Chinese supermarket. The annual turnover is more than $4 million.
E3Then the position you provide must be full time, ongoing and available for at least three years. It must also be a highly skilled occupation that is on the Employer Nomination Scheme Occupation List (ENSOL).
M3这份清单是什么？我没听说过。我给我侄子安排的职位是食品技术员，您能帮我查查这个职位是否在这份清单上吗？What’s this list? I have never heard of it. The position I’m going to offer my nephew is food technologist. Could you check for me and see if it’s on the list?What’s this list? I have never heard of it. The position I’m going to offer my nephew is food technologist. Could you check for me and see if it’s on the list?
E4You can only nominate overseas workers to fill a position in an occupation that appears on the ENSOL. Let me have a look. Well, food technologist is on the list. That’s very good!
M4那我的侄子需要满足哪些条件呢？他在国内大学学的就是食品科技，毕业后至今已经有了五年的相关工作经验。在技术与资质方面 他应该是没问题的。What requirements does my nephew need to meet? He studied food technology at university in China and has five years’ relevant working experience after graduation. There shouldn’t be any problems in terms of his skills and qualifications.
E5That seems good enough. You should also be aware that he needs to meet the vocational English language requirements. That is to say, he needs to score 5 in each of the four sections of the IELTS test.
M5我不太了解他的英语水平。不过雅思 5 分对于他这样一个读过大学的人来说应该不是什么难事吧。I’m not sure about his English level. However, for a university graduate, scoring 5 in the IELTS test should not be very difficult, I reckon.
E6Other requirements are more general. For example, your nephew needs to show evidence of good health and character and sign the Australian Values Statement.
M6多谢你啦，让我有了一个大致的概念。看来这并没有我想像的困难。Thank you for giving me a rough idea. It seems like it’s not as difficult as I thought it would be.

entry.title: 母亲移民
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Lu, a new migrant, and Henry, an immigration officer. Mr. Lu is seeking advice regarding bringing his mother to live in Australia.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Sorry for keeping you waiting for so long. How can I help you today?
M1我希望你可以帮到我。我想要一些资讯关于我的母亲是不是可以在澳洲和我的家人生活在一起。I do hope you can help me. I’d like to have some information regarding whether my mother can come and live with my family in Australia.
E2Well first of all, are you an Australian citizen or permanent resident? Also, if you don’t mind me asking, how old is your mother?我从 1999 年就已经是澳洲公民了。我的父亲不久前刚去世, 留下我
M2母亲一个人生活。到三月份我母亲就 63 岁了。I have been an Australian citizen since 1999. My father passed away not long ago, leaving my mother alone. My mother will turn 63 in March.
E3In that case, she may be eligible to apply for a parent visa to come to Australia as an aged parent. But she also needs to pass the family balance test.
M3你能不能给我解释一下什么是家庭平衡测试？我听说过，但是并不是真正的明白。Could you explain to me what the family balance test is? I have heard of it but I don’t really understand.
E4Basically to pass the family balance test, at least half of your mother’s children must be living in Australia or more of them live in Australia than any other single country.
M4我明白了。我有一个妹妹她生活在墨尔本，和一个哥哥住在中国。我母亲没有其他的子女或继子女了。I see. I have a younger sister living in Melbourne and an older brother living in China. My mother doesn’t have any other children or step-children.
E5Well if that is the case, it makes your mother eligible to apply to come here as an aged parent. She needs to complete and sign an application form. I would also suggest that you buy a copy of our parent migration booklet.
M5行，我会先把表格里该填都帮她填好，然后再寄回中国给她签字。 可是，为什么我需要买一本小册子呢？Ok. I will fill out everything on the application form for her first, and then send it to China so she can sign it. But why do I need to buy a booklet?
E6It contains the information you need regarding applying for a parent visa. It lists the basic requirements that must be met and sponsorship information and contains the application form and a checklist of supporting documents.
M6我明白了。好吧。我买一份。告诉我，一旦你们收到她的申请表，申请的处理过程需要多长时间？申请费要多少钱？I see. Ok, I’ll take one. Could you tell me, once you receive her application, how long will it take to process? How much is the application fee?
E7We can’t begin processing her application unless the application form is signed and every question is answered. Also, the supporting documents must be attached and the visa application fee must be paid. The current fee is 1,470 Australian dollars and there’s no refund given for unsuccessful applications.
M7我明白了。那么，如果我们递交了所有的文件和表格，并且付了申请费。申请受理要多长时间？I understand. So how long is the processing time once we have submitted all the documents and forms and paid the fee?
E8I’m afraid that the average timeframe for processing applications in the aged parent category is currently 24 to 48 months.
M8我明白了。好的，我要一份小册子。再回家和我的家人商量一下。谢谢。All right. I see. I’ll take a booklet. Then I’ll go home and discuss it with my family. Thank you.

entry.title: 入籍咨询
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Guan and Allan, an immigration officer. Mr Guan is enquiring about applying for Australian citizenship.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，我今天来是想咨询一下入籍的事。我有几个问题想向你请教。Hello! I’m here today to enquire about citizenship. I’ve got some questions to ask you.
M1Sure, I can help you with that. I’m Allan, a citizenship department officer. Please go ahead.
E2我去年 2 月成为了永久居民。我现在在犹豫要不要入籍。你可以告诉我成为澳大利亚公民有哪些好处呢？I became a permanent resident last February. Now I’m considering whether I should apply for citizenship. Can you tell me the benefits of becoming an Australian citizen?
M2Australian citizenship offers a range of benefits. You can vote in federal and state or territory elections and in a referendum and seek election to parliament. You can also apply for work in the Australian Public Service or in the Australian Defence Force.
E3同样，我也可以持有澳大利亚护照了吧。凭着这本护照，我可以免签去很多国家，对吗？我对于我来说是个比较实际的好处。Also I can receive an Australian passport with which I can visit many countries visa-free, right? For me this is a real benefit.
M3That’s correct. An Australian passport will enable you to visit over 100 countries on a visa-free basis. Also, you can receive help from Australian consular and embassy officials while overseas.
E4嗯…那何时可以申请入籍呢？是当我成为永久居民满两年吗？还是有其他什么具体规定？Hmm… Then when can I apply for Australian citizenship? Is it after I have been a permanent resident for two years or are there other conditions?
M4Actually, you have to meet the residence requirements. This means you must have 4 years lawful residence in Australia. This period must include 12 months as a permanent resident immediately before making an application for Australian citizenship. Also, you must have absences from Australia of no more than 12 months in total in the 4 years prior to application, including not more than 90 days in the 12 months immediately prior to the application.
E5除了我的身份证件以外，我还需要递交哪些文件呢？是否需要无犯罪记录证明呢？这个申请不会向永久居留的申请那样等那么久吧？What documents should I submit besides my identification documents? Do I need to provide a certificate of no criminal record? Will the processing time of this application be as long as the application for permanent residency?
M5Yes you need to include documents proving you are of good character. I can’t tell you the exact processing time but I think it should be much shorter than the processing time for permanent residency.
E6最后我要参加入籍仪式对吧。你可以跟我说一下入籍仪式有哪些内容呢？The final step of citizenship is to attend the citizenship ceremony, right? Could you tell me what will happen at the ceremony?
M6After you are seated there will be a formal introduction, speeches and an address that precedes making the pledge. Then the presiding officer will ask you to repeat the pledge of commitment out loud. Once you have made the pledge you will be an Australian citizen.

entry.title: 工签续签
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Liu (L), who wants to extend his working visa and an immigration officer, Haydn (H).
IDOriginalTranslated
E1So, your visa expires next month, in 25 days actually and you want an extension. On what grounds are you applying for an extension?
M1我持有工作签证在这里已经有十一个月了。在这期间，我不但交了很多税，还经常去教堂做义工。I have been here on a working visa for eleven months. During this period, I not only paid a lot of taxes, but also often volunteered at a church.
E2I’m afraid you need to include some reasons other than those in your application. Is there an employer who can guarantee your employment and can claim that he needs your skills?
M2收到全球金融危机的冲击，我之前的雇主不得不裁减员工。不幸的是我也是其中一个。所以现在没有工作。如果你给我些时间，我可以找到。Because of the impact of the global financial crisis, my previous employer had to cut staff. Unfortunately, I was one of them. So I don’t have an employer now but if you give me more time I can find one.
E3It’s not up to me to give you time to do that. You do have 35 days after the expiry of your visa to find another job. However, if you aren’t able to secure employment within 35 days, you simply must leave. There is no other way.
M3我明白。但是我原想你能对我的案子区别对待。澳洲这么大，多一个人，又有什么关系？I understand that but I thought you might treat my case as an exception. Australia is such a big country. Does it really matter to keep one more person here?
E4You don’t seem to understand that this situation is out of my control. I’m not the one who makes the law. I’m afraid only the Minister has the discretion to approve special applications.
M4求求你。你一定有什么可以帮我的。我有家庭，他们得靠我。如果我不寄钱回家他们会挨饿。我实在是很绝望。Please. You must be able to help me in some way. I have a family who depends on me and they will starve if I stop sending money back home. I’m really desperate!
E5Please sir. You are making this very difficult. There’s simply nothing I can do to help you. If you can find someone to sponsor you within the next two months you might be okay. Otherwise, I’m afraid you have to leave Australia.
M5我怎样才能找到一位雇主呢？一般人雇主都不大喜欢快过期的签证。有谁你认识的可以帮助我？How can I find an employer? Most employers would frown upon people with expiring visas. Do you know someone who can help me?
E6Why don’t you try your local community? Find out if there is a group that can help you. There are usually community welfare groups or you can check the Chinese newspaper.
M6对了。这我可没想到。你讲得没错。一定办法的。谢谢你的指点。Yes. I hadn’t thought of that. You are right. There must be a solution to this. Thanks for your advice.

entry.title: 签证拒签
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Lu, and Henry, an immigration officer. Mr. Lu is enquiring about the visa rejection decision on behalf of his family.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon, Mr. Lu. I understand that you have come in to clarify some matters regarding your family’s visa applications.
M1是的，没错。我们刚受到了你们移民局发来的签证拒签通知。我们很震惊，也很失望。Yes, that’s right. We have just received the visa rejection notice from you. We feel shocked and very disappointed.
E2I’m sorry to hear that. However, we cannot comment on the rejection of their applications apart from the information contained in the decision letter.
M2我知道，你之前就说过了。今天我想问的是移民部是否能重新考虑这个案子。我赚钱养国内的家人。我很久没见到他们了。I know, you told me that already. What I’m asking you is whether your department can reconsider the case. I am supporting my family back in China. I haven’t seen them for a long time.
E3That’s really quite unfortunate. However, all we can do is to consider applications, then accept or refuse them. One path for you would be to encourage your family to reapply.
M3好吧，那他们重新申请了会怎样？会有什么不同吗？他们第一次申请时就满足了所有要求，但你们还是拒绝批签。Ok and what if they reapply? Will it make any difference? They met all the requirements in the first application but you still refused to grant them the visa.
E4I have already said that I can’t comment. I appreciate your circumstances and I sincerely wish that I could help. Believe me, I meet people in your position every day and I have nothing but sympathy for them.
M4我不是对你无礼，但我觉得你有点不讲道理。一开始你告诉我你们部门可能有失误，然后你又说我家人的文件有问题。你也不告诉我是哪些问题。Look, I don’t want to be rude but I think you are being a bit unreasonable. First you told me your department might have made a mistake. Then you said that there are some problems with their documents. But you won’t tell me what these problems are.
E5No, no. Let me make this clear. With respect, Mr. Lu, I think you have the facts a little distorted. I said that I suspected that there had been some problems with the documentation. If you want to keep on arguing, this interview is a waste of your time and mine.
M5好，那我冷静一下。但他们在家等移民部的回复已经浪费了几个月了。想想他们吧。你让他们建立起很高的期望，然后又让这期望破灭。至少给我们一个机会吧。Fine. I’ll calm down, but my family have wasted months sitting around waiting for a reply from your department. Please think of them. You built up their expectations and then you destroyed them. Give us a chance at least.
E6Nobody’s expectations were built up. They should not have made any assumptions until their visas were granted.
M6我要拿头撞墙了。给我个直截了当的答案，是还是否。他们重新申请签证到底有没有意义？你一定能给我点有用的信息。Oh, come on! Just give me a straight answer – yes or no. Is there any point in them reapplying for a visa? Surely you can give me some idea.

entry.title: 庇护签证
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Guan and Adam, an immigration officer. Mr. Guan is enquiring about applying for a protection visa.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，我想申请庇护签证，希望你能帮我。Hello! I’d like to apply for a protection visa and I hope you can help me with that.
M1Well, I will see what I can do. First, please let me ask a few questions. When did you come to Australia and what visa are you on?
E2我 2008 年 2 月持 573 学生签证来悉尼大学读传媒学的硕士。I came to Australia in February 2008 on a student visa subclass 573. I came here to do a master’s degree majoring in media at the University of Sydney.
M2So on what grounds are you applying for a protection visa then? You should be aware that you must be a refugee as defined by the Refugee Convention.
E3我在中国时是个记者，来澳以后在博客上写了很多批评中国政府的文章。我担心回国以后会因政治原因受到迫害。I was a journalist in China. After I came here I wrote many articles on my blog, criticising the Chinese government. I’m worried that when I go back there I will be persecuted for political reasons.
M3How can you be sure you will be persecuted? Publishing some articles alone seems insufficient to support the claim that you will be in danger back home.
E4我的家人已经被监视了！我现在已不敢和他们联系。我一个同事因为发表一些揭露政府丑闻的文章已失踪了。My family has been under surveillance! I’m too afraid to contact them now. One of my colleagues has gone missing for publishing some articles exposing the government scandals.
M4You should include documentary evidence to support your claims in your application. If the documents are not written in English, you will need to have them translated by an accredited translator. It’s strongly recommended that you seek legal advice before lodging the application.
E5如果我的签证批准了，我可以把国内的父母接来澳洲吗？因为我会不可能回国了。If my visa application is approved, can my parents come to Australia to join me as I will never get the chance to go back to my country?
M5You can sponsor your immediate family, such as your spouse and children to come to Australia when you are granted with the protection visa. However parents are not included. You can choose to apply through the parent migration program after you have had the protection visa for over two years.
E6那如果我的申请不被批准会怎样？我能够有上诉重申吗？要知道， 我要是被捕了，有可能会受到睡眠剥夺等非人道的折磨。What if my application is rejected? Can I have my case reviewed? You know, if I am arrested, I may face various kinds of inhumane torture such
M6Yes. You have the right to seek a review of this decision by an independent tribunal, either the Refugee Review Tribunal (RRT) or the Administrative Appeals Tribunal (AAT).as sleep deprivation.

entry.title: 逾期打工
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Liu, who has overstayed his visa and an immigration officer Henry。
IDOriginalTranslated
E1You are here because your six-month tourist visa expired three months ago. We also understand you have been working in violation of your visa conditions. Do you have any comments to make?
M1我的签证是过期了。但我觉得我应该可以留下来。你说我打工违反了签证规定，那就更荒谬了，这不是为澳大利亚做贡献吗？My visa did expire but I thought I could stay on. You said I violated the visa conditions by working here. That’s even more ridiculous. Wasn’t I contributing to Australia?
E2Please be aware that, you were issued with a six-month visa only, and overstaying that period and working are considered to be breaking the law.
M2我从来没意识的这是违法的。我本来是打算来旅游的，但来这儿以后我就爱上这个地方了。I didn’t know that that was illegal. I intended to do some sightseeing around here but when I got here, I fell in love with this place.
E3Thousands of people come to Australia every year, but there is a quota for migration and there is also a waiting list. You have to meet certain requirements and go through a series of procedures to migrate here.
M3我并不知道，因为当时我的旅游签证是一个中介帮我递交的。当我来这以后，我遇到很多其他的游客也在打工，所以我觉得我也可以这样做。I didn’t know that because my tourist visa application was lodged through an agent. After I got here I met plenty of other tourists who were working, so I believed I could do the same.
E4What others do does not make what you did legal. The tourists you talked about might hold a working holiday visa, which gives them the right to work.
M4有人愿意雇我说明我有足够的技能在澳大利亚工作生活，我可以通过技术移民的方式继续留在澳洲吗？The fact that somebody would like to hire me shows that I have enough skills to work and live in Australia. Can I continue to stay here through skilled migration?
E5You came to Australia on a visitor’s visa and have since broken the law so you haven’t made a very good start if you want to apply for another visa.
M5我并不是故意违反法律的。我不是那种人。我之前在国内从来没遇到过这种麻烦。I didn’t mean to break the law. I’m not that kind of person. I’ve never broken the law in my own country.
E6At this stage there is little I can do for you. You are permitted to make an application for permanent residency but you will have to lodge the application from outside the country. I can tell you now that by overstaying your current visa you have harmed your chances.
M6我听说过有人会通过各种手段留在这里，比如跟不认识的人结婚， 宣称回国的话会被杀掉，甚至使用假身份。我从来没做过那些事。你真没有别的 办法帮我了吗？I’ve heard about people trying all sorts of things to stay here, like marrying someone they hardly know, claiming they’ll be killed if they go back home, or even taking on a false identity. I haven’t done anything like that. Is there nothing you can do to help me?

entry.title: 初始咨询
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mrs. Lu, a new migrant, and Henry, a community service officer. Mrs. Lu is seeking advice regarding settling in Australia.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mrs. Lu. What can I do for you today?
M1你好，女士。我这个月初刚移民到澳大利亚。我儿子告诉我你可以就如何适应这儿的新生活给我一些建议。所以我跟你约了这次会面。Hello, I just migrated to Australia early this month. My son told me you can give me some tips on how to adapt to my new life here. That’s why I made this appointment with you.
E2Right. Everyone who’s new to this country needs to go through the process of becoming established and independent in Australia. We can give you some advice to get through this process smoothly.
M2我想我最大的问题是英语。我一点儿也不会说，而且我觉得像我这么老的人学习英语也很困难了。I think my biggest problem is English. I don’t speak English at all and I think it will be very difficult to learn English as I’m already this old.
E3There is a national translation and interpreting service for non-English speakers. You are also eligible for our free Adult Migrant English Program (AMEP) since you’re holding a permanent resident visa.
M3听起来不错。但我如何参加课程呢？课程有多长呢？我需要大老远的来市中心上课吗？It’s good to know that but how can I join the program? Also, how long will the course last? Will I have to go all the way to the city to attend classes?
E4Well you can find your nearest course provider in this booklet. You can study English with the AMEP for up to 510 hours or until you reach functional English, whichever comes first.
M4其他我还需要做什么吗？我听说我需要申请一个税号。如 果我不工作的话也需要税号吗？What else do I need to do? I heard that I need to apply for a Tax File Number. Do I have to have this number if I don’t work?
E5Anyone who receives any type of income, including a Centrelink payment needs a Tax File Number. You can visit the website of the Australian Taxation Office to do that.
M5你之前提到了Centrelink。那是什么？类似于中国的社保局吗？You mentioned Centrelink before. What is it? Is it similar to the Social Security Bureau in China?
E6Kind of, I guess. Centrelink is a government agency which delivers a range of services to the community. You need to register with them but as a newly arrived migrant, you are not immediately eligible for social security.
M6我了解了。感谢你抽时间来见我。澳大利亚公务员确实更知道如何为人民服务啊。I understand. Thanks for your time. It’s true that Australian public servants know how to serve the people better.

entry.title: 商业移民
entry.content:
The following dialogue is between Mr. Guan and Adam, a migration agent. Mr. Guan is enquiring about applying for a business visa for his brother.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1我想把我的弟弟带出来到澳大利亚来生活。他现在住在中国。Hello! I’d like to bring my younger brother to Australia permanently. He lives in China now.
M1How long have you been living in Australia? How long have you been a permanent resident of Australia? Or are you an Australian citizen?
E2我 8 年前持学生签证来到澳大利亚。在我得到学位之后，我申请独立技术移民。很幸运的, 我在 2003 年得到了永居签证。I came to Australia eight years ago on a student visa. I applied for permanent residency after I finished my degree. Fortunately, I received permanent residency in 2003.
M2Okay I see. Are you considering sponsoring your brother under the family reunion scheme? If you want to sponsor your brother under this category, you need to pass the family balance test.
E3不用。实际上呢他在寻求作为商业移民的移民可能。我已经给了他一些有关的信息，他非常有兴趣来到澳洲并在这里建立生意。No. Actually he is looking at the possibility of migrating here on a business visa. I have given him some relevant information and he is very
M3That’s good. Australia needs business people with capital and expertise in overseas markets. Is your brother already running a business in China or is he looking at investing in a business here?interested in starting a business in Australia.
E4他有他自己的计算机软件生意在北京。他的公司开发反病毒软件。 我想他是想把他的生意扩展到澳大利亚来。
M4Well that sounds viable. The software area is still growing and there are so many computer bugs around. Australia needs well-trained IT people so he’ll be able to make full use of his talent.He is running his own computer software business in Beijing. It’s a company developing anti-virus software. I think he is looking forward to moving his business to Australia.
E5对于他的申请他需要准备什么样的文件呢？我要怎样做才能够加速他的申请？
M5The guidelines in this How to Migrate to Australia booklet might help. It details all the documents your brother needs to prepare. There’s a special chapter on business migration.What documents does he need to prepare for the application? What can I do to speed up the process of his application?
E6好的，谢谢。申请费是多少呢？在递交申请以前，需要去体检吗？
M6The application fee for the business migration program is $3,800. We’ll advise him if he needs to have a health check after we’re convinced that his business plan is a viable one.All right, thank you. How much is the application fee? Does he need to have a medical check before lodging the application?

entry.title: 父母移民
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Liu (L), who wants his parents to migrate to Australia, and a migration agent Henry (H).
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Liu, how can I help you today?10
M1早上好。我想申请签证让我父母移民来澳大利亚，这样我就好照顾他们了。所以我来见你。Good morning. I’d like to apply for a visa so my parents can migrate toAustralia and I can look after them. That’s why I’m here.
E2Sure. First of all, could you tell me your residential status? That is, are you an Australian resident and if so, when were you granted your permanent residency?
M2是的，我是持庇护签证的永久居民。我 2005 年 3 月来澳寻求庇护, 在 2006 年 1 月获得了永久签证。Yes I am a permanent resident on a protection visa. I came to Australia and sought protection in March, 2005 and I got permanent resident status in January, 2006.
E3In this case, I think you are a qualified sponsor. How many children orstep-children do your parents have and where do they reside now?
M3只有我和我哥哥。但他两个月前因癌症在中国去世。在中国没什么比晚年丧子更悲剧的了，所以我想把他们接到澳大利亚来照顾。Just me and my older brother who died of cancer in China two months ago. You know, in China nothing is more tragic than losing your child when you’re old. That’s why I want to take care of my parents in Australia.
E4I’m sorry to hear that. Now, I think you meet the basic requirements for sponsoring your parents to come to Australia since you have been a permanent resident for over two years and you are the only remaining child of your parents.
M4我听说有两种选择，一种是一般父母移民签证，另一种是付费父母移民签证，你能解释一下这两者的区别吗？I heard that there are two options, which are a parent migrant visa and a contributory parent migrant visa. Could you explain the difference between them?
E5For the contributory parent migrant visa, applicants have to pay a much larger visa application fee and an Assurance of Support bond. Applicants or their sponsor contribute to Government revenue and help to offset some costs but the waiting period is much shorter than for the parent migrant visa.
M5我可能选择付费父母移民，因为我想尽早把他们借来。他们同样需要满足健康状况要求，是吗？I may choose the contributory one because I want to have them here as soon as possible. They also need to meet health requirements, right?
E6That’s correct. You can find what the requirements are in this brochure. You also need to provide an Assurance of Support before their visa application can be granted.
M6好的，感谢你的指点。我马上就去准备签证申请。OK, thanks for this information. I will go and prepare everything for this visa application.

entry.title: 开附属卡
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr Chen (C), a Mandarin speaking customer, and John (J), a credit card specialist from the Commonwealth Bank. Mr Chen is enquiring about his credit card over the phone.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Thank you for calling the Commonwealth Bank Card Centre, my name is John. How can I help you today?Hello John. I’m calling because I’d like to know whether I can apply for an additional card for my younger brother. I’d also like to know if there are any charges.
M1你好, John。我打电话来是想问一下我是不是可以为我弟弟申请一张附属信用卡。我还想知道是否会产生费用？
E2Definitely, there may be a cost involved depending on the type of credit card that you are holding. Now, may I have your name, date of birth and customer reference number please?32
M2我叫 Tim Chen。我的生日是 1980 年 11 月 26 日。客戶號碼呀，你等等…我的客户号码是 5353 8092。My name is Tim Chan. My date of birth is 26 November 1980. My customer reference number? Just a second…my customer reference number is 5353 8092.
E3Thank you Mr. Chen. Our records show that you have a ‘low-fee’ credit card which allows you to add one additional card holder without any extra charges.27
M3那真是太好了。我弟弟下個星期會来澳洲读书。我想给他一张附属卡，以备不时之需。那你能否帮到我呢？That’s great! My younger brother is coming to study in Australia next week. I want to give him a card in case he needs it. Will you be able to help me with this now?
E4Sorry Mr. Chen, I’m not authorised to do it over the phone for security reasons. We need to check your ID documents at a branch before we can process this.30
M4好，我明白了。那就等他来了之后，我再带他到你们在这附近的分行去申请吧。我还想问问这卡的限额是怎么分配的。OK, I understand. After he has arrived, I’ll take him to the branch near our place to apply for the card. I’d also like to know how the card limit is split.
E5The card limit will be shared between the two cardholders. That means the total amount spent by both of you must not exceed the card limit, say $7000 in your case.33
M5多谢你回答我全部的疑问。但听起来似乎很麻烦啊。有没有什么我要注意的地方呢？Thank you very much for answering all my questions. But it sounds very troublesome. Is there anything else that I need to be aware of?
E6He’ll get a card in his own name but with your account number. That means he can do most of the things you can do as the primary cardholder, such as setting up regular payments.35
M6我需要考虑一下。不管怎么说，非常感谢你。I’ll need to think about it. Anyway, thank you very much!

entry.title: 彩票理财
entry.content:
A Mandarin speaking client is asking a financial advisor about how to invest his money.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon, Mr. Su. Please come in. I am the bank manager here. Now, what can I do for you?20
M1我从来都没有想过我会向别人请教如何花钱，但我最近看了很多文章，发现专业的理财还是很有必要的。所以我来找你。I never thought that I would ask people about how to spend money. But I’ve read a lot of articles recently and found professional money management is quite necessary. That’s why I’m here.
E2Sure I will try my best to assist you. By the way, I can tell that you are a business-minded person, am I right? First, can you tell me how much money you have to invest?36
M2其实我没做过生意，但是我上周很幸运的中了彩票，得到了 6 万多块钱。我想那其中的 5 万来投资。Actually, I’ve never had my own business. But luckily, I won the lottery last week and got a little over $60,000. So I want to take $50,000 of it to invest.
E3There are a number of investment options you could consider, including investing in shares, although this is not a good idea at present. Other options could be a savings investment account or buying various bonds.35
M3确实，最近的全球金融危机和欧洲的债务危机基本已经打消了我投资股票的念头。你能跟我说说其他的一些选项吗？Indeed, I have given up on the idea of investing in shares due to the recent global financial crisis and European debt crisis. Can you tell me other options?
E4Certainly. Government bonds are an outstanding investment for cautious investors, at least in this country. They are a good way to save for your retirement, children’s education, an overseas holiday and many other things.34
M4那储蓄投资帐户是什么呢？这里的柜员常常向我推荐，但我总是谢绝了。So what is the savings investment account? The tellers here have recommended it to me many times, but I always respectfully declined their offer.
E5A savings investment account is ideal for those people who still need to withdraw on the amount they’ve invested. All you need is to deposit $200. The interest is calculated on a daily basis.34
M5如果我需要取钱，我需要等待多久呢？你们这里的定期存款项目是怎样的呢？If I need to make a withdrawal, how long do I need to wait before I can get the money? How about the term deposit scheme here?
E6You can make a withdrawal at any time. A term deposit is ideal if you have money to invest and are prepared to commit it for a certain period in order to earn higher interest.33
M6非常感谢你的详细解释。不过我觉得我还需要更多时间来考虑，然后才能决定。我过两天再来见你，好吗？Thank you for your detailed explanation. However I still need some time to think it over before making a decision. I will come to see you again within a couple of days. Is that ok?

entry.title: 理财咨询
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a financial adviser and a Mandarin speaking client, Mr. Yang.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Thank you for coming today Mr. Yang. We have vast experience in financial matters and we can help you accumulate wealth.21
M1谢谢你。我的朋友向我推荐过你，而且你的履历也让我印象深刻。似乎你总是能赚钱，从不赔钱。Thank you. My friend recommended you to me, and your track record is quite impressive. It seems that you always make a profit and never make a loss.
E2You have gone so far as to say we always win and never lose. That is not true, unless it is in very general terms. Nobody out there is able to win all the time.35
M2如果你不是过于谦虚的话，那我可不放心我把我钱交给你管。也许我来得不是地方， 因为我就是要找只赚不赔的人。If it’s not that you are just being too modest, I don’t think I can trust you and let you manage my money. Maybe I didn’t come to the right place. I’m looking for someone who always wins and never loses.
E3I’m just being honest with you, Mr. Yang. Would my promise make me a better financial adviser? People who claim they never lose are bound to be lying and they’ll get you into trouble.34
M3好吧。那么你就是承认你不总是赚钱咯？如果我把钱给你，你全赔光了可怎么办？这可是我毕生的积蓄，是我们家美好未来的保障！Right. So you admit that you do not always win? What if I give you my money and you lose it all? They are my entire life savings, and the guarantee of a good future for my family.
E4It’s not as simple as that. When you start to invest, you should never put all your eggs in one basket. That is what diversification means.26
M4之前我有听过这个概念，但我不是很理解。它是不是说不要把你的钱全放在一家银行里？I’ve heard this concept before but I don’t quite understand. Does it mean that we should not put all our money in one bank?
E5Not exactly, but close enough. It means that your money should be invested in many different income-producing schemes, such as shares, property trusts and bank accounts.27
M5我明白了。也就是说，要是一项投资赔钱了，整体的投资组合未必赔钱。其他的投资项目可能能抵消这一项的损失。I see. That is to say, even if one investment makes a loss, the entire investment portfolio may not make a loss. The gain from other investments may offset the loss of one investment.
E6You’ve got it. You catch on very quickly and that’s good when you are about to invest. Let’s say it’s almost impossible for all investments to be losing simultaneously unless some extreme events occur like war.36
M6好吧，我觉得你还是值得信任的。希望我能跟你合作！Alright, I think you are trustworthy. I hope you’ll be able to help me.

entry.title: 房屋保险
entry.content:
Mr. Wang approaches a customer service officer of an insurance company to ask about the home insurance they provide.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，我最近新买了一间三房两卫的房子，想上个保险。正好最近在电视上看到你们的广告，所有就过来了。Hello, I bought a house with three bedrooms and two bathrooms recently and I want to have it insured. I just saw your ads on TV, so I’m here.
M1You’ve come to the right place as our company provides a comprehensive property policy. We recommend you consider insuring both the building and its contents.26
E2我知道很多公司都提供这种类型的住房保险的，我要货比三家再决定。那你们这种综合险可以保哪些东西呢？I know there are many companies providing this kind of home insurance. I need to compare before deciding which one to go for. So what does your comprehensive policy cover?
M2It will cover your home against any of these events: fire, lightning strikes, earthquakes, theft, impact by any vehicle or falling trees, etc. You can get more details on our website or you can take a brochure home.39
E3我拿一本小册子回去慢慢看。另外，如果我房子里的家具或电器损坏了，且无法维修，那怎么办？如果，连房子都完全损毁呢？I’ll take a brochure and read it carefully. Also, what if the furniture and appliances in my house are so damaged that they cannot be repaired? What if my house is completely destroyed?
M3You must provide all the invoices of your damaged appliances. The policy will enable you to build a new home of an equivalent style, material and size. Also, should you decide to purchase your property insurance policy with us we can offer a lot of other benefits.48
E4对了，你能跟我说说其他的可报销项目吗？比如，如果我的房屋受损需要维修，这段期间你们会提供临时的住所吗？Oh also, can you tell me more about the other benefits? For example, if my house is damaged and needs repair, will you provide me with temporary accommodation?
M4Yes, we can help you with the cost of temporary accommodation for up to 12 months. Other benefits include any debris removal costs, architects’ and surveyors’ fees and accidental breakage of fixed glass and mirrors.35
E5那还真不错。难怪，我有一个朋友向我推荐你们公司。你们的套餐确实很吸引人。有一件事我还不清楚，应该如何如何查询我的保险额呢？That’s pretty good. No wonder a friend of mine recommended your company to me. Your policy is really appealing. One more thing, how can I find out the amount insured?
M5You need to measure the house and get the total area. Then multiply the number of square metres by the approximate building costs listed in the table provided.28
E6假如我还不是知道如何计算，你们有同事可以帮我吗？还有，我可以选择垫底费是多少吗？If I still don’t know how to calculate it, will your colleagues help me with that? Also, can I choose how much the excess is?
M6Yes, we can arrange to have one of our agents make the calculation for you. The excess will vary according to the sum insured. For example, if you are insured for $100,000 the excess is $200.36

entry.title: 三方财险
entry.content:
Mr. Huang is calling AAMI to make a claim.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, you’ve called AAMI. My name is Harry. How can I help you?14
M1你好，我叫黄晓明。我买了你们的第三方财险。今天早上发生了起事故，我想索赔。Hello, my name is Huang Xiaoming. I purchased your Third Party Property Damage insurance policy. I had an accident this morning, so I want to make a claim.
E2OK，Mr. Huang, could you please tell me your date of birth so I can verify your identity? Also, could you please tell me the reference number of your policy?31
M2我的生日是 1983 年 7 月 14 日。请稍等一下，我找一下保单号…有了，是 TPPD21853.My birthday is the 14th of July, 1983. Please hold on, let me find my policy number. Got it, it’s TPPD21853.
E3Yes you are holding our Third Party Property Damage Insurance. Now, Mr. Huang, could you please tell me what happened this morning?22
M3我住在一个带地下车库的公寓里。今天早上我开车出门时，一不小心蹭到了对面车库的门，撞凹进去了一块。live in an apartment with an underground garage. When I drove out this morning, I accidentally scraped my car against the gate of another garage right in front, leaving a dent on it.
E4Your policy covers you for damage caused by your car to property owned by a third party in the event of an accident. In this case, we can pay for the cost of repairing the gate. However, the cost of repair for your car is not covered.46
M4我知道。不过我车也没受什么损伤，就车头有一点划痕。那我是不是要付垫底I know that. But there is little damage to my car, just a bit of a scratch on the front. Do I have to pay the excess?
E5Yes, you need to pay the excess of $550. If you have a credit card on hand, we can process the payment right now. Alternatively, you can ring us later to update your payment information.
M5我现在就付吧。可是，如果修理车库门所花的实际费用低于这 550块垫底费怎么办？I’ll pay it now. But, what if the actual cost of repair is lower than $550?
E6If that’s the case, I can simply cancel your claim and notify you. Then you can pay the owner of the garage directly. However, according to my experience, that kind of repair normally costs more than $550.
M6我了解了。我公寓大楼管理员的电话是 0439 872 109，你可以和他联系来确定维修的费用。I see. The number of the building manager for this apartment block is 0439 872 109. You can contact him to confirm the cost.

entry.title: 旅游保险
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between an insurance advisor, Mr Nelson, and Miss Lu, about taking out an insurance policy for her parents.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, Miss Lu. Nice to meet you. How can I help you today?13
M1是这样的，我父母下个月要来澳洲看我，顺便到处玩玩，你们公司有什么针对海外游客的保险吗？Well, my parents are coming to Australia next month to visit me and travel around as well. Do you provide any insurance policies for overseas visitors?
E2We have three types of visitor cover: basic, mid-range, and top cover. The premium for each type varies depending on the coverage. How long are your parents going to stay here?31
M2两个月，他们想等国内天气凉了之后再回国。保险的价格是多少？我想选个实惠又比较全面的保险。Two months. They don’t want to go back until the weather in China cools down. How much is the insurance cover? I’d like to choose an inexpensive yet fairly comprehensive plan.
E3The monthly price for these plans ranges from $190 to $520. If you are on a budget, basic cover may be sufficient.25
M3那基本保险的保险范围是什么？虽然我父母现在总的来说健康状况不错，但也可能需要时不时去看看医生。What’s the coverage of the basic cover? Even though my parents are generally healthy now, they may need to see a doctor from time to time.
E4That’s very wise. No one wants to worry about healthcare expenses when going on vacation. Well, the basic plan includes public hospital in-patient services, and outpatient services such as GP or specialist visits.33
M4听起来还不错。对了，我妈妈有牙疼的老毛病，她在澳期间可能需要去看牙医。牙科费用是否包括在基本保险中？It sounds good. Well, my mum has long had toothaches and she may need to go to the dentist while she’s in Australia. Are dental costs covered by the basic plan?
E5Yes. Expenses for dental surgery are covered. In fact, a lot of other items are included as well, such as heart surgery, eye procedures, and emergency treatment required after an accident.31
M5我了解了。最后，我想知道一下如何付保险费。是不是需要我到你们的店面去付钱呢？I see. Lastly, I’d like to know how I can pay the premium. Do I need to go down to one of your branches to make the payment?
E6We offer a variety of payment options. You can pay online, over the telephone, by mail or at one of our local branches. Here’s a bilingual brochure that you can read.31
M6太好了，我现在就读一下，如果我还有什么问题，我会再请教你的。谢谢！Great. I’ll read it now. I’ll let you know if I have any more questions. Thank you!

entry.title: 房屋扩建
entry.content:
A resident approaches an officer of the local council to ask about building an extension to a house.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1我想询问关于对房子进行扩建的事情。我需不需要填一张申请表呢？我需要做什么才能得到批准呢？I’d like to enquire about house extensions. Do I need to fill out an application form? What should I do to get my application approved?
M1You’ve come to the right place. It really depends on where you are, the type of dwelling and the nature of the extension. What sort of renovations did you have in mind?
E2我想扩建房子后部的厨房和餐厅。所以我们想从后墙再扩建 3 米，那样就应该给我们足够大地方的厨房和一个吃饭的地方，甚至还有一间太阳房。I’d like to extend my kitchen and dining room at the rear of my house. We want a three-metre extension from the back wall, which will give us enough room for the new kitchen and dining room, and even a sunroom.
M2According to Section 77 of the Environmental Planning & Assessment Act 1979, you need to apply for a Construction Certificate. You also need to submit a building application. What street do you live on?
E3我住在 Mason 街。是那些全砖的加利福尼亚平房的一间。你能给我一些关于我应该怎么做的信息吗？你们有没有一个中文小册子呢？I live on Mason street. My house is one of the brick California bungalows. Can you give me some information about what I should do? Do you have any brochures in Chinese?
M3Here’s the checklist for building applications. You’ll get a written acknowledgement in the post once your application has been received. That will contain a reference number for making further enquiries. The application is then passed on to a town planner or building surveyor for assessment.45
E4我听说在澳大利亚我的邻居和住这个地区的人们是可以反对我的改建提议的。是这样的吗？这看起来有一点不公平吧。我的意思是，那完全取决于你跟邻居的关系怎么样。I was told that in Australia my neighbours and people living in this area can oppose my proposal. Is that so? It is not very fair. I mean, it totally depends on your relationship with your neighbours.
M4Your neighbours are notified by mail and invited to inspect the proposal at the council over a 14-day period. If no objections are received and the proposal complies with council’s planning controls, approval will be granted.36
E5那如果有人反对呢？我是不是就扩建不成了？还是由政府来仲裁？What if there are objections? Does it mean that I can’t extend my house? Or will the government arbitrate?
M5The council will examine the reasons for the objection and then decide whether you can make the extension.18
E6这听起来对我来说都很复杂。我希望没有人反对。不管怎么说呢我需要知道费用的问题。申请费多少钱呢？并且我的改建提议要具体到什么程度呢？It all sounds complicated to me. I hope there are no objections. Anyway I need to know how much the fees are. How much is the application fee? And how detailed does my proposal need to be?
M6The fee is based on the estimated cost of the building works, so you should get a rough quote from a builder. Building plans must include a site plan, floor plans, elevations and section plans as well as a drainage plan.41

entry.title: 租房报修
entry.content:
A tenant has just moved into a house and rings the agent managing the house to report a number of repairs that are required.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, Smith Real Estate. This is Chris speaking, how can I help you?13
M1你好。我的名字是 Jason. 我想我上周见过你。我刚刚搬进你们名下在 Kensington St 的房子。我想报告一些问题。Hello. My name is Jason. I think we met last week. I just moved into your property in Kensington St. I’d like to report some problems.
E2Yes Jason, I remember you. You are at number 73, aren’t you? Hang on and I’ll just get the file. Okay, I’ve got it here. Now what are the problems you’ve noticed?32
M2这里有很多东西需要维修。但我最关心的是厨房里有一股让人感到昏晕的瓦斯气味。我很担心这可能是漏气了。Many things here need repairing. But I’m most concerned about the gas smell in the kitchen, which is making me dizzy. I’m worried it is a gas leak.
E3Yes. You are quite right. That’s something that needs to be checked straight away. Will you be home all day today? I’ll ring the gas company straight away to check your stove but they usually don’t specify what time they’ll come.41
M3我本来打算外出购物。但既然如此，我就在家等着吧。不过，这听起来可不是很合理。I was going to go shopping. But if that’s the case, I’ll wait at home. However, it doesn’t sound very reasonable.
E4I agree with you but if there’s a gas leak they usually come pretty quickly. They’ll probably be there within an hour. Now, you mentioned that there’s something else you’d like to report.33
M4是的。首先烤箱上其中的一个按钮坏掉了。所以我用不了烤箱。同样，一些门把手松了，还有在窗轨和滑道处，充满了污垢。Yes. First, a button on the oven is broken, so I can’t use it. Also, some of the door knobs are loose and there is dirt all through the track of the sliding window.
E5I’ll send a contractor to fix it as soon as possible, probably within a day or two. Loose door handles are considered minor problems, minor repairs and such are the tenants own responsibility. The same applies to cleaning the sliding window tracks.44
M5我不认为这是公平的。在上一个房客搬走之后，新房客搬进来之前，你们需要适当的清洁房子。I don’t think that’s fair. You should clean the place properly after the last tenant moves out and before the new tenant moves in.
E6I’m sorry you feel that way but that’s the way we normally do things. I will contact the gas company now as that matter is urgent. Thank you for calling.
M6那好吧，我要是发现了其他问题再来找你。Fine. I’ll call you if there is anything else.

entry.title: 悉尼买房
entry.content:
Mr. Li, a business migrant, is having a conversation with a real estate agent. Mr. Li is looking for a house to purchase.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1下午好。我和我的家庭刚到悉尼，现在住在 Vibe 酒店。我们想尽快买房子。你能帮助我们吗？Good afternoon. My family and I just arrived in Sydney and we are staying at Vibe Hotel. We’d like to buy a home as soon as possible. Can you help us?
M1Good afternoon Mr. Li. Of course, I’d be delighted to help you. Now tell me, what type of place are you looking for?23
E2我想买个大一点的房子，要有 4 个卧室，两个卫生间，有大花园，最好是有些树，孩子们可以在树荫下玩。We want a big house with four bedrooms, two bathrooms and a big garden. It would be better to have some trees so that my children can play in the shade.
M2There are a number of properties like that on the market so there shouldn’t be any problem. Do you know which suburb you’d like to live in? Have you done some research on your own?35
E3我对悉尼不太熟悉。我想最好离市中心和大学比较近。因为我太太和我不想一天花几个小时上下班。朋友建议说住在 Hurstville 或是住在 Burwood. 这附近你有适合的房源吗？I’m not familiar with Sydney. I’d prefer somewhere close to the CBD and university because my wife doesn’t want to spend hours commuting every day. My friend recommended Hurstville and Burwood. Do you have any good houses there?
M3Yes we do. They are both excellent areas with large Chinese communities. Burwood is slightly more expensive. What we really need to discuss now is the price range you are considering.31
E4如果我投资预算在 75 万澳币左右，能否买到我想要的那种房子呢？我想最好是不用去贷款。Can I get that kind of house, if my budget is around $750,000? I’d prefer not to take out a loan.
M4Factors such as location and block size all have a bearing on the price. It’s quite possible in Hurstville but it may be difficult to find something suitable in Burwood around that price.33
E5我听说了，悉尼的房价飞涨，一天一个样。所以，你还是帮我们尽快找到一个合适的房子吧。对了，你可以带我们去看看房子吗？Ｉheard that the housing prices in Sydney are now soaring up and up every day. So please help us find a suitable property as soon as possible. Also, can you take us to inspect the houses?
M5Sure. You might be interested in a solar energy house in Hurstville which has just come onto the market. I’d be happy to pick you up at your hotel.Just let me know the best time for you.38
E6好的，那我们明天上午 10 点间如何？你最好能准备一些正在出售的房子的书面情况介绍。Good. Shall we meet at 10am tomorrow? It would be great if you could prepare some written details about the houses for sale.
M6Sure. Of course, you are under no obligation to buy and there are no fees or charges involved. We’ve also got arrangements with other agents so we can show you houses they have on the market.36

entry.title: 理科天才
entry.content:
The following is a dialogue between a teacher and the mother of a 19-year-old girl who is doing her Higher School Certificate.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good to see you again, Mrs. Tian. This time I have good news for you regarding your daughter’s results. She is doing extremely well.24
M1哦！我太高兴了。过去两年一直非常担心。对于她还有整个家庭，过去两年很困难。Oh, I’m so happy. I’d been very worried over the past two years. It has been a difficult time for her and the whole family.
E2Trust me, she is an excellent achiever. Her marks are in the top 40% and that really means something, considering her background.22
M2的确如此。她来到一个新的国家，说着不同的语言，还必须同时学习这里的语言和学校的功课，要是人们知道这有多困难就好了。ndeed. She came to a new country where people speak a different language, and she has had to study the language and the school subjects at the same time. I wish people could understand how hard it is.
E3I know, it must have been extremely difficult for all of you but Tracy is such a smart girl! Plus, she has been working very hard. All her teachers are so impressed by her incredible success.
M3他对于数学和物理非常感兴趣，所以她这两门的成绩非常好。她因此还选了另外四门工科的课。哦，我真希望能够有个好成绩，因为她特别想学工程。She is very interested in maths and physics, so she has got very good results in them. And that’s why she also chose four other science subjects. Oh, I hope she can do well because she is so eager to study engineering.
E4Well, we’ll have to wait until she sits her exams before we can consider what she can do. I’m confident she’ll get into university, if not at her preferred institution, but at least in her preferred subject area.
M4你为什么能这么肯定？你说过她在模拟考试中考得很好，能够考上大学，不过我觉得她跟当地孩子比起来，没有那么聪明。Why are you so sure? You said she did great in the mock exam and was able to make her way to university. But as I understand it, she is not that smart, compared to local kids.
E5I think what you are saying is not quite right because based on her current performance, she could easily be admitted for an arts degree. I understand that may not be what she wants currently, but if it is the only choice, she’ll have to take it, I’m afraid.
M5但如果她学习不喜欢的东西，她是不会学好的。如果不是她感兴趣的专业，她是不会接受的。而且因为她语言背景的问题，文科比理科更难学。If she studied something she doesn’t like, she wouldn’t do well at it. She wouldn’t take a major, if she wasn’t interested in it. Plus because of her language background, arts are more difficult than science.
E6Yes, that’s the irony of it. We all know that she can do a lot better in a science course rather than an arts one, and yet being admitted to a science course requires higher grades.
M6我知道你已经帮了 Tracy 很多了，但请你再尽力帮帮她。如果她没有考到一个好成绩，并没能在大学学到理科，她的未来就完了！I know you have helped Tracy a lot. But please try to help her more! If she doesn’t get a good score and fails to get into science at university, she won’t have a future.

entry.title: 体育项目
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr Wang (W), a parent and Mr Hargraves (H), the sports coordinator of Burwood High School. Mr Wang recently received a letter from the school and would like to ask some questions about the sports programs at the school.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, I am Mr. Hargraves, one of the teachers here. How can I help you?15
M1你好。我想问一下负责体育活动的Tyler 老師在不在啊？我跟他约了今天十点钟的见面。Hi, I would like to know whether Mr Tyler, who is responsible for sports, is here or not. I have made an appointment to see him at 10 today.
E2Oh, Mr. Tyler … He’s currently on long service leave so I have replaced him as the sports coordinator. Don’t worry! I’ll be able to help you. What’s the matter?30
M2我是 8 年纪B 班 Jenny 的爸爸，我姓王。我上星期三收到學校寄来的一封信，通知我说我女儿对于星期三的運動項目选择有問題。到底是什么問題呢？I’m the father of Jenny from Year 8B. My family name is Wong. Last Wednesday, I received a letter from the school which informed me that there’s something wrong with my daughter’s Wednesday sports choices. What’s the problem?
E3Just wait a second … I need to get her record from the computer system. Our records show that she hasn’t picked a sport to do and that seems to be the problem.33
M3是呀。我觉得有点贵，所以我没有让她參加。比如说游泳吧，半年就要 200块。我怕我负担不起。Yes. I think it’s a bit expensive, that’s why I didn’t allow her to join. Take swimming as an example, it costs $200 for half a year. I’m afraid that we can’t afford it.
E4I see. But look Mr. Wong, Jenny has to enrol in one of the sports programs. It’s the policy of the Department of Education that schools have to offer sports, including free sports, for students to participate in.38
M4我真不知道學校原來有免費的運動項目呀，可能我填申請表的时候没有看到吧，那么哪些项目是免費的呢？I really didn’t know the school offers free sports. Maybe I overlooked them when I was filling out the application form. So, what sports are free?
E5There are lots of them. For example, volleyball, badminton, table tennis and multi-sports in which students will do a different sport each week for 20 weeks.
M5这個「多種運動」听起来不错，我想让她学习不同的运动和各自相关的规则。不知道还有没有位置呢？如果没有了，她可以去打羽毛球吗？“Multi-sports” sounds great! I want her to learn different sports and their rules. Are there any positions left? If not, can she join badminton?
E6Yes. Jenny can definitely join multi-sports and I’ll put her name down in a minute. Please remind her to bring a bottle of water and a towel with her every Wednesday!
M6好。我明白。真是非常感谢。OK. I understand. Thank you very much.

entry.title: 教育系统
entry.content:
A Chinese education official is asking an Australian counterpart about the education system in Australia.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1感谢您能抽空回答我关于澳大利亚教育系统的问题。首先我想问的是在澳大利亚，教育是联邦政府的责任还是州级政府的责任？Thank you for taking time to answer my questions regarding the Australian education system. My first question is, in Australia is it the Federal Government or the State Government that is responsible for education?
M1Each state government manages the school system within their state. This means that they provide funds for and regulate their schools. Also, each state has a Vocational Education and Training (VET) or Technical and Further Education (TAFE) system. However, funding for universities is the federal government’s responsibility.47
E2中国的教育系统有三级，即初级教育，中等教育以及高等教育，请问澳大利亚也是采用类似的模式吗？China has a three-tier education system which includes primary education, secondary education and tertiary education. Do you follow a similar model?
M2Yes, Australia follows a similar three-tier model and school education, including primary education and secondary education are both compulsory.19
E3哦，这跟中国不一样。我们实行的是九年制义务教育，即小学和初中是强制的。那你们中小学的课程大纲是由州教育部编写吗？Oh, that is different from us. China has implemented a nine-year compulsory education, which means primary school and junior secondary school are compulsory. Is the state education department in charge of preparing curriculum for schools?
M3No. Each state has its own Board of Studies which sets the core curriculum by developing syllabuses for Kindergarten to Year 12 and provides support materials for teachers and parents. It also manages the School Certificate external tests and the Higher School Certificate examinations each year.46
E4说到 HSC，它是不是指大学的入学考试，就如同中国的高考呢？在学生课业方面，有怎样特别的安排呢？Speaking of the HSC, is it a university entrance examination? Just like China’s Gao Kao? And are there any special arrangements in terms of the courses for students?
M4The Higher School Certificate (HSC) is the highest award in secondary education in New South Wales. To be eligible for the award, students must complete Years 11 and 12, satisfy HSC course requirements and sit for the state wide HSC examinations.41
E5那学生能进什么样的大学，读什么样的专业，是不是由 HSC 考试的成绩决定的呢？So the result of the HSC determines which university a student enters and what major he or she can study, right?
M5Not exactly. The HSC Mark is a 50:50 combination of a student’s HSC examination and school-based assessment mark for each course. Then they have to use this final mark to work out what their ATAR is before applying for undergraduate university programs. Notes: Australian Tertiary Admission Rank (ATAR)42
E6那您可以说说澳洲的高等职业教育吗？我听说职业教育在这里非常受欢迎，而且蓝领工人的薪水往往比白领们要高。Can you talk about Australia’s tertiary education? I heard vocational education is very popular here and blue-collar workers often earn more than white-collar workers.
M6In Australia, most vocational tertiary education courses are provided by TAFE. Studying in TAFE has many advantages including a focus on applied rather than abstract skills, practical teaching strategies and seamless pathways between TAFE qualifications.35

entry.title: 女儿选课
entry.content:
Mr Lee (L) is talking to a school teacher (T) regarding his daughter’s subject choices.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mr. Lee. You said you want to talk to me about your daughter, Cindy. What’s the problem?19
M1早上好,Tomas 老师。Cindy 明年就升入 11 年级了，但是我对这边的高考制度不是很了解，所以想跟你谈一谈，听取些建议。Good morning, Mr. Tomas. Cindy’s starting Year 11 next year but I’m not very familiar with the “Higher School Certificate” examination system here. That’s why I want to talk to you about this and get some advice.
E2I see. Cindy’s a very capable student and I’m sure she’ll do well in Years 11 and 12. I’d like to know whether you know anything about the HSC system before I start explaining it.35
M2我不是在这里受的教育，但我听我朋友说，12 年级的学生要参加一个州级范围的考试。而这个考试的结果会决定他们去哪所大学就读。是这样吗？I wasn’t educated here but I was told by my friends that Year 12 students need to sit a state-wide examination. The result of that examination determines which university they can go to. Am I right?
E3You’re quite right. But there are some more specific requirements that students need to follow in order to graduate. The basic rule is that they need to complete at least 10 units of study in their HSC.37
M310 个单元学分的学习？是 10 个科目的意思吗？这对她来说会不会太困难了？那她一周要去学校上课上多久呀？10 units of study? Does that mean 10 subjects? Will that be too hard for her? How long does she need to go to school for each week?
E4Mr. Lee, I think you’ve misunderstood me. “10 units of study” doesn’t mean 10 subjects at all. In the HSC, each subject can have between 1 to 4 units and students can go beyond 10 units if they want to.
M4我明白了。Cindy 跟我说过，英文是必修科目，所以除了英文，她还想选读生物、化学、物理还有四个单元学分的数学。这样加起来够十个学分了吗？I see. Cindy told me that English is a compulsory subject and she wants to study biology, chemistry, physics and 4-unit mathematics apart from English. Do they make 10 units altogether?
E5That is 12 units in total. In this case, her final mark for HSC will be calculated using her English result and the best marks for eight of her other units.
M5不过呢，我想让她学语言，特别是中文。因为我们都是炎黄子孙，她能够讲中文不是会更好？请问 HSC 里面有语言类考试吗？By the way, I want her to study languages especially Chinese. This is because she is Chinese and it’d be good for her to be able to speak the language. Are there any language examinations in the HSC?
E6Definitely, there are more than 30 languages for students to choose from in the HSC; but our school only offers Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Italian, Latin and Spanish.
M6好的，我明白了。太谢谢你了！OK. I understand. Thank you very much!

entry.title: 女儿入学
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

Mrs. Lin is enquiring about the admission procedure for an Australian university.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning. Mrs. Lin. What can I help you with today?11
M1我的外甥女在中国，高中快毕业了，想来澳洲念大学。说实话，虽然在这呆了这么多年，我对这里的大学也不了解，所以来问问你。My niece has almost finished high school in China and she wants to study at a university here. To be honest, even having stayed here for many years, I have little knowledge about universities here. So I came to ask you.
E2Sure. What would you like to ask about? Is it regarding the areas of study, admission, or applying for a student visa?24
M2我想她已经决定要学心理学了。现在的问题是她不知道入学须满足那些条件。她也不清楚申请你们大学的流程是怎样的。I think she has decided to study psychology. The problem now is that she doesn’t know the admission requirements. She isn’t clear on the application process for your university either.
E3To be eligible to apply to our university for an undergraduate program, your niece must have a recognised secondary qualification or have finished a recognised foundation course. She also needs to provide evidence of proficiency in English.37
M3那对她的高中成绩又有怎样的要求呢？她正在备考雅思，请问要考多少分才能在你们学校学习心理学呢？What are the requirements regarding her high school results? She is preparing for the IELTS test. What does she need to score in order to study psychology at your university?
E4We use the Australian Tertiary Admissions Rank (ATAR) to determine whether the candidate meets our academic requirements. The required ATAR score for undergraduate psychology study is 84.5, which is equivalent to 520 points on the Chinese Gao Kao. For IELTS, she needs to get 6.5, with a minimum of 6.0 in each section.50
M4在我外甥女确定她满足一切条件时，她就可以递交申请了对吧？她可以在网上申请吗？申请费多少呢？什么时候递交申请为宜呢？She can lodge her application when she meets all the requirements, right? Can she apply online? How much is the application fee? When is a good time to lodge the application?
E5Yes, she can apply for the course online and upload scanned copies of the required documents. There is a reduced application fee of only AU$55 if you submit your application online. It is a good idea to apply at least two to three months before semester begins, in order to avoid disappointment, and to give you enough time to organise her student visa.64
M5申请之后她就只需要等待你们发录取意向书了吧？但我听说光凭录取意向书还不能去申请学生签证，对吗？After that, she will only need to wait for your offer, right? But I heard that an offer alone is not enough to apply for a student visa, right?
E6She needs to accept the offer and pay the required commencement fee and organise her compulsory Overseas Student Health Cover. Then we can issue an electronic Confirmation of Enrolment for you to proceed with the visa application.38
M6我了解了。我回去告诉她这些信息，看看她还有什么问题，然后再来问你。谢谢！I see. I’ll tell her this information and see what other questions she has. Then I’ll come back to you. Thank you!

entry.title: 外校参观
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

This dialogue takes place between a Chinese teacher who is visiting an Australian school and the headmaster of that school.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1感谢您能带我们参观校园。现在我想向您请教一些关于课程设置的问题。Thanks for showing us around the campus. Now I’d like to ask you some things about your curriculum.
M1It is my pleasure, Mr. He. When I visited schools in China I was also amazed by your way of teaching. So I’m more than willing to share some ideas regarding our curriculum with you.35
E2首先是主课。这个年纪的中国学生一般都会把语文看做一个非常枯燥的学科，而且特别怕写作文。而这里的学生则很享受语文的学习，这一点我很惊奇。Firstly, regarding core subjects, in China, students of this age normally regard Chinese as a very dull subject and writing an essay is the last thing they want to do. By contrast, students here enjoy learning English very much, which is very surprising.
M2We try to make English in Years 7 – 10 challenging but enjoyable. The aim is to improve students’ knowledge, skills and understanding of language by responding to and composing texts, including visual and multimedia texts.35
E3寓教于乐，我想这是我们应该借鉴的。另外，这里的学生学习数学可比中国学生轻松多了。而且，老师的教学似乎非常容易激发学生的兴趣。Learning while having fun. I think that is something we can adopt. Besides, the maths study here is much easier than in China. Teachers seem very good at arousing students’ interest.
M3We understand maths is an abstract subject which can easily frustrate students. That’s why we strive to create confidence and enjoyment in doing mathematical activities. Students develop an awareness of how maths can be used to solve problems in everyday life.41
E4我想中国的大多数学生还没有意识到，学校所学的知识和日常生活的相关性。这需要老师的正确引导。我注意到你们有各种各样的技术课，您能介绍一些这些课程吗？I think most students in China haven’t realised the links between the knowledge they learn at school and their daily life. Teachers should guide them properly. I also noticed that you have various technology classes. Can you tell me a bit about them?
M4The technology course gives students an appreciation of design and design processes. It lets them understand how humankind uses technology to develop creative and innovative solutions to real problems. For example, in food technology, students will learn how to prepare some basic food and create their own recipes.48
E5这在中国是不可想象的。因为我们并没有这样实用的生活化课程。那这些课程是选修还是必修？你们不担心学习这些课程会影响学生主课的学习？It’s unimaginable in China, as we don’t have these kinds of practical courses that are so closely related to daily life. Then are these subjects elective or compulsory? Don’t you worry that studying these subjects will affect their results in core subjects?
M5The Year 7-8 technology course is mandatory. After that the course is elective but students can choose, for example, food technology as one of their HSC courses. This means the grade for that course will be taken into account when students apply for university.42
E6这种体系很好，能让学生尽早认识自己的兴趣所在，从而能够在进入大学时明智地选择专业。但可惜，这在中国还行不通。It’s a good system. It can help students find out what they are interested in as early as possible so that they can choose their majors wisely when entering university. But it is infeasible in China, which is quite unfortunate.
M6Yes, different countries have different situations. China’s huge population does pose quite a challenge for workers in the education sector.20

entry.title: 儿子入学
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

This is a dialogue between Mr. Gao and a school principal about enrolling Mr. Gao’s son in school.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Gao. Thank you for coming to this enrolment appointment for Jeremy.14
M1早上好。我已经带来了注册入学所需要的文件。这是市政税的账单，上面有我们的住址。这是 Jeremy 的出生证明。Good morning. I have brought here all the required documents for enrolment. This is my council tax bill with our residential address. And this is Jeremy’s birth certificate.
E2Thank you. Well, on top of that, we still need his immunisation history statement from the Australian Childhood Immunisation Register.20
M2哦，这我也带来，给你。我还有个担心。虽然 Jeremy 在国内也学习过英语，但这毕竟不是他的母语。我怕他上课跟不上。Yes, I have it here too. Here you are. Now I still have one concern. Jeremy has studied English in China, but English is not his mother tongue. I’m worrying that he may have difficulty keeping up in class
E3I understand your concern. Fortunately, we have ‘English as a Second Language’ programs, which can support students who have recently arrived in Australia and Australian born students from a non-English speaking background.32
M3那他是否能在学校学习中文呢？一方面，我希望他能够继承祖先的文化，另一方面，能说双语也是他未来发展的优势。
E4Although there are no Chinese language classes available at our school, Jeremy can attend the Saturday School of Community Languages. We also have resources to promote different traditions in language and culture.Can he study Chinese at school? Not only do I hope he can carry on the Chinese culture, but being bilingual can be an advantage for his career development.
M4那太好了。Jeremy 在中国的时候已经读完了 2 年级，他在这里应该从几年级开始读呢？他会学习哪些科目呢？
E5I think he should start from Year 2 since the curriculum here is quite different. He will be learning English, Mathematics, Science and Technology, Human Society and its Environments, Personal Development, Health and Physical Education and Creative Arts.That’s great. Jeremy had finished Grade 2 in China. What year should he start from here? What subjects will he study?
M5这些课程听上去很高级啊，比如科技和人类社会什么的，中国的小学生都不学这个的。如果 Jeremy 觉得有困难，你们是否能够提供辅导来帮助他进步呢？
E6Yes. Students who experience learning difficulties in grasping the basics are supported through the Learning Assistance Program at their local school. Students in primary and secondary school may receive additional assistance in literacy, numeracy and language.These courses all sound quite advanced. Chinese students do not study technology and human society in primary schools. If Jeremy has difficulty, can you provide tutoring to help him progress?
M6那我就放心了。校长先生，希望我儿子入校后你能够关注他的成长，有什么问题随时联系我，谢谢。

entry.title: 组织野营
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

A parent has received a notice from the school about a school camp. He was worried and made an appointment to see the headmaster.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1Nice to meet you, Mr. Hu. I understand that you’re worried about the camp for the Year 7 students.19
M1我当然担心了！我就这么一个女儿，我得充分确保她的安全！这野营是必须的吗？Of course I’m worried. She is my only daughter. I have to make sure she is completely safe. Is this camp compulsory?
E2No, not at all. However, we would like all the students to go. This is an educational activity and it’s also an important part of the school curriculum.28
M2多花点时间在学校学习不是更有教育意义吗？对我来说，这似乎是浪费时间与金钱。组织野营活动到底有什么意义？Isn’t spending more time on studying at school more educational? To me, it seems a waste of time and money. What’s the point of arranging this camp?
E3It is not going to be a holiday. The children will be learning a great deal on the camp. It offers a new learning experience, which is different from traditional classroom learning. They will live on a farm for three days, observing and experiencing what farm life is really like.50
M3在一个农场住三天？学校难道就没有比去农场更好的事情做了吗？这个农场有什么特别的？Living on a farm for three days? Doesn’t the school have better things to do than visiting a farm? What’s special about this farm?
E4It’s a mixed farm, specially set up to show children the different kinds of farms there are and all the daily activities that happen on a farm. Children can see for themselves how different animals are cared for and they even have a chance to help milk the cows.49
M4听起来如果我女儿参加的话，会玩得很开心。我带她去了今年的复活节农展会，她很喜欢。不过，这样的经历真的是学校教育的必要组成吗？It sounds like my daughter will have fun if she goes. I took her to this year’s Easter Show, she enjoyed it very much. But is this experience really a necessary part of school education?
E5Most of the children in urban schools drink milk and eat bread and eggs everyday but they have no idea where these things come from. They can find out all the information first hand through personal observation and experience.39
M5你说得对。这可比单纯从书本上来获得这些知识要好得多。可是我女儿对乳制品和鸡蛋过敏，我们必须非常小心她的饮食。You are right. It’s so much better than merely learning about this kind of information from books. But my daughter is allergic to dairy and eggs. We have to be very careful about her food and drink intake.
E6If that really worries you, you can choose not to let your daughter go. As I said before, we are not forcing any of the students to go.28
M6我确实不愿意让我女儿冒这个风险。但我会回家先问问她的意见，再来回复您。谢谢。I really don’t want my daughter to take the risk. But I will talk to her first at home before getting back to you.

entry.title: 受辱转队
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

This dialogue takes place at a basketball club between a mandarin speaking parent of a teenage boy and an Australian senior sports coach, Mr. Harris.”

IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Lin. You sounded quite worried over the phone. How can I help you?16
M1哈里斯先生，感谢您能提早赶来见我。我想让我儿子转到另外一个球队，所以想听听您的意见。Mr. Harris, thank you for coming early to see me. I’d like to have my son transferred to another team, so I’m hoping you can give me some advice.
E2Well, generally I wouldn’t recommend moving kids around in the middle of the season, but can you tell me why you think he needs to change to another team?29
M2我觉得他呆在 B 队并不开心。我问过他很多次，但他总说没什么。我觉得可能队友欺负他，或歧视他。I don’t think he is happy staying in Team B. I’ve asked him many times but he always says there’s nothing to worry about. I think his teammates may be bullying him or discriminating against him.
E3If it is true that he is being bullied or discriminated against as you suspect, this is a very serious matter. There are certain procedures we should follow to make sure such behaviour is stopped immediately.36
M3感谢您能严肃对待此事。我怀疑的依据是我儿子训练回家后所说的一些小事，比如“为什么我不像其他人一样出生在澳洲”或者“他们总排斥我”。Thank you for taking this matter seriously. My suspicion stems from some little things my son mentioned when he came home from training, like “why was I not born in Australia like everybody else” or “they always exclude me”.
E4We’ll first need to find out if your suspicion is justified. Could you have a talk with your son to find out who said those things? Also, get him to tell you some examples of incidents that have upset him.40
M4我试试看吧，但我觉得他不一定会愿意说。我并不很擅长与人沟通，所以我对是否能做到你让我做的没有信心。I’ll give it a shot but I don’t think he is willing to tell me. I’m not good at communicating with people so I don’t have confidence in doing what you’ve asked me to do.
E5If that’s the case, you might want to have your son sit down with Chris Paul who is our club’s child protection officer. He also happens to be a practising counsellor. Your son should feel comfortable talking with Chris.39
M5这太好了。我儿子在家的时候，总是少言寡语的，但我觉得他可能会更愿意跟辅导员聊聊心事。那么，如果他真的是被欺负或受到歧视了呢？That’s great. My son doesn’t talk much at home, and I think he may be more willing to talk to a counsellor. What if he really has been bullied or discriminated against?
E6If we find out that your son has indeed been bullied or discriminated against, we will follow the government’s guidelines for solving such problems in sporting and recreation clubs.29
M6我希望我儿子能够像他最近迷上的 NBA 球员林书豪一样，克服这些困难，开开心心地参加自己热爱的运动。我今天回去先和他谈谈，然后我们下周见！I hope my son can overcome these difficulties and enjoy the sport he likes, like Jeremy Lin, his new NBA idol. I’ll talk with him when I get back and I’ll see you next week.

entry.title: 土澳之旅
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

A mandarin speaking travel agent is now visiting an overseas travel agent, enquiring about information regarding an eco-tourism resort in the world heritage rainforest area in Far North Queensland.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1谢谢你今天来见我。我知道在每年的这个时间你有多么的忙。Thank you for meeting me today. I know how busy you are around this time every year.
M1It’s a pleasure. You mentioned that you’d like to get some information about eco-tourism possibilities. What would you like to know exactly?22
