E2我有几个问题想咨询你。首先，有些人想参加美丽自然风光的旅行团。这个团的人对自然和当地传统文化非常敏感。你这方面有什么好介绍吗？I have several questions. Firstly, some of our guests want to join a tour to appreciate the natural beauty there. They are very interested in the natural scenery and local traditions and culture. What would you recommend?
M2This kind of tourism has become extremely popular in the last few years. There’s a fantastic new resort up in the Daintree rainforest.23
E3他们提供什么样的住宿呢？我的客人喜爱美丽的自然风光，但他们确实也不想睡在外或者条件很差的地方。What kind of accommodations do they provide? Even though my guests love beautiful natural scenery, they don’t want to camp in the open-air or stay in a run-down place.
M3This resort is world class. It has a four and a half star rating and the restaurant offers fresh fish from the Great Barrier Reef. The guests stay in air- conditioned villas with an en-suite bathroom. It also has two swimming pools and a spa that’s open 24 hours.48
E4听起来好极了。可以给游客提供什么样的活动呢？在热带雨林穿行安全吗？那里有没有毒蛇或鳄鱼？ 虽然是想亲眼看看野生动植物，但还是安全第一。Fantastic! What activities are provided for tourists? Is it safe to walk through the tropical rainforest? Are there any poisonous snakes or crocodiles? We’d like to see some wildlife, but we have to put safety first.
M4The cost of basic accommodation covers a lot of extras, including walking in the rainforest with a local Aboriginal guide and a full-day four-wheel drive tour. It’s very safe and they can go on a river cruise. So if they’d like to see crocodiles, that’s also easy to arrange.49
E5你提过大堡礁。游客在那里可以进行哪些活动呢？我知道那是一个潜水胜地，但这些旅客并不很有兴趣。You mentioned the Great Barrier Reef. What can tourists do around there? I know it is a great place for diving, but they are not very interested in that.
M5In addition to diving, guests can go swimming and fishing there or if they’d prefer, they can go horse riding and then come back and enjoy a free massage and spa treatment.32
E6最后一个问题，有机会了解土著人的艺术和文化吗？我之前体验过回旋镖，那确实令人印象深刻啊。Last question: Do they have the opportunity to learn about Aboriginal art and culture? I once tried throwing a boomerang, which I have fond memories of.
M6Yes, of course. The local Aboriginal people run various workshops and they are very welcoming of visitors.14

entry.title: 乐器检疫
entry.content:
·Audio Not Avaliable·

A Chinese tourist, Mr. Zhuang, is stopped by a customs officer, Alfred, at the airport.

IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, Sir. Please put your bags over here, and please open and unpack them for us.16
M1到底怎么回事？为什么我们需要打开自己的行李给你们检查呢？What is going on? Why do we need to open our luggage and have it checked by you?
E2Just put them up here and unlock them please. This is a routine quarantine inspection. What are in these bags? According to your passport, you’ve been visiting Thailand. Did you visit any farms while you were away?37
M2这是一个脏衣服袋子。那一个装了一些化妆品在里面。我们没有参观任何的农场。我们只和我的岳父母呆在首都或者一些大城市。It’s a laundry bag. That one has some cosmetics in it. We didn’t visit any farms. We just stayed in the capital and other big cities with my parents-in-law.
E3I see. What is in this case? Did you pack all these tea packets in between the contents of this case?21
M3那是小提琴箱子，给我妹妹的。一定是我的岳母把它们放在那里的，用来保护小提琴。她知道我妹妹喜欢绿茶，她没有意识到这是不允许的。That’s a violin case for my sister. It must have been my mother-in-law who put them in there to protect the violin. She knows my sister loves green tea but she wasn’t aware that it’s not allowed.
E4We’ll have to remove them. You can’t bring any loose plant material into Australia. In fact, you could be fined $200 but this time we’ll simply confiscate it. Have you got any souvenirs made of plant or animal products?39
M4我们在曼谷机场买了这个编织的麦杆草帽和相配的手袋。我还有两个木制的鼓在那边的袋子里。We bought this wheat straw hat and the matching handbag at Bangkok airport. We also have two wooden drums in the bag over there.
E5The hat and the bag are okay but we’ll have to fumigate the drums and we’ll return them to you in a week or so.25
M5这个熏蒸消毒过程会不会弄坏鼓的？它们很值钱的。我们是从一个非常有名的声誉很好的做鼓的人那里买的这个鼓，我有收据在这里。我们需要它们为两周后的音乐会排练。Will my drums get damaged by fumigation? They are pricey. We bought them from a very reputable craftsman and we have got the receipt here. We need them in the rehearsals for a concert we have in two weeks.
E6No. It won’t damage them at all. We fumigate musical instruments regularly. Please fill in this form and these tags with all the details and don’t worry, they’ll be ready in time for your rehearsals.42
M6那好吧，谢谢，希望你们能尽快把他们交还给我。Alright then. Thanks. I hope you can return them as soon as possible.

entry.title: 预定航班
entry.content:
This  dialogue  takes  place  between  a  travel  agent  at  Asian  Explorer  Holidays  and  a  Mandarin speaking client, who wants to book a business trip to Hong Kong.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Wu. Thanks for dropping in. Please take a seat.12
M1早上好，Jacob 小姐。很高兴昨天给我打电话。我想不明白要提前这么长的时间来预订去香港的航班。Good morning, Mr Jacob. I’m very glad you called yesterday. I don’t know why I have to book a flight to Hong Kong so far in advance.
E2Hong Kong is always very popular with holiday-makers and January is the peak season. When exactly would you like to travel and which class of seating would you like?29
M2我想订商务仓。我不喜欢太挤了。让我看看。会议是一月 11 号开始，但我想有几天的时间来买东西，见一见朋友。一月 8 号星期五有没有空位啊？I would like Business class. I like to have more personal space. Let me see… The meeting starts on the 11th of January but I’d like to have a few days to shop and meet friends. Do you have any seats available on Friday the 8th of January?
E3That’s fine. There are a couple of flights with seats available on the 8th. You could fly direct, leaving at 10 in the morning or go via Melbourne, leaving here at 1 in the afternoon.35
M3我想我愿意直飞。我不喜欢在机场里等待。随便哪家航空公司都行，只要不是中国南方航空的就行，他们的服务太烂了。I’d like to fly direct! I don’t like waiting at the airport. Any airline is fine with me as long as it’s not China Southern. Their service is terrible!
E4I’d recommend Cathay Pacific as it is a Hong Kong owned airline. I will just key this into the computer. Now, Mr. Wu, can I please have your first name and the initials of any other names, if you have them?41
M4国泰不错的，我以前坐过的。我的个人名字是 Ming。我没有其他的字首缩写。Cathay Pacific is pretty good. I have flown with them before. My first name is Ming and I don’t have any other names.
E5Ming Wu; Cathay Pacific, flight CX131 to Hong Kong departing Sydney, on the 8th of January 2012, at 9.15 am. That’s all in order. Now, how would you like to pay?31
M5信用卡吧。对了，如果我要改变行程，我是跟你联系还是跟航空公司联系呢？需不需要收取费用呢？By Credit card, please. By the way, if I need to change my itinerary, should I contact you or the airline? Are there any fees involved?
E6You can ring the airline directly to make changes to your itinerary. You need to tell them your Booking Reference:  Number. They won’t charge you if there are seats available in business class.33
M6我明白了。多谢你的帮助。I see. Thanks for your help.

entry.title: 赴华之旅
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a Chinese trip organiser Mrs. Gao and Mr. Smith about organising a study tour to China.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mrs Gao, welcome to Melbourne. It’s good to see you again.12
M1很高兴又见到了你史密斯先生。谢谢你去年组织一个学习参观团到中国与我们见面。我认为那十分的成功。It is very nice to see you again, Mr. Smith. Thank you for organizing the study tour to visit us in China last year. I thought it was very successful.
E2I thought so too. That’s why I’ve invited you here today. I’d like to discuss with you the opportunity to organise another study tour to China this year. I’m looking at maybe autumn this time.35
M2那太好了。秋天正是游览中国的最好季节之一。你认为会有多少人参与这个学习参观团？他们有一个什么样的背景？Fantastic! Autumn is one of the best seasons for traveling around China. How many people do you expect will join the tour? What backgrounds do they have?
E3There will be approximately 20 well-educated, professionals from various backgrounds. They want to understand more about Chinese culture and history. So I think they should definitely visit some of the key places of cultural and historical significance.37
M320 人应该比较好安排。我完全同意你。我认为他们应该参观北京，南京当然还有西安。这三个是中国非常有名的具有历史意义的地方。Twenty people should be easy to deal with. I totally agree with you. I believe they should visit Beijing, Nanjing and of course Xi’an. These are very famous cities with historical significance.
E4That sounds good. Some of them are also interested in spending some time enjoying the natural scenery in the countryside. Is it possible to arrange something like that?28
M4是的，没问题。当他们参观这些城市的时候他们也有机会看到中国非常漂亮的风景。比如去西安时可以顺道游览一下雄壮险峻的华山。Yes, sure. They will have opportunities to appreciate some spectacular scenery when they visit these cities. For example, we can go and experience the magnificent Huashan Mountain on the way to Xi’an.
E5Now, the group doesn’t wish to spend too much money on accommodation. They would prefer to stay somewhere like the Holiday Inn, which they think would be adequate.28
M5没问题。我们会尽量帮他们预定当地的假日酒店，或者是标准相近的酒店。我们还能保证旅行团到的每一个地方有当地的导游作陪。No problem. We will try to book the Holiday Inn, or hotels of a similar standard for them. We can also guarantee that wherever the group goes, they will have a local tour guide with them.
E6That’s excellent. A local guide will be able to answer any questions they have. We are looking forward to the trip already.22
M6我也期待他们能来到中国。我会尽快算出行程大约要花费多少钱，并且连同详细行程单一起发邮件给你。I also look forward to seeing them in China. I will work out an approximate cost for the whole trip as soon as possible. Then I will email it to you as well as a detailed itinerary.

entry.title: 虫草检疫
entry.content:
A Chinese tourist, Mr. Wang, is stopped by a customs officer at the airport.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Mr Wang, the interpreter is here so now we’ll be able to understand each other.15
M1多谢你们的安排。我刚才在担心呢，害怕你们会因为我不会说英语，无法辩白而把我抓起来。Thanks for arranging this. I was worried that you would arrest me because I don’t speak English and cannot defend myself.
E2Now, can you tell me, what is your purpose for travel? I know you are on a visitor’s visa. Where will you be staying in Australia and how long do you intend to stay?34
M2我的女儿女婿在悉尼工作，我这次来看望他们，准备在他们家住两个月。入境卡上的地址就是他们家的地址。My daughter and her husband work in Sydney. I came here to visit them and I will stay at their place for two months. It’s their address that I have put on the incoming passenger card.
E3All right. We’ve stopped you at customs because we found this bag, with suspicious contents, in your luggage and we believe it contains pests. Can you tell me what is in this bag?33
M3这是一包中草药，需要煎着喝的。我女儿的肠胃功能比较弱，中药能帮她调理调理。This is a bag of Chinese herbs, which need to be boiled in water before taking. My daughter has poor digestion and Chinese medicine is able to help improve her condition.
E4But this is something you need to declare. Did your daughter ask you to bring this for her? Did you watch the Australian Quarantine video about what goods should be declared on the plane?34
M4不是我女儿要我带的，是我自己要带给她的。我不懂英语，所以视频里说了什么我也不知道。入境卡上除了个人信息，我也是看人家怎么填我就怎么填的。My daughter didn’t ask me to. It was my own idea to bring her these. I don’t speak English so I didn’t understand what the video said. As for the information on the card, except the personal details, I just copied what others wrote.
E5This dried plant material is very likely to harbour pests and diseases. Oh, there is a bug crawling. I think it is a khapra beetle, which is the number one pest we want to keep out of Australia.38
M5我不知道里面怎么会有活的虫子的。那你们要拿它怎么样呢？你们要没收就没收吧，但我真的是无意触犯贵国的法律。I don’t know why there are living bugs in it. What are you going to do about it? You can confiscate it if you want, but I really didn’t mean to break the law.
E6We have to seize and destroy it because it is contaminated with pests. I’m going to give you a written warning and please be aware, next time when you travel, you must declare if you are bringing in something like this.41
M6我知道了，我保证以后不会再犯同样的错误。I understand. I promise I won’t make the same mistake again.

entry.title: 临时入住
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Li, a Chinese-speaking guest, and Herbert, a hotel concierge. Mr Li has just arrived at the hotel and wants to check in.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good Afternoon! My name is Herbert, how can I help you, sir?12
M1不好意思， 我们没有提前做预订， 但是， 我们想要一个带浴室的双 人房，有漂亮的风景的。不知道你们有没有这样的空房？Sorry, we didn’t make a booking in advance. But we’d like to have a double room with a private bathroom and a nice view. Do you have such a room available?
E2Yes, we still have one double room with a harbour view available. It’s on the top floor with a bath and a shower. How many nights will you be staying with us?32
M2听起来很不错。 我们今晚肯定是要住这里， 或许还有明天晚上， 这 要看你们提供的价位怎样。还有，你们接受运通卡吗？Sounds good. We’ll stay here definitely tonight and perhaps tomorrow night as well. It depends on the price you offer. One more thing, do you accept American Express?
E3The room costs $200.00 per night for two people. We can provide an extra bed if it’s for three guests, but you need to pay an additional $50.00 per night for that. And yes, we do accept American Express.
M3就我跟我朋友两个人。 这个价位可以接受。 那我们就定两晚吧。 给 你。 对了，今天下午我们想在这个城市周围逛逛， 而且我们晚上想 吃海鲜。你推荐我们去哪里呢？It’s just the two of us, myself and my friend. The price sounds reasonable so we would like to stay two nights. By the way, we’d love to stroll around the city and have some seafood for dinner. Do you have any recommendations?
E4You can catch a free tram called the city circle near the main entrance of the hotel. It will take you to several sightseeing spots around the city. Our hotel’s restaurant offers a fantastic seafood buffet dinner. It costs $60 per person, but you will receive a 20% discount as you are staying as guests at the hotel.
M4我在旅行指南上看到过你们酒店的海鲜自助， 据说还挺受欢迎的， 或许我们今天应该试试。 能否告诉我，从你们酒店这里到机场要多 长时间？I know your hotel’s seafood buffet is quite popular. I have read about it in a tourist guidebook. Maybe we should give it a try today. How long does it take to get to the airport from here?
E5It usually takes only half an hour but I suggest you allow an hour just to be on the safe side. May I have your card please, and would you please print your full name and address on this form?
M5给你。当然， 都写好了。 所以后天我们会退房。 您可以帮我们在那 天叫一辆九点钟的出租车吗？我们能不能有一个早上六点的叫醒服 务?Here you are. Sure. All done. So we will check out the day after tomorrow. Can you help us book a taxi for 9am on that day? And can we have a morning call service at 6am as well?
E6Certainly. Oh, one more thing. We’ve had some issues in the past with the bath. It only takes 3 minutes to fill so please watch it so it doesn’t overflow. You will have to pay for any water damage if the carpet gets wet.
M6谢谢你的提醒，我会小心的。Thanks for warning us. We’ll be careful.

entry.title: 儿童津贴
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a customer (C) and a Centrelink officer regarding the baby bonus.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, please take a seat. How can I help you?11
M1是这样，我和我的老公正在考虑从中国领养一个婴儿。我听说有新生儿的父 母可以领取儿童津贴。但是我不太了解如果我领养了孩子可不可以拿到，你 能给我提供更多的关于这个的信息?Well, my husband and I are thinking of adopting a baby from China. I heard that parents with a new born baby are eligible to get the Baby Bonus, but I have no idea if I can get it for my adopted child. Can you please give me more information about it?
E2Well, the Baby Bonus is a payment provided to parents who are having a baby or adopting a child. We also provide related services to parents in addition to the financial support which helps cover the many costs involved with raising a child.43
M2这就是说我领养的小孩也可以拿到补贴是吗? 你能在详细给我解释下我都应 该怎么做来申领这个津贴吗？That means I am entitled to the Baby Bonus for my adopted child too, right? Can you explain in more detail what I should do to claim it?
E3OK. If you adopt a child from overseas you can add them to your Medicare card from the date the child arrives in Australia. This is provided the child is either on a permanent resident visa or a temporary visa with an application lodged for permanent residency.47
M3哦，我明白了。除了这个还有别的什么要求吗? 还有什么其他的我可以领取 的津贴吗？Oh, I see. Apart from this, are there any other requirements we need to meet? Are there any other payments available for me?
E4An income test applies to the Baby Bonus. This is based on your family’s income for the first six months after your child is born or adopted. If your family does not qualify for the Baby Bonus, you may be eligible for the Paid Parental Leave scheme.47
M4带薪育儿假计划? 听说过。领养小孩可以享受产假听起来不错。我从来没有 过小孩，对于这些政策是一窍不通。Paid Parental Leave scheme? I’ve heard of that. It’s pretty good that parents who adopt a child are also eligible for parental leave. I have never had a child before, so I know nothing about those policies.
E5I know. If you are eligible for both payments, you can only choose one of them. This is based on which payment is the best decision for your family financially. So I suggest you think about which payment to claim before you send in the application.46
M5好的，我会好好计算一下每一笔津贴我能领取的金额。 还有一个问题， 如 果我带孩子回中国住的话，我还可以拿到津贴吗。Sure, I will calculate carefully how much I can receive for each payment. One more question, what if I take my child to live in China? Can I still receive the payments?
E6Perhaps, but please be aware that payments can be paid for temporary absences of up to 13 weeks outside Australia, in most circumstances. However, you won’t get any payment beyond that period.32
M6我明白了。谢谢您的详细解释。如果有什么问题我会再过来咨询的。I see. Thank you for your detailed explanation. If I have any questions, I will come back to you.

entry.title: 戒烟专线
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. Bennett, a health advisor from I-Can-Quit NSW, and Mr. Lin, who is planning to quit smoking.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mr Lin. I’m glad to see you here today. How can I help you?16
M1早上好，Bennett 先生。我太太劝我戒烟，因为我们打算要宝宝了。所以，我今天过来寻求一些专业的建议。Good morning, Mr. Bennett. My wife is urging me to quit smoking as we’re planning to have a baby. So I’m here today to get some professional advice.
E2That’s good to hear. There will be many benefits to you if you quit. Tell me about your smoking; how long have you smoked.”24
M2我今年 30 岁了。但我刚 20 岁出头的时候，就开始吸烟了。一开始，我就是觉得吸烟是一件很酷的事，但没想到会上瘾。I’m 30, but I started smoking in my early twenties. In the beginning, I was only doing it as I thought it was cool. But I didn’t expect that I would get addicted.
E3Well, that’s often the case for many young adults who smoke. They are tempted to try smoking for fun without thinking much about the severe consequences, until one day they find it’s impossible to stop.35
M3对，就是这样。我现在呢，每天都至少要抽一整包的烟。我太太总抱怨说我就像个烟囱似的。Yes, exactly. Now I have to smoke at least one pack a day. My wife often complainsthat I smoke like a chimney.
E4If you are already aware of the health risks associated with smoking, you should also understand that kicking this bad habit is not just good for you but for those around you.32
M4是的，我懂。其实，我很早以前就想戒烟了。我甚至自己都尝试过几次，但所有的努力最终还是失败了。Yes. I do. Actually I’ve been wanting to stop for a long time. I have even tried a few times by myself, but all my efforts ended in failure.)Yes. I do. Actually I’ve been wanting to stop for a long time. I have even tried a few times by myself, but all my efforts ended in failure.)Yes. I do. Actually I’ve been wanting to stop for a long time. I have even tried a few times by myself, but all my efforts ended in failure.)Yes. I do. Actually I’ve been wanting to stop for a long time. I have even tried a few times by myself, but all my efforts ended in failure.)
E5Don’t worry. We are here to provide you with professional guidance. We’ll make a plan together and support you with follow-up calls and consultations, until you are able to make a positive change.33
M5但我还是没有信心。我怕我抵抗不了烟瘾。之前我尝试戒烟的时候，整天都头痛、恶心，吃饭也没有胃口。太难受了！But I still have no confidence. I’m afraid I won’t be able to resist the cravings. I was suffering from headaches, nausea and a poor appetite all day long last time I tried to stop smoking. It’s awful!
E6Those are common withdrawal symptoms caused by the addiction to nicotine. I believe with your willpower and our tailor-made program, you will win this battle eventually.26
M6那好吧。希望这次真的可以戒烟成功。那就请您为我制定合适的计划吧。Alright. I hope I can really quite for good this time. So please make a suitable plan for me.

entry.title: 家暴求助
entry.content:
Susan, a Mandarin-speaking woman, has made an appointment with a support worker at the Women and Children Support Centre in Melbourne. She wishes to discuss issues she’s been facing with her husband at home.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hi! Susan, is it? Please take a seat. Oh, I see you’re expecting. How far along are you?18
M1我怀孕五个月了。这是我第三个孩子。上周我才知道怀的是个女孩儿。I’m five months pregnant. It’s my third child. I just found out last week that it’s a girl.
E2That’s fantastic. Congratulations! So what brings you here today? Are you facing any kind of difficulties at the moment? If I may ask, are you single, married or do you have a partner?33
M2我结婚了，嫁给了一个澳洲男人。我们在一起 6 年了。您瞧，我的邻居建议 我来见您，因为我的家庭一直存在些问题。我过去几周情况变得越来越糟 了。现在我很担心我孩子们的安全。I’m married to an Australian man. We’ve been together for 6 years. Look, my neighbour recommended I see you because of the problems I’ve been having at home. Over the past few weeks it’s just been getting worse and worse. And now I am worried about the safety of my children.
E3I understand. It sounds like you’re talking about domestic violence. You did the right thing in taking your neighbor’s advice. Susan, please trust me when I say that you can open up to me and tell me your problems and I will try our best to help you out.61
M3我和我丈夫时不时会吵架。我们吵架时，他有时会把我绊倒或者扯我的头 发。有几次他还打我们的两个儿子。你知道, 他们还很小，他们一个 5 岁， 一个才 3 岁。My husband and I have quarrels from time to time. When we get into a row he sometimes does things like trip me over or pull my hair. He has also beaten our two sons a few times. You know, they are still quite young, one 5 and the other only 3.
E4His behaviour is certainly unacceptable! There is no excuse for violence against women or children. Everyone has the right to feel safe in their own home. If we work together, we can find a way to protect both you and the children.42
M4我觉得我很傻，我当初真不该认识他。这么多年我一直想申请暴力禁止令， 但是因为害怕我丈夫所以我就没这么做。我真是太懦弱了。I feel so stupid. I regret the day I ever met him. Over the years I’ve considered applying for an AVO, but I didn’t do it out of fear of my husband. I’m such a coward.
E5You’re not a coward, Susan. Anyone in your situation would feel the same. But you’ve taken a positive step by coming to see me today. Can you tell me, Susan，have you ever suffered from depression or anxiety?38
M5有时我觉得活着没意思。我甚至还考虑过自杀。但我一想到我再也见不到我 的孩子们了我就改变了主意。他们让我的生命有了光彩，没有他们我什么都 不是。Sometimes I feel like life is not worth living. I’ve even considered suicide. But when I think about not being able to see my kids anymore I change my mind. They light up my life and I’m nothing without them.
E6Look, Susan, I’ve been a counsellor here at the Women and Children Support Centre for over 10 years, and during that time I’ve met many women in similar situations to you. For most women who don’t apply for an intervention order, the situation gets worse and the consequences can be serious.51
M6我知道了，我之前读过干涉令的一些信息。但是如果我要申请的话，我和孩 子去哪住呢？我在墨尔本没有亲人。而且现在我所有的朋友都不来找我了， 就是因为我老公的原因。I know. I’ve read some information about intervention orders. But if I do apply for one, where are my children and I supposed to live? I’ve got no relatives in Melbourne, and none of my friends come to see me anymore because of my husband.
E7There are refuges available that provide safe accommodation for people in this situation. The staff there coud give you practical and emotional support. I’m not saying it’s an easy process. But the most important thing is that you have a support network here for you. You are not alone in this.51
M7谢谢你今天跟我聊这么多。我又重新感觉生活有希望了。Thank you for talking with me today. I feel like there is some hope again.

entry.title: 起始津贴
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr Zhang, who has just lost his job, and a Centrelink officer, John
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mr Zhang, I’m your case officer. How can I help you today?14
M1早上好，我的工厂最近裁员，不幸的是我是其中的一个。没有这个收入，我 的家庭现在生活很困难。Good morning. My factory has recently laid off employees, and unfortunately I was one of them. Without this income, my family is having difficulty making ends meet.
E2In terms of your situation, I think we are able to help you through a variety of payments such as the Family Tax Benefit and Newstart Allowance. First of all, I need to determine whether you are eligible for these allowances. Can I have a look at your ID please?50
M2给你。我们全家三年前就成为澳大利亚永久居民了。但从来都没问政府要过 救济。我也希望我可以尽快找到工作，这样就可以不需要靠救济金度日了。Here it is. My family and I became permanent residents of Australia three years ago. But we have never asked for government benefits. I hope I can find a job soon so that I don’t need to count on your payments.
E3Don’t worry. It’s our responsibility to help people get through difficulties. Since you meet the residency requirement, you should be able to claim the Newstart Allowance if you also pass the income and assets tests.35
M3好的，如果能在这个时候得到一笔钱，真的是雪中送炭呀。您能为我解释一 下怎样才能通过收入与资产测试？Okay. It would be such a timely relief if I could get some money now. Could you explain to me how I can pass the income and assets test?
E4You may not receive Newstart Allowance straight away if you earn $600 per fortnight. You can refer to our website if you need to acquire more information about this. Now, I believe you should be aware of some job seeker workshops that will increase your chances of getting employed as soon as possible.53
M4我还听说你们有举办帮助寻工者尽快就业的讲习班？我有资格参加么？都会 涉及到哪些内容？我需要做什么？I also heard that you have some workshops to help job seekers get employed as soon as possible. Am I eligible to register? What will be covered in these workshops? What do I need to do?
E5Of course, you are. The workshops cover a wide range of topics including local labour market opportunities, job search techniques and skills that are in demand and other services. As part of the workshop, you are required to pass an activity test to continue claiming the allowance.47
M5寻工活动评估？那是关于什么的？是意味着我必须参加这些活动, 不然就没 有补助金?如果偶尔不去，会对我的收入有影响吗？Activity test? What’s that about? Does it mean I have to participate in all the activities, otherwise no payment? If I miss it occasionally, will it affect the payments that I’m about to claim?
E6It is used to measure if you are actively looking for a job through participating in employment expos and workshops. After we design a plan and you have agreed to it, you’ll have to participate in the activities. Otherwise, there will be a “No show, no pay” penalty.48
M6好的我知道了。谢谢您的解释。Got it. Thanks for the explanation.

entry.title: 正齿计划
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Ms. Zhong and a dentist, Dr James. This dialogue is about Ms. Zhong’s daughter’s teeth problems.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon Ms. Zhong. Thank you for coming in. Please have a seat.13
M1牙医您好。我想请您看一下我女儿 Amy 的牙齿。她的上下两排牙齿都不怎么齐，我觉得可能有必要矫正一下。Hello doctor. I’d like you to check my daughter Amy’s teeth. Her upper and lower teeth are not very straight. I think it may be necessary to straighten them.
E2Let me see. You are right. Amy definitely has some alignment problems. Some of her teeth are overlapping, and some others have gaps between them. She also has an overbite.30
M2是啊，我担心以后影响美观，还有就是我发现她刷牙不容易刷干净，可能也是因为牙齿太参差不齐了。That’s right. I’m worried that it may affect her appearance in the future. I also noticed that she has difficulty brushing her teeth properly, probably because her teeth are too crooked.
E3That could be part of the reason indeed. Fortunately, your daughter is only 14 years old, and these problems can easily be fixed by wearing braces.26
M3我和她爸爸也是这么想的。如果趁年纪还小矫正过来，这对孩子长大以后的自信心也有帮助。Her father and I think so too. If the problems can be fixed when she is still young, it will also be good for her self-confidence when she grows up.
E4Exactly. Many of my patients have self-esteem issues. They tend to be very self-conscious when smiling or speaking, and not as confident in general.24
M4这正是我所担心的，我希望我女儿 成为一个开朗活泼的人。那您看我女儿的情况，要多久才能矫正过来？That’s exactly what I’m concerned about. I hope my daughter grows up to be a happy, outgoing person. In my daughter’s case, how long do you think it will take for her teeth to be fixed?
E5It may take up to three years. I need to watch how much force to put on her teeth and let them move bit by bit. Too much force is likely to damage the gums.35
M5虽然时间很长，不过从长远来看也是有必要的。那怎么开始做呢？请您为我们安排。That’s quite a long time, but it’ll be worth it in the long run. How should we start then? Please arrange it for us.
E6Sure. I’ll make another appointment with you and your daughter in the following week, then I can examine her teeth more closely and make a treatment plan. How’s 10am next Thursday?31
M6没问题，到时我们再来。谢谢您。No problem. We’ll be here then. Thank you.

entry.title: 谈性骚扰
entry.content:
This dialogue is an interview between a mandarin-speaking journalist and a guest professor who is an expert in the study of sexual harassment.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1您的到场让我倍感荣幸，教授。非常感谢您能以专业的视角探讨性骚扰这 个话题，并和我们分享一些您的看法。I am very honoured by your presence, professor. I will be highly grateful if you could offer your professional perspectives on the topic of sexual harassment and share with me some of your points of view on this issue.
M1And I am very glad to be here and thanks for having me. I will try and address your issues to the very best of my ability.27
E2太好了。谢谢。现在人们的共识是，性骚扰就是指在性的方面骚扰某人。 这个定义准确吗？Great. Thank you. Now it is common knowledge that sexual harassment indicates bothering someone in a sexual way. Is this an accurate definition?
M2No, I am afraid not. It is in fact a violation of the Civil Rights Act. The confusing aspect is that perpetrators are not arrested. This is because sexual harassment is not a violation of state criminal codes. Rather, remedy is through civil litigation.44
E3谢谢您为我们解释清楚。那么，就定义而言，如果我说，工作场所的性骚 扰可以发生在任何时间、任何地点，发生在任何两个人之间，这种说法对吗？Thanks for your clarification. Speaking of the definition, am I right in saying that workplace sexual harassment can occur anytime, anywhere, between any two people?
M3Not really. As in the discussion above, workplace sexual harassment can occur outside the office, but it cannot occur between any two people. The harassment must take place between people who work together.33
E4我明白了。那骚扰者一定必须是老板或者上级吗？如果是客户或顾客呢？ 如果他们做出不当性关注，会怎么样呢？这算是性骚扰吗？I see. So the harasser has to be a boss or supervisor? What about a client or a customer? What if they make unwanted sexual advances? Can it be regarded as sexual harassment?
M4The same definition applies. If the harasser is a co-worker, not a boss, it can also be regarded as sexual harassment. The workplace is supposed to be a safe place where employees are protected from any inappropriate or promiscuous actions by customers, vendors, or clients.45
E5如果不当触碰只发生了一次会怎样？那就不算性骚扰，是吗？我听说性骚 扰的受害者并不一定是异性，对吗？What if the unwanted touching occurs only once, that is not a sexual harassment, is it? I’ve also heard that the victim of sexual harassment does not necessarily have to be of the opposite gender, do they?
M5That is correct; people can also be capable of committing sexual harassment against individuals of the same sex under current laws. However, many people are not aware of this yet. And as to your other question, even if it only occurs once it is still considered sexual harassment.48
E6是啊。很多人对于同性之间的性骚扰仍然存在一些误解。下面我问个棘手 的问题，根据刑法，旁观者可能成为性骚扰的受害者吗？True. Still many people have some misunderstandings when it comes to sexual harassment occurring among people of the same sex. Now this is a tricky issue, according to criminal law, can a bystander be the victim of sexual harassment?
6.1The truth is, criminal law stipulates that the victim can be anyone who is affected by offensive conduct. Let me break it down for you, a more likely example would be one in which person A (a supervisor) is having a sexual relationship with person B (a subordinate).48
6.2Person C (a subordinate) is not getting promotions due to the fact that he or she is not in a sexual relationship with person A. This type of favouritism creates a discriminatory situation for person C as well as the potential for coercion due to the implication that C must have sex with A in order to get ahead.59
M6非常感谢您为我答疑解惑。您对于性骚扰这一复杂问题的详细解释让我受益匪浅。Many thanks for answering my questions and addressing my concerns, I learned a lot from your explanations on these complicated issues regarding sexual harassment.

entry.title: 赴澳买房
entry.content:
Scenario: Mr Zhang is seeking advice from a real estate agent about buying property.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1早上好，我和我家人刚刚移民到澳洲，想尽快买房子，我在报纸上看到了你们的广告，你们可以帮助我吗？Good morning. My family and I just migrated to Australia, and want to buy a property as soon as possible. I’ve read your advertisement in the newspaper. Can you help me with that?
M1Good morning! Absolutely, I would be more than happy to assist you. We have various types of properties in different price ranges. Could you tell me what type of property you’re after?32
E2我家有 4 口人，所以想要个大一点的房子，要有 4 个卧室，3 个卫生间。有个大的后花园，我们可以种一些植物，我太太喜欢园艺。My family has 4 members in total, so I’d like to have a bigger house with 4 bedrooms and 3 bathrooms. We also need a big back yard where we can grow some plants. My wife loves gardening.
M2There shouldn’t be any problem. We have a long list of properties falling into that category. First of all, could you tell me which suburb you would like to live in?31
E3我的朋友建议过 Hurstville 或是 Chatswood，因为那里华人比较多，所以对于英文不好的我们来说，会比较方便。另外，我和太太白天要去 city 工作，所以不想离市中心太远。My friend suggested Hurstville or Chatswood, because of the big Chinese communities there. It would be more convenient for people like us with poor English. Besides, my wife and I both need to work in the city during the day, so we don’t want to live too far from the city.
M3I see. Both of the places you just mentioned are not too far from the city; usually, it takes about 20 mins by train. I’d also suggest Burwood and Strathfield as they would suit your needs and have better housing conditions.41
E4是吗？ Strathfield 也住着很多华人吗？记得我朋友说，那里主要是韩国人啊。我不太了解这两个区。您可以解释的更详细点吗？Really? Are there many Chinese living in Strathfield as well? My friend told me most of the residents there are Korean. I don’t really know much about those two suburbs. Could you describe them to me in a little more detail?
M4Sure. Strathfield is now one of the main suburbs with a big Chinese population. The properties we have in Burwood and Strathfield are newly renovated and in a leafy and quiet residential area. They are close to shopping centres as well as local parks where children can play after school. Of course, the price there could be a little higher than Hurstville.62
E5我明白了。根据我的估算，我大概可以承担不超过 80 万的房子。不知道这个价钱是否可以在 Burwood 买个合适的独栋房。但是，我要先看房子才能做决定。37I see. Based on my estimation, I can’t afford anything over $800,000. I don’t know if that’s enough to buy a good house in Burwood. But, I will make the decision after inspecting them.
M5I believe you can buy a very good house for that price in this area. I’ll show you the properties in all the areas and leave you to make a decision. Shall we make an appointment now to inspect the properties?41
E6好主意，明天早上 11 点好吗，我现在住在朋友家。我还没有买车，你能来我朋友家接我吗？Good idea. How about 11 am tomorrow? I’m living at my friend’s place at the moment. I haven’t got a car yet. Could you come here to pick me up?
M6Sure, I will pick you up at your friend’s place at 11 am tomorrow. Could you email me your friend’s address later, thanks?23

entry.title: 网瘾少女
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr David Brown, a school advisor, and Mrs Yu, a mother who is concerned about her daughter socialising on the internet.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Thanks for coming in on time, Mrs Yu. I’m David Brown. Nice to meet you.15
M1很高兴见到你，布朗先生。我发现近来我女儿总是花很长时间上网。这让我很担心。Nice to meet you too, Mr. Brown. I have noticed that my daughter has been spending too much time on the internet recently. It really worries me.
E2Well, children these days often have a strong thirst for knowledge and the internet has become their main source of all kinds of information. Do you have any particular concerns?30
M2是的。她爸爸上个月给她买了一台 iPad 作为生日礼物。但从那以后，她无论到哪里都带着那个 iPad，整天都在网上跟朋友聊天。Yes. Her father bought her an iPad for her birthday last month. Since then, she has been taking it with her everywhere she goes, chatting with friends online all the time.
E3Chatting on the net is very popular among young teenagers. It can be a great way to meet and talk to people from different time zones and backgrounds. However, risks also exist, especially in chat rooms.36
M3是这样的。我真的很想知道她究竟进的是哪些聊天室，跟什么人聊天。可是她拒绝告诉我，说那是她的隐私。That’s true. I really want to know which chat rooms she is visiting and who she is chatting with. But she refuses to tell me, saying that it’s personal.
E4Do you socialise online yourself? I know many parents are doing this as well so that they can add their children’s “friends” and see what they are doing.28
M4我基本连互联网都不怎么用，除非是为了我的生意，更别说那些五花八门的网络聊天应用程序了。这一代的小孩子的确比他们的父母更懂得用电脑。I don’t even use the internet very often unless it’s for my business, let alone all these kinds of online chat applications. In fact, children of this generation know more about computers than their parents.
E5I’d like to suggest you get involved in your daughter’s use of new technologies, as parental supervision is a must to protect children from potential dangers, such as cyber-bullying, identity theft, scams and harassment.34
M5您说的对。那我就让她来教我怎么上网聊天。或许，她会因为我的参与而更加愿意跟我分享她在网上的一些经历。I agree. I think I can get her to show me how it works. Maybe she’ll be more willing to share her online experiences with me if I’m more involved.
E6That’s a great idea. Keeping the lines of communication open is just as important as setting the rules. Give her the confidence that you’re always the right person to turn to.31
M6我会听从您的建议的。谢谢您的宝贵时间。I’ll do as you’ve suggested. Thank you for your time.

entry.title: 呼气测试
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a police officer and Mr. Zhang at an information session about random breath testing (RBT).
IDOriginalTranslated
E1By law, probationary drivers (P-platers) must maintain a zero BAC (Blood Alcohol Concentration). New alcohol laws affecting P-platers are expected to be introduced in January 2012. Does anyone have any other questions?32
M1我有。根椐现行法律，警察是否有权叫我靠边停车，让我做随机呼吸测试？ 或者这么说吧， 法律是不是规定他们随便什么时候想做随机呼吸测试都可 以？Yes. According to the current legal system, does a police officer have the authority to pull me over and randomly breathalyse me? Or let me put it this way, are they authorized by law to perform a RBT whenever they want to?
E2Thanks for your question. As you might already know, the police do have the power to randomly stop vehicles, but it does not mean they can perform random preliminary breath tests in any circumstances.34
M2我知道了。 也就是说他们并不能在任何情况下都有权利命令我们做呼吸测 试。那么，在什么情况下他们可以要求司机停车接受检查呢？I see. So they can’t perform breath tests whenever they want. Then, in what circumstances can they ask drivers to pull over and take a breath test?
E3They have the authority to require a person to comply with a preliminary breath test if they have reasonable suspicions that a person is driving, attempting to drive or is in charge of a motor vehicle on a road or other public place while under the influence of alcohol.49
M3很多人都认为喝了一点点酒再开车是没有那么危险的。但我并不这样认为。 您能否具体介绍一下什么是 BAC，它又是怎么个测算法的呢?Many people think that driving after drinking a little bit is not that dangerous. But I don’t agree. Would you please explain in detail what BAC is and how BAC is measured?
E4Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC) is a measurement of the amount of alcohol in the body. BAC is measured in grams of alcohol per 100 millilitres of blood. The legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit is 0.05. This means that a driver’s body must contain less than 50 milligrams of alcohol per 100 millilitres of blood.55
M4我觉得我有必要记住这些具体的规定。非常感谢您的解释。我还想问一下， 如果警察有合理的怀疑的话，是否有权在我家进行呼吸测试呢？我之前听说 有类似的情况。I think it’s necessary to memorize those specific regulations just in case. And I thank you for your clarifications. I wonder whether they are also authorized to require a breath test in my own home if they have reasonable suspicions. I have heard of such cases before.
E5I fully understand your concern. The truth of the matter is, as long as a police officer is not trespassing on a suspects property then any breath test requirement they make will be lawful, meaning you would have to comply with their requests.43
M5这个倒是说得通。还好，那如果是青少年犯罪的情况呢？我是说如果青少年 酒后驾驶的话，需要其监护人出面吗？Fine, that makes sense. What if the offender is a teenager? I mean if a teenager commits drink-driving, will their guardian need to be present?
E6In cases where a juvenile has been detained at a police station for a drink driving related offence there is no requirement for the police to wait for an appropriate adult to be present to commence the breath testing procedure. // The procedures do not constitute an interview and therefore should not be unduly delayed.54
M6你所提供的有关酒后驾驶的信息非常有用。谢谢您抽时间向我解释。Your explanations regarding drink-driving is very helpful and I greatly appreciate  your time.

entry.title: 脑伤就医
entry.content:
Mrs. Yang is consulting her GP about a head injury.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mrs Yang. The receptionist told me you have a deep cut on your head which needs to be treated urgently. How are you feeling now?27
M1是的，医生。我不小心割伤了额头，现在还是很疼，但是已经不流血了。 麻烦您先帮我看看。That’s correct, doctor. I accidentally cut my forehead. It’s very painful but has stopped bleeding. Please have a look at it for me.
E2Hmm, nothing too serious. The cut is still fresh, now we need to get that treated first. It shouldn’t leave any scaring. But how did you injure yourself?28
M2一小时前我在家晕倒了，醒来的时候我, 觉得额头有灼痛, 一照镜子，发现 了这么大一道伤口，我想我一定是晕倒后，头磕到了鞋架的边缘。I passed out at home one hour ago. After I regained consciousness, I felt a burning pain in my forehead. Then when I looked into the mirror, I found this big cut. I guess I must have hit my head on the edge of the shoe rack after I fell down.
E3I see. Have you passed out like this before? Apart from the pain, did you feel anything else after the fall? Like a headache or nausea? Have you noticed whether there are bumps or bruises on other parts of your head?38
M3没有，我从来没有这样过。估计是因为低血糖。头疼倒没有，可是我觉得 没有胃口，还有一点耳鸣。脑袋后面都有一点疼，我之前都没有觉察到。 医生，应该不会很严重吧？No, it’s the first time. I think maybe hypoglycaemia is the trigger. I don’t have a headache, but I have no appetite. Also my ears have been ringing. The back of my head is in a bit of pain but I didn’t notice that before. Is it serious, doctor?
E4I am trying to rule out the possibilities of concussion. Since your fall might have been quite hard, I am worried about the potential brain injuries, like mild concussion.28
M4天啊，脑震荡，不会这么严重吧？那我该怎么办呢？会不会有后遗症，我 需不需要做手术？Concussion? Oh dear! Is it that severe? So what should I do now? Is there going to be any after-effect? Do I need surgery?
E5I’ll get you to see Dr Merphy, the specialist. Don’t worry Mrs Yang. Like any condition, early detection is best. That’s why I want you to see a specialist soon. Chances are things are not as bad as they sound.42
M5好吧，医生。那我接下来有什么需要注意的呢？剧烈运动肯定是不能参加 了吧？我该怎样预防伤口感染呢？Alright, doctor. What things do I need to pay attention to from now on? I can’t do any strenuous activities, right? What should I do to prevent the wound from infection?
E6Try to rest until you see Dr Merphy. I will give you some disinfectant to wash the wound. Try to keep it clean and dry, so it won’t get infected.32
M6好的。 医生， 多谢您的关照。 请你给我开一封转接信。 我明天就去 Merphy 医生那儿。Ok. Thank you for looking after me. Can you please write me a referral. I’ll go see Dr. Merphy tomorrow.

entry.title: 父亲施暴
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Mr. James, a school principal, and Mr. Luo, a father, who has been called in for an interview regarding hitting his son.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Thanks for coming in, Mr. Luo. I am the school principal, Steven James. Nice to meet you.17
M1您好，校长先生。您今天找我来，是为了什么事吗？是我儿子最近在学校调皮捣蛋了？Hello, Principal. What is the problem that you need to talk to me about? Has my son been acting up at school lately?
E2Well, Tom has been very well behaved recently. But his teacher noticed some bruises on his back while he was doing sports yesterday. Do you know anything about that?29
M2噢， 您是说那些淤青啊 … 那是因为他上一次考试又不及格， 我实在太生气了，就教训了他一下。Oh, you mean the bruise … that is because I disciplined him a bit. You know, he failed the exam again, and I was so angry.
E3By saying “discipline”, do you mean you, as a matter of fact, have physically punished your son and it was you who caused the bruise?25
M3你这样说，也可以。男孩子总是很顽皮的，我觉得体罚作为一种教育手段，是必要的。而且，这种瘀伤，很快就能好的。Well, you can put it that way. Boys are often quite naughty and I think physical punishment is necessary as a disciplinary measure. Besides, he will recover soon from this kind of bruises.
E4But physical violence against children is harmful, and it can cause serious injuries. I think that it is wrong to consider it a way to resolve issues with your child.30
M4怎么会呢？我小的时候做错了事、或者不听话的时候，也总是挨父亲的揍。但是您看，我现在不是好好的，也没受到任何的伤害呀？How come? When I was little, my father often hit me if I did something wrong or behaved disrespectfully. Look, I turned out to be alright now. It didn’t do me any harm.
E5I can’t agree to that, Mr. Luo. Violence only creates problems and conflicts, not the other way around. Children never deserve to be physically hit or abused. They will learn nothing positive from such action.35
M5这就是我们中国家长的教育方式。我们才不像澳大利亚的父母，让孩子们为所欲为，都把孩子给惯坏了！This is the way we Chinese parents educate our kids. We are different from Australian parents who let their kids do whatever they want. Their kids are all getting spoiled!
E6But I must ask you to stop such action since it is against Australian law. Otherwise you may be charged with child abuse and facing possible penalties and even jail sentences.30
M6真有这么严重？对不起，之前我还真不懂这些。好吧，那我以后不打他了。Is it really that serious? Sorry, I didn’t know that. Alright, I won’t hit him anymore.

entry.title: 预定团游
entry.content:
A Mandarin speaking man is making a phone call to a travel company named NT Adventures. He has an enquiry about making a tour booking to Kakadu National Park for a group of Chinese tourists.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, NT Adventures. How may I help you?9
M1你好，我想了解一下怎么样能够去卡卡杜国家公园。我是想给一群中国游客 来预定。Hi there. I’d like to make an enquiry about booking a tour to Kakadu National Park for a group of Chinese tourists.
E2Certainly. Could you start by telling me how many people are in your group and how long you’d like to tour the park? NT Adventures has a number of different tour packages available. I’ll try my best to find one most suitable for you.44
M2嗯，这个团总共有六个人，他们将会于 9 月 30 号从澳门飞去达尔文，然后 10 月 3 号的早上去飞回去。所以我们想要的是一个两日游。我去年的时候其 实用过你们公司而且觉得你们的工作做的相当好。It’s a six-person group. They’re flying from Macau to Darwin on the 30 thof September and are flying back on the 3 rdof October in the morning. So we’re after a 2-day tour. I actually used your company last year and thought you did a fantastic job.
E3Well, that’s good to hear. In that case, there is one particular tour that I think would be suitable for you. It’s a two-day 4WD tour that takes you to see some spectacular waterfalls and local wildlife, including crocs of course.
M3听起来很棒，因为我们时间紧，可是又想游览到卡卡杜国家公园最美的景 点。那住宿怎么安排呢？这些客人都是来自大公司的企业家，住惯了五星级 酒店。这次他们想尝试些新鲜的体验。It sounds great as we are short on time and want to see the best of Kakadu National Park. How about accommodation then? The guests are all entrepreneurs from big companies. They have gotten used to five-star hotels. But this time they want to try something different.
E4Well, there are two options. One is to camp under the stars. The other is to stay in a cabin. Which do you think your group would prefer?28
M4我想一个小木屋对我们这个团应该更合适一些。那么，两日游总共有的花费 要多少呢？I think a cabin would be more suitable for this group. How much will the two-day tour cost in total then?
E5Well, in total it comes to $810 for twin share and $900 for single room. That price includes entrance to the national park, as well as GST. I’ve added a 10 per cent discount considering you are a repeat customer.
M5很好。我们这个团说他们想看一下本地的这个传统土著居民的艺术和文化。 他们还说他们想爬爬山，但是我觉得我们可能没有时间去爬山。您怎么看 呢？Great. They told me they’d like to check out some of the traditional Aboriginal art and culture. They also said they’d like to do some hiking but I don’t think we’ll have time for that. What do you think?
E6Well, during the tour we actually take you to a local Aboriginal community to experience traditional Aboriginal culture, and see ancient rock paintings. There is some hiking during the tour on the second day, but nothing too strenuous.38
M6好，听起来不错。我们这个团里还有这个养鸟的爱好者，他们说如果可能的 话想去看看本地的鸟。OK, that sounds fine. There are a couple of bird enthusiasts in the group. They said they’d like to do some bird watching if possible.
E7We may be able to arrange something on the second day of the tour. I’d have to get back to you on that one.24
M7谢谢。我 15 分钟之后就给你打个电话过来，商量更多的预定信息。Thanks. I’ll call you back in 15 minutes, to go through more details about the group booking.

entry.title: RO 段落排序
entry.content:
001Indian IT 真题
1Innovation in India is as much due to entrepreneurialism as it is to IT skills, says Arun Maria, chairman of Boston Consulting Group in India.
2Indian businessmen have used IT to create new business models that enable them to provide services in a more cost-effective way.
3This is not something that necessarily requires expensive technical research.
4He suggests the country’s computer services industry can simply outsource research to foreign universities if the capability is not available locally.
5“This way, I will have access to the best scientists in the world without having to produce them myself” says Mr. Maria.

002Foreign aid 真题
1But beginning in the 1990s, foreign aid had begun to slowly improve.
2Scrutiny by the news media shamed many developed countries into curbing their bad practices.
3Today, the projects of organizations like the World Bank are meticulously inspected by watchdog groups.
4Although the system is far from perfect, it is certainly more transparent than it was when foreign aid routinely helped ruthless dictators stay in power.

003Jet Stream 真题
1Jet stream, narrow, swift currents or tubes of air found at heights ranging from 7 to 8 mi (11.3–12.9 km) above the surface of the earth.
2They are caused by great temperature differences between adjacent air masses. There are four major jet streams.
3Instead of moving along a straight line, the jet stream flows in a wavelike fashion; the waves propagate eastward (in the Northern Hemisphere) at speeds considerably slower than the wind speed itself.
4Since the progress of an airplane is aided or impeded depending on whether tail winds or head winds are encountered, in the Northern Hemisphere the jet stream is sought by eastbound aircraft, in order to

004Map 真题
1For as long as I can remember, there has been a map in the ticket hall of Piccadilly Circus tube station supposedly showing night and day across the time zones of the world.
2This is somewhat surprising given the London Underground’s historic difficultly in grasping the concept of punctuality.
3But this map has always fascinated me, and still does, even though it now seems very primitive.
4This is because it chops the world up equally by longitude, without regard the reality of either political divisions or the changing seasons.

005Chimpanzees 真题
1A simple way to disprove this hypothesis (the Innateness Hypothesis) is to demonstrate that other species have the capacity to speak but for some reason simply have not developed speech.
2A logical candidate for such a species is the chimpanzee, which shares 98.4% of the human genetic code.
3Chimpanzees cannot speak because, unlike homo sapiens, their vocal cords are located higher in their throats and cannot be controlled as delicately as human vocal cords.
4It does not follow from their lack of speech, however, that chimpanzees are incapable of language.
5Perhaps they can acquire grammar like humans if they could only express it some other way.

006Immigration Effect 真题
1In the early years of the twenty-first century the impact of immigrants on the welfare state and, specifically, the capacity of the welfare state to absorb large numbers of immigrants has become a staple of discussion among policy makers and politicians.
2It is also a recurrent theme in the press, from the highbrow pages of Prospect to the populism of the Daily Mail.
3Inevitably, these discussions focus on present-day dilemmas.
4But the issues themselves are not new and have historical roots that go much deeper than have been acknowledged

007Mother of Storm 真题
1Unlike Barnes’ previous books, Mother of Storms has a fairly large cast of viewpoint characters.
2This usually irritates me, but I didn’t mind it here, and their interactions are well-handled and informative, although occasionally in moving them about the author’s manipulations are a bit blatant.
3They’re not all necessarily good guys, either, although with the hurricanes wreaking wholesale destruction upon the world’s coastal areas, ethical categories tend to become irrelevant.
4But even the Evil American Corporate Magnate is a pretty likable guy.

008Charles Lindbergh 真题
1After finishing first in his pilot training class, Lindbergh took his first job as the chief pilot of an airmail route operated by Robertson Aircraft Co. of Lambert Field in St. Louis, Missouri.
2He flew the mail in a de Havilland DH-4 biplane to Springfield, Illinois, Peoria and Chicago.
3During his tenure on the mail route, he was renowned for delivering the mail under any circumstances.
4After a crash, he even salvaged bags of mail from his burning aircraft and immediately phoned Alexander Varney, Peoria’s airport manager, to advise him to send a truck.

009SEPAHUA 真题
1SEPAHUA, a ramshackle town on the edge of Peru’s Amazon jungle, nestles in a pocket on the map where a river of the same name flows into the Urubamba.
2That pocket denotes a tiny patch of legally loggable land sandwiched between four natural reserves, all rich in mahogany and accessible from the town. “Boundaries are on maps,” says a local logger, “maps
3In 2001 the government, egged on by WWF, a green group, tried to regulate logging in the relatively small part of the Peruvian Amazon where this is allowed.
4It abolished the previous system of annual contracts.
5Instead, it auctioned 40-year concessions to areas ruled off on a map, with the right to log 5% of the area each year. The aim was to encourage strict management plans and sustainable extraction.

011International Date Line 真题
1International Date Line, imaginary line on the earth’s surface, generally following the 180° meridian of longitude, where, by international agreement, travelers change dates.
2The date line is necessary to avoid a confusion that would otherwise result.
3For example, if an airplane were to travel westward with the sun, 24 hr would elapse as it circled the globe, but it would still be the same day for those in the airplane while it would be one day latefor
4The same problem would arise if two travelers journeyed in opposite directions to a point on the opposite side of the earth, 180° of longitude distant.
5The apparent paradox is resolved by requiring that the traveler crossing the date line change his date, thus bringing the travelers into agreement when they meet.

012Aviation 真题
1During the 1920s and 1930s great progress was made in the field of aviation, including the first transatlantic flight of Alcock and Brown in 1919, Charles Lindbergh’s solo transatlantic flight in 1927, and Charles Kingsford Smith’s transpacific flight the following year.
2One of the most successful designs of this period was the Douglas DC-3, which became the first airliner to be profitable carrying passengers
3By the beginning of World War II, many towns and cities had built airports, and there were numerous qualified pilots available.
4The war brought many innovations to aviation, including the first jet aircraft and the first liquid-fueled rockets.

013Aviation after World War II 真题
1After World War II, especially in North America, there was a boom in general aviation, both private and commercial, as thousands of pilots were released from military service and many inexpensive war-surplus transport and training aircraft became available.
2Manufacturers such as Cessna, Piper, and Beechcraft expanded production to provide light aircraft for the new middle-class market.
3By the 1950s, the development of civil jets grew, beginning with the de Havilland Comet, though the first widely used passenger jet was the Boeing 707, because it was much more economical than other
4At the same time, turboprop propulsion began to appear for smaller commuter planes, making it possible to serve small-volume routes in a much wider range of weather conditions.

014Green tea 真题
1In May 2006, researchers at Yale University weighed in on green tea’s health benefits with a review article that examined more than 100 studies on the subject.
2They pointed to the “Asian paradox,” lower rates of heart disease and cancer in Asia despite high rates of smoking.
3They theorized that the 1.2 liters of green tea consumed by many Asians each day, provides high levels of polyphenols and other antioxidants.
4These compounds may work in several ways to improve cardiovascular health.
5Specifically, green tea may prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol (the “bad” type), which in turn can reduce the buildup of plaque in arteries, the researchers wrote.

015Ocean floors 真题
1The topography of the ocean floors is none too well known, since in great areas the available soundings are hundreds or even thousands of miles apart.
2However, the floor of the Atlantic is becoming fairly well known as a result of special surveys since 1920.
3A broad, well-defined ridge-the Mid-Atlantic ridge-runs north and south between Africa and the two Americas. Numerous other major irregularities diversify the Atlantic floor.
4Closely spaced soundings show that many parts of the oceanic floors are rugged as mountainous regions of the continents.
5Use of the recently perfected method of echo sounding is rapidly enlarging our knowledge of submarine topography.
6During World War II great strides were made in mapping submarine surfaces, particularly in many parts of the vast pacific basin.”

016Volkswagen 真题
1Despite posting healthy profits, Volkswagen shares trade at a discount to peers due to bad reputation among investors, high capital expenditure and its close links to the state of Lower Saxony, which effectively has a blocking minority.
2A disastrous capital hike, an expensive foray into truck business and uncertainty about the reason for a share buyback has in recent years left investors bewildered.
3“The main problem with Volkswagen is the past. Many investors have been disappointed and frightened away,” said one Paris-based fund managers.
4Volkswagen shares trade at about nine times the 2002 estimated, compared to BMW’s 19 and are the second cheapest in the sector.

017Marquez 真题
1Marquez arrived in October 1577 at the abandoned town of Sahta Elena with two ships carrying pre-fabricated posts and heavy planking
2He erected fort San Marcos in six days in defense against a Native American attack such as the one that forced the abandonment of the town a year earlier.
3The town had flourished, nearing 400 residents, since its establishment more than a decade earlier in 1566 by Pedro Menendez de Aviles who had founded La Florida and St. Augustine the year before.
4In 1571 it became the capital of Spanish Florida.

018Earth-like planets 真题
1A team of scientists has discovered two Earth-like planets in the habitable orbit of a Sun-like star.
2Using observations gathered by NASA’s Kepler Mission, the team found five planets orbiting a Sun-like star called Kepler-62.
3Four of these planets are so-called super-Earths, larger than our own planet, but smaller than even the smallest ice giant planet in our Solar System.
4These new super-Earths have radii of 1.3, 1.4, 1.6, and 1.9 times that of Earth.
5In addition, one of the five was a roughly Mars-sized planet, half the size of Earth.

019Carbon Detox 真题
1In his fascinating book Carbon Detox, George Marshall argues that people are not persuaded by information.
2Our views are formed by the views of the people with whom we mix.
3Of the narratives that might penetrate these circles, we are more likely to listen to those that offer us some reward.
4He proposes that instead of arguing for sacrifice, environmentalists should show where the rewards might lie.
5We should emphasize the old-fashioned virtues of uniting in the face of a crisis, of resourcefulness and community action.

020Battle with enemy 真题
1With regard to defence, the purpose of the military is to defend the nation and be prepared to do battles with its enemy.
2How do you do battle with your enemy?
3The idea is to destroy the enemy’s productive capacity, and depending upon the economic foundation, that the productive capacity is different in each case.
4So in the agrarian era, if you need to destroy the enemy’s productive capacity, what you want to do is bum his fields, or if you’re really vicious, salt them.
5But in the industrial era destroying the enemy’s productive capacity means bombing the factories which are located in the cities.
6Now in the information era, destroying the enemy’s productive capacity means destroying the information infrastructure.

021An underperforming company 非原文
1Take an underperforming company
2Add some generous helping of debt, a few spoonful of management incentives and trim all the fat.
3Leave to cook for five years and you have a feast of profits.
4That has been the recipe for private-equity groups during the past 200 years.

022Environmental revolution 真题
1The environmental revolution has been almost three decades in the making, and it has changed forever how companies do business.
2In the 1960s and 1970s, corporations were in a state of denial regarding their impact on the environment.
3Then a series of highly visible ecological problems created a groundswell of support for strict government regulation
4In the United States, Lake Erie was dead. In Europe, the Rhine was on fire. In Japan, people were dying of mercury poisoning.
5Today many companies have accepted their responsibility to do no harm to the environment.

023German invasion 真题
1German invasion of Poland officially triggered the Second World War.
2In the beginning, Britain and France were hopeful that Poland should be able to defend her borders.
3But Polish forces could not defend a long border.
4They lacked compact defence lines and additionally their supply lines were also poorly protected.
5Meanwhile, the world had woken up to the potential of atomic energy and countries were conducting testes to exploit the same.

024Top executives 真题
1The top executives of the large, mature, publicly held companies hold the conventional view when they stop to think of the equity owners’ welfare.
