095Games affect brains 真题
1Palaus and his colleagues wanted to see if any trends had emerged from the research to date concerning how video games affect the structure and activity of our brains.
2They collected the results from 116 scientific studies, 22 of which looked at structural changes in the brain and 100 of which looked at changes in brain functionality and/or behavior.
3The studies show that playing video games can change how our brains perform, and even their structure.
4For example, playing video games affects our attention, and some studies found that gamers show improvements in several types of attention, such as sustained attention or selective attention.

096Copernicus’s Heliocentric Theory 真题
1Copernicus probably hit upon his main idea sometime between 1508 and 1514.
2For years, however, he delayed publication of his controversial work, which contradicted all the authorities of the time.
3The historic book that contains the final version of his theory, De Revolutionibus Orbium Coelestium Libri VI (“Six Books Concerning the Revolutions of the Heavenly Orbs”), did not appear in print until
4According to legend, Copernicus received a copy as he was dying, on May 24, 1543.
5The book opened the way to a truly scientific approach to astronomy. It had a profound influence on later thinkers of the scientific revolution, including such major figures as Galileo, Johannes Keplerand

036Ancestors lit fire 非原文
1Many years ago, humans had been eating raw meat only.
2(to be confirmed)
3Then they learned how to light a fire.
4Since then humans started eating cooked meat.
5And they also learned to cook many other food.

068Martin Luther King 非原文
1Rose Parks has a great impact on the civil rights movements.
2She refused to give her bus seat to a white man.
3The bus driver arrested her.
4Her arrested was … by Martin Luther King.
5King then … a boycott on the bus system.

097Music and Language 非原文
1Language can convey message
2Especially written language
3Music was conveyed orally only, until the 11th century when physical instruments were invented to perform music.
4It was hard to teach music.
5But now it’s easy.

098Fibers for clothing 非原文
1Fibers suitable for clothing have been made for the first time from the wheat protein gluten.
2The fibers are as strong and soft as wool and silk.
3However, up to 30 times cheaper.
4Narendra Reddy and Yiqi Yang, who produced the fibers at the University of Nebraska in Lincoln. He says that because they are biodegradable, they might be used in biomedical applications such as surgical

099Native English Speaker 非原文
1Anyone wanting to get to the top of international business, medicine or academia (but possibly not sport) needs to be able to speak English to a pretty high level.
2Equally, any native English speaker wanting to deal with these new high achievers needs to know how to talk without baffling them.
3Because so many English-speakers today are monoglots, they have little idea how difficult it is to master another language.
4Many think the best way to make foreigners understand is to be chatty and informal.
5This may seem friendly but, as it probably involves using colloquial expressions, it makes comprehension harder.

100Client Management System 非原文
1You may have heard about a client management system that can collaborate clients’ data.
2If we have such a system…
3Now we have this system…
4This system can benefit …
5Once you have this system, even those people who don’t understand management can use it well.

101Validity & Reliability 非原文
1Psychologists measure results in terms of validity and reliability.
2Validity is defined as …
3For example, when a survey is asking about someone’s personality, it shouldn’t ask him chemistry questions.
4Meanwhile, a survey also values reliability.

102Hip Hop Culture 非原文
1Hip Hop culture emerged as a reaction to the gang culture and violence of the South Bronx in the 1970s, and daily experiences of poverty, racism, exclusion, crime, violence, and neglect.
2It necessarily embodies and values resilience, understanding, community and social justice.
3Without these, Hip Hop culture would never have been, and it is because these values remain at its core that Hip Hop is such a powerful agent of positive social change around the world.
4Yet, the Hip Hop project is not yet free from these difficult circumstances.

103Copernicanism 非原文
1The expending influence of Copernicanism through the seventeenth century transformed not only the natural philosophic leaning of astronomers but also the store of conceptual material accessible to writers of fiction.
2During this period of scientific revolution, a new literary genre arose, namely that of the scientific cosmic voyage.
3In do doing, they discover that these once remote world are themselves earth-like in character.
4Descriptions of these planetary bodies as terrestrial in kind demonstrate the seventeenthcentury intellectual shift from the Aristotelian to the Copernican.

104EU Fish Problems 非原文
1The European Union has two big fish problems.
2One is that, partly as a result of its failure to manage them properly, its own fisheries can no longer meet European demand.
3The other is that its governments won’t confront their fishing lobbies and decommission all the surplus boats.
4The EU has tried to solve both problems by sending its fishermen to West Africa. Since 1979 it has struck agreements with the government of Senegal, granting our fleets access to its waters.
5As a result, Senegal’s marine ecosystem has started to go the same way as ours.

105New Ventures 非原文
1New Ventures is a program that helps entrepreneurs in some of the world’s most dynamic, emerging economies– Brazil, China, Colombia, India, Indonesia and Mexico.
2We have facilitated more than $203 million in investment, and worked with 250 innovative businesses whose goods and services produce clear, measurable environmental benefits, such as clean energyefficient
3Often they also address the challenges experienced by the world’s poor.
4For example, one of the companies we work with in China, called Eco-star, refurbishes copy machines from the United States and re-sells or leases them for 20 percent less than a branded photocopier.

106Glow Worms 非原文
1The Newnes railroad was closed in 1932 after 25 years of shipping oil shale.
2The rails were pulled out of the 600-meter tunnel, which had been bored through the sandstone in the Wollemi National Park, and the tunnel was left to its own devices.
3For Newnes, that meant becoming home to thousands and thousands of glow worms.
4The glow worm is a catch-all name for the bioluminescent larvae of various species, in this case, the Arachnocampa richardsae, a type of fungus gnat.
5Found in massive numbers in caves, the fungus gnat larvae cling to the rocky walls of the abandoned tunnel and hunt with long, glowing strings of sticky mucus.

107解谜实验 非原文
1When I was a young scholar, I do a … research that change my perception…
2I invited one student from their school each time, set them comfort and then give them a puzzle.
3One is very easily, another is very hard.
4The students do the question with confuse and….
5I observe their behavior and record their strategies

108Festival in the Desert 非原文
1The “Festival in The Desert” is a celebration of the musical heritage of the Touareg, a fiercely independent nomadic people.
2It is held annually near Essakane, an oasis some 40 miles north-west of Timbuktu, the ancient city on the Niger River.
3Reaching it tests endurance, with miles of impermanent sand tracks to negotiate.
4The reward of navigating this rough terrain comes in the form of a three-day feast of music and dance.

109International Economics 非原文
1International Economics: Theory and Policy is a proven approach in which each half of the book leads with an intuitive introduction to theory and follows with self-contained chapters to cover key policy applications.
2The Eighth Edition integrates the latest research, data, and policy in hot topics such as outsourcing, economic geography, trade and environment, financial derivatives, the subprime crisis, and China’s exchange rate policies.
3New for the Eighth Edition, all end-of-chapter problems are integrated into MyEconLab, the online assessment and tutorial system that accompanies the text.
4Students get instant, targeted feedback, and instructors can encourage practice without needing to grade work by hand.

110Green Tea Health Benefits 非原文
1In May 2006, researchers at Yale University School of Medicine weighed in on the issue with a review article that looked at more than 100 studies on the health benefits of green tea.
2They pointed to what they called an “Asian paradox,” which refers to lower rates of heart disease and cancer in Asia despite high rates of cigarette smoking.
3They theorized that the 1.2 liters of green tea that is consumed by many Asians each day provides high levels of polyphenols and other antioxidants.
4These compounds may work in several ways to improve cardiovascular health.
5Specifically, green tea may prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol (the “bad” type), which, in turn, can reduce the buildup of plaque in arteries, the researchers wrote.

111Musical Notion 非原文
1Over the years many human endeavours have had the benefit of language.
2In particular a written language can convey a lot of information about past events, places, people and things.
3But it is difficult to describe music in words, and even more difficult to specify a tune.
4It was the development of a standard musical notation in the 11th century that allowed music to be documented in a physical form.
5Now music could be communicated efficiently, and succeeding generations would know something about the music of their ancestors.

112Taking Lecture Notes 非原文
1Your main job in taking lecture notes is to be a good listener.
2To be a good listener, you must learn to focus and concentrate on the main points of the lecture.
3Get them down, and then later reorganize them in your own words.
4Once you have done this, you have set the stage for successful reviewing and revising.

113Australia’s native plants and animals 非原文
1Australia’s native plants and animals adapted to life on an isolated continent over millions of years.
2Since European settlement they have had to compete with a range of introduced animals for habitat, food and shelter.
3Some have also had to face new predators.
4These new pressures have also caused a major impact on our country’s soil and waterways and on its native plants and animals.

114Internship 非原文
1During the school year, we had the benefit of being both unaccountable and omnipotent.
2Insulated from the consequences of such decisions, and privy to all critical information about the case, we were able to solve complex business problems with relative ease.
3We knew that once we began our internships, this would no longer be the case.
4The information would be more nebulous and the outcomes of our decisions would be unpredictable.
5So in approaching this impending summer period, what lingered in the back of our minds was a collectively felt, unspeakable thought: “Were we really up to the challenge?”

115Pidgins 非原文
1In some areas, the standard chosen may be a variety that originally had no native speakers in the country.
2For example, in Papua New Guinea, a lot of official business is conducted in Tok Pisin.
3This language is now used by over a million people, but it began many years earlier as a kind of ‘contact’ language called a pidgin.
4A pidgin is a variety of a language (e.g. English) that developed for some practical purpose, such as trading, among groups of people who had a lot of contact, but who did not know each other’s languages.

116Sea Level Rise 非原文
1Sea level rise led to 36 thousand people die every year.
2This number can be raised if sea level ceaseless goes up, scientists notified.
3According to the research, if sea level rises 50 centimeters, 86 million people will die.
4If sea level rises 1 meter, 168 million people will die all around the world.

117Fruit and Vegetable Intake 非原文
1Fruit and vegetable intake is important for the prevention of future chronic disease, so it’s important to know whether intakes of teens are approaching national objectives for fruit and vegetable consumption.
2Larson and colleagues from the University of Minnesota undertook the study to examine whether or not teens in the state were increasing their intake of fruits and vegetables.
3The study gathered information about fruit and vegetable intake among 944 boys and 1,161 girls in 1999 and again in 2004.
4Teens in middle adolescence are eating fewer fruits and vegetables than in 1999, Larson and colleagues found.
5This is giving us the message that we need new and enhanced efforts to increase fruit and vegetable intake that we haven’t been doing in the past.

118Blue Halos 非原文
1Latest research has found that several common flower species have nanoscale ridges on the surface of their petals that meddle with light when viewed from certain angles.
2These nanostructures scatter light particles in the blue to ultraviolet colour spectrum, generating a subtle effect that scientists have christened the ‘blue halo’.
3By manufacturing artificial surfaces that replicated ‘blue halos’, scientists were able to test the effect on pollinators, in this case foraging bumblebees.
4They found that bees can see the blue halo, and use it as a signal to locate flowers more efficiently.

119Japanese girl leaning English 非原文
1This paper summarizes some of the major data gathered in a longitudinal, naturalistic study of a Japanese girl learning English as a second language.
2The subject in this study is Uguisu, “nightingale” in Japanese.
3Her family came to the United States for a period of two years while her father was a visiting scholar at Harvard, and they took residence in North Cambridge, a working-class neighbourhood.
4The children in that neighbourhood were her primary source of language input.
5Uguisu also attended public kindergarten of two hours every day, and later elementary school, but with no tutoring in English syntax. Most of her neighbourhood friends were in her same class at school

010Piano 非原文
1Piano keys are generally made of spruce or basswood, for lightness.
2Spruce is normally used in high-quality pianos.
3Traditionally, the black keys were made from ebony and the white keys were covered with strips of ivory, but since ivory-yielding species are now endangered and protected by treaty, plastics are now almost exclusively used.
4Also, ivory tends to chip more easily than plastic.

067Young Employees 非原文
1Employers are often reluctant to hire young people, even though there are more than 850,000 unemployed 16-to24-year-olds and UK businesses are struggling to fill one in five vacancies because of skills
shortages.2
3But a growing number of companies are setting up schemes to recruit young workers.
4They can be surprised by the results.

074United Nations Conference 非原文
1Conferences have played a key role in guiding the work of the United Nations since its very inception.
2In fact, the world body was born when delegates from 50 nations met in San Francisco in April 1945 for the United Nations Conference on International Organization.
3The recent high-profile conferences on development issues, which have continued a series that began in the 1970s, have broken new ground in many areas: by involving Presidents, Prime Ministers and other
4These events have put long-term, difficult problems like poverty and environmental degradation at the top of the global agenda.
5These problems otherwise would not have the political urgency to grab front-page headlines and command the attention of world leaders.

076Silent Students in Tutorials 非原文
1Many students sit in a tutorial week after week without saying anything.
2Why is that?
3Maybe they do not know the purpose of a tutorial.
4They think it is like a small lecture where the tutor gives them information.
5Even if students do know what a tutorial is for, there can be other reasons why they keep quiet.

077Amino Acid 非原文
1Amino acid, which is also known as Leucine, is a fundamental element in the muscle’s formation…
2Animals’ protein has a x% of the Leucine, which is higher than those in plants’ protein.
3Plants’ protein….
4However, there are also some exceptions exist.

078Diversity 非原文
1To see whether diversity matters on the land and in the sea, …… join the forces.
2These researchers will test the full resources of …
3The data range from … and a database, to kitchen’s recorders and archaeologists.
4The results of this research will be published in science.

079The Job of a Manager 非原文
1The job of a manager in the workplace is to get things done through employees.
2In order to accomplish this, the manager should be able to motivate employees.
3That is, however, easier said than done.
4Motivation practice and theory are difficult subjects, encompassing various disciplines.

085Superpower 非原文
1The ‘superpower’ has international text, which means having control over resources … political power.
2It terms of superpower, it included …
3… ‘green superpower’, …
4In addition to green energy superpower, company should meet the above global average … emissions … and …

090伦敦不夜城 非原文
1伦敦变成不夜城
2This change 的背后……
3原因是因为街上安装了电灯
4This invention ……

091Farmlands 农耕地 非原文
1小草长出来
2然后长大了变成树苗，挡住下面的草地
3树苗又变成大树，变成森林
4如果放弃的话，就会从头再来
5Farmlands always comes from forests.

entry.title: 命令行玩转格式转换
entry.content:
多格式通用 .png .jpg .jpeg & .icon, etc,.

修改图片像素大小

convert 文件名。jpg -resize 380x278 新文件名。jpg

转为黑白图片

convert 文件名。jpg -monochrome 新文件名。jpg

转为灰色图片

convert 文件名。jpg -colorspace Gray 新文件名。jpg

转为 4 张不同灰色图片

convert 文件名。png -separate 新文件名。jpg

透明背景转白色背景

convert -flatten 文件名。png 新文件名。png

转 .pdf 为 .jpg

正常。jpg
sips -s format jpeg 文件名。pdf --out 新文件名。jpg

透明背景

sips -s format png 文件名。pdf --out 新文件名。png

Batch Processing 将所有 .png 转为：.jpg:

for i in *.png ; do convert "$i" "${i%.*}.jpg" ; done

OR

ls -1 *.png | xargs -n 1 bash -c 'convert "$0" "${0%.*}.jpg"'

OR

mogrify -format jpg *.png

OR 同时删除原文件

mogrify -format jpg *.png && rm *.png

OR 同时删除原文件

find . -iname '*.png' | while read i; do mogrify -format jpg "$i" && rm "$i"; echo "Converted $i to ${i%.*}.jpg"; done

OR 同时删除原文件

for i in *.png; do mogrify -format jpg "$i" && rm "$i"; echo "$i converted to ${i%.*}.jpg"; done

entry.title: 遗嘱咨询
entry.content:
Scenario: Mrs Lee goes to see her solicitor about making a will
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mrs Lee. The interpreter is here so we can understand each other now. Could you tell me how I can help you today?25
M1我今天来是想咨询立遗嘱的事情, 我的年纪大了,觉得是时候处理这个问题了。I came here today to enquire about making a will. Considering my age, I feel it’s the right time to deal with this issue.
E2As a matter of fact, no matter how old they are, everyone should have a valid will, which makes sure that their belongings are distributed according to their wishes rather than under the rigid formula that exists for those who die intestate.42
M2说得有道理。那就请您先解答我的几个疑问吧。首先，立遗嘱应该先做什 么？遗嘱都应该包括什么呢？I agree. Could you start by answering a couple of questions for me? Firstly, what should I start with when making a will, and what should be included?
E3Firstly, you should know that a will must be accepted by a court and it must also be in writing, signed and witnessed by two witnesses.// As for the other question, all your property and possessions should be included in the will. This includes everything you own: your home, car, money, insurance policies, shares, securities, jewellery and so on.59
M3我明白了。我还有一个问题，不好意思，我对于法律方面一窍不通。是您帮 我处理和遗嘱相关的所有事宜，对吗？I see. I have another question. Sorry, I have no knowledge of law at all. So you will be the one helping me deal with all the issues related to the will, right?
E4Of course, I’m more than happy to take care of it for you. Also, you need to start considering appointing an executor to handle your affairs after you die. This can be anyone you want, including myself.37
M4我可以指定我的一个孩子做遗嘱执行人么？我有两个孩子，一儿一女。但他 们也都没有学过法律专业。执行人都有哪些职责呢？Can I appoint one of my children to be the executor? I have two children, one son and a daughter. But neither of them has studied law. What are the responsibilities of an executor?
E5The executor will be the one who has to obtain probate of the will and pay any taxes, debts or expenses before distributing the balance to the beneficiaries named in your will. So it has to be an independent party, and can’t be the beneficiaries.45
M5这样的话，那我想，我最好还是找一个专业人士吧。我可以请您做我的遗嘱 执行人吗？If that’s the case, I’d better find a professional to do this. So can I ask you to be the executor of my will?
E6Certainly! I assure you that you can trust me and my professional knowledge and experience. The first thing you need to do is to give me a list of your possessions and we can start to draft a will if you like.42
M6我在 Hurstville 有一栋房子，拥有两台车。除了银行存款以外，我还有一些股 票证券方面的投资，价值大约有 50 来万吧。其他的小额财产，我需要回家好 好整理下，再告诉您。I have a house in Hurstville and two cars. Apart from bank savings, I have investments such as shares and securities, roughly valued at $500,000. As for other small value possessions, I have to sort them out at home first before I get back to you.

entry.title: 斐济换钱
entry.content:
This is a conversation between a financial advisor and Mr Wang about exchanging money into Fijian Dollars.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1G’day Mr Wang! I’m David McDonald, financial adviser of the Commonwealth Bank of Australia. How can I help you today?20
M1你好，麦当劳先生。我今天是想来咨询一下关于我去斐济旅游的问题，我是 否需要提前在澳洲兑换一些斐济币。Good morning Mr McDonald. I’m here to inquire about my trip to Fiji. Do I need to buy some Fijian dollars here in advance?
E2Certainly you could, but as the Fijian dollar is not a common currency, we need you to make an appointment. But, let me check for you and see how much cash we have on hand. So, how much do you want?41
M2我想兑换大概价值澳元 3000 元的斐济币。大概是多少钱呢？我要去那边呆 1 个月呢，而且可能去外岛度假，所以可能会花不少钱。我怕那边太偏远，没 法用信用卡，所以我还是比较想换好现金再去。I’d like to convert about AU$3000 to Fijian dollars. How much can I get? I’ll need to stay there for about a month and I may spend some time during the holiday going to outer islands as well, so it may cost quite a lot of money. I’m worried that the place is too remote to use my credit card, so I want exchange some cash to take with me.
E3Yes, you are right. If you go to some remote islands, we would suggest that you to take enough cash to last the whole time. However, if you arrive in Fiji at Nadi International Airport, there are ATM facilities and exchange booths for traveler’s cheques and Australian dollar notes. Umm …. According to our records, we have only 1000 Fijian dollars at this branch.64
M3这不够啊！我是会在楠迪机场降落，但是我要立刻转机，可能没有时间出去 找两替店了。我知道信用卡可以用，但是我听说不论走万事达还是走维萨的 通道，都会收取高达 3%的手续费。刚才你说的旅行支票是什么？Ah that won’t be enough! It is true I will land at Nadi Airport, but I’m going to transfer straight away, so I may not have time to find an exchange booth. I know I can use my credit card, but I heard that both Visa and Master card charge a transaction fee as high as 3%. You just mentioned traveller’s cheques. What is that?
E4Yes, you are right. You will be charged at least 3% of the transaction as a processing fee. And we are not sure whether they accept American Express since we don’t have any ATMs there. As I know, ANZ has some ATMs at Nadi Airport. You can enquire with them about this. In terms of traveller’s cheques, it’s like a bank cheque, which allows you to withdraw cash overseas in a safe and reliable way.75
M4那好的，我看不必要去问 ANZ 了。我可以兑换你现有的 1000 斐济元现金 么？然后其余的 2500 澳币左右我想要旅行支票。这样我可以一降落就马上 把旅行支票兑换成当地货币。Okay. I don’t think I need to ask ANZ. Can I buy the 1000 Fijian Dollars you have now? And I’d like a travellers cheque for the remaining AU$2500 so that I can exchange it for local currency right after landing.
E5Sure, I will process this for you now. So you want all 50 dollar notes or a combination of 50 and 100 dollar notes?24
M5我想要 50 一张的纸币作为零花钱，免得到了当地老拿出大钞来，别人以为 我很有钱，抢劫我。I’d like to have 50 dollar notes as pocket money, so that I don’t have to use large notes all the time. Otherwise people may think I’m very rich and want to rob me.
E6Yes. That’s a good way to avoid any risk when you are travelling overseas. Here you are, that’s your $1000 Fijian dollar cash and your traveller’s cheque. Please keep it safe and if any is stolen or lost, please report it to us at the earliest opportunity.47
M6好的，如果真的掉了我会跟你们电话报失的。谢谢。再见。Fine. If I were to lose it I would report to you. Thank you, bye.

entry.title: 性别歧视
entry.content:
Ms. Yang is speaking to a Legal Aid officer about gender discrimination and unfair dismissal.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，我想来咨询一下关于性别歧视和不公平解雇方面的问题。Hello, I am here to make an enquiry about gender discrimination and unfair dismissal.
M1Certainly. Do you feel you have been the victim of this in your work place? Would you like to tell me more about it?24
E2在被解雇前, 我在一家俱乐部做代理经理，每星期工作 20 小时。前 不久俱乐部的董事们决定将这个职位提升为全职职位。 // 可是一星 期前董事们给了我两周通告，把我辞退了， 理由是他们想找个男的 来做这份工作。我觉得这太不公平了！I was the acting manager of a club before I got dismissed. I worked there 20 hours a week. Not long ago, the club directors decided to make this role a full-time position. However, a week ago, they gave me a two- week dismissal notice, because they preferred to get a male staff member to do the job. I think it’s so unfair!
M2Based on what you’ve told me, the actions of the club directors are indeed against the law. Have you tried talking to them? If the club has a set of policies or a procedure to deal with this kind of thing, it would be advisable to try to get this problem addressed through these channels first.56
E3我已经试过和董事们沟通了，但他们不听，我也没有办法。俱乐部也没有专门应付此类事件的策略或程序 – 我是代理经理，这一点我最清楚了。I tried to talk to the directors but they wouldn’t listen to me. There’s nothing I could do about it. And the club doesn’t have any policies or procedures to deal with that kind of issue. I know that better than anyone else as a previous acting manager there.
M3What I suggest you do then is to write to the Anti-Discrimination Board, explain what happened and ask them to help you. It won’t cost you anything and you don’t need a lawyer to do it, but the complaint must be in written form, and you must sign it.49
E4可是，我的英语水平很差， 肯定看都看不懂那些表格和文件。万一 写错了什么地方，可怎么办呢？所以，您能不能替我起草一份投诉书呢？But my English is very poor. I can’t even understand those documents and forms. What if I make a mistake? Can you help me draft a complaint application?
M4You can write to the board in any language, and there are discrimination complaint forms you can obtain from their website, so you don’t have to worry about your English skills. The board staff can also help you complete the form.41
E5那太好了。那您能否现在帮我打开那个反歧视委员会的网站，然后帮我把表格打印出来呢？口译员在这里，更好可以帮我翻译。那么我投诉之后会怎样呢？That’s great. Could you now go to the website of the AntiDiscrimination Board and print out the forms for me? The interpreter here can help me translate. So what will happen after the complaint is lodged?
M5Sure. Here you are. The board will investigate your complaint, and if they find that the actions of your employer were against the law, they will try to facilitate conciliation. The settlement will be a private agreement between you and your employer, and what it entails depends on what both parties are willing to offer and accept.57
E6听起来有些复杂而且很耗时间吧？我想继续在俱乐部里做经理，您觉得这有可能吗？It sounds a bit complicated and time-consuming. I’d like to continue being a manager at the club. Do you think this would be possible?
M6It will depend on what your employers are willing to offer. They may choose to offer you an apology, financial compensation, or perhaps your old position. You will have to talk to them about it when the board organises a conciliation session.42

entry.title: 照料津贴
entry.content:
Mr Gao, a Mandarin-speaking man, has come to a local Centrelink office. He has asked to speak to a Centrelink officer about applying for carer payments.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning. Please take a seat. Before we start could I confirm your CRN (Client Reference:  Number) and your name please?18
M1我上个星期才拿到卡，号码是 524 094 104K.我的名字是 Qiang Gao.I only got the card last week. The number is 524 094 104K. My name is Qiang Gao.
E2Cheers, Mr. Gao. So, what can I help you with today?11
M2是关于我的孙子，他叫查斯特，今年 12 岁了。他有唐氏综合症和智障。因为 我要全职的照顾他所以我没办法工作。最糟糕的是，我的太太最近又丢了工 作。如果 Centrelink 可以给我们一些帮助我们会很感激的。It’s about my grandson, Chester. He is 12 years old. He has Down’s syndrome and an intellectual disability. Since I provide full-time care for him I am not able to work. The worst thing is, my wife has lost her job. We would be extremely grateful for any kind of assistance that Centrelink can give us.
E3Well you may be eligible for Carer Payment, and I see on your file that you and your grandson are both permanent residents. So, may I ask, when did you first arrive and settle in Australia?36
M3我们是 2005 年一月从中国移民过来的。那年七月，我们全家搬到墨尔本边上 的一个小镇上，然后就一直住到现在。We migrated here from China in January 2005. Then in July the same year, my family and I moved to a small town near Melbourne. We have been living there since then.
E4The reason I asked about when you came to Australia is because to qualify for a Carer Payment both you and the person you are looking after must be Australian residents and they must have been living in the country for at least two years. Now, I can see this would not be a problem in your case.58
M4我想这份补助真的会帮到我们很大的忙。除了查斯特的医药费以外，我们还有 汽油费，每天开两个小时的车去特殊学校。您觉得我们每个月可以拿多少钱 呢？I think this payment would help us a lot. On top of the cost of his medical expenses, there are also petrol expenses. We have to drive two hours every day to get to a special school. So how much money do you think we can get per month?
E5Well I’m afraid I won’t be able to give you an exact figure today. The Carer Payment is income and assets tested, so individual payments do vary. I would advise you to register an ‘intent to claim’ form for the Carer Payment as soon as you can.47
M5那还有别的什么补助，我有可能符合资格领取吗？请你帮我查一下好吗？Are there other kinds of payments that we may be eligible to receive? Could you find out for me?
E6Now as part of the payment you will also receive a Pensioner Concession Card and Pharmaceutical Allowance. You may also be able to get a few other payments such as Rent Assistance and Utilities Allowance. And if you are eligible you will be notified automatically. You won’t need to apply for them yourself.53
M6嗯，那个太好啦。我这就会去填表。谢谢！OK. I think I’ll go fill in the form right away. Thank you!

entry.title: 疑似失眠
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Dr. Philip, a GP, and Mrs. Li, about her sleeping problems.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon Mrs. Li. So what has brought you here today?11
M1下午好，Philip 医生。我最近感觉不太好，因为我晚上总是睡不好觉， 所以白天就无精打采的，还很易怒。Good afternoon, Doctor Philip. I’ve been feeling very well lately. I am listless and  moody during the day because I have been having sleeping problems.
E2I see. How long exactly have you been suffering from disturbed sleep? Also, could you tell me what kind of sleeping problems you have specifically?25
M2大概有三个多星期了， 就是自从我退休之后。 晚上的时候， 我觉得焦躁不安，总要在床上翻来覆去几个小时，很难入睡。It has been over three weeks probably, ever since I retired. I feel restless through the night and I often toss and turn for hours but just can’t fall asleep.
E3Sleeping problems are particularly common in people over 65. They can affect people in different ways. For example, they may have trouble falling asleep, they may wake frequently or have poor sleep quality.33
M3是呀，是呀。您说的这些症状，我都有。我该怎么办呢？医生，您说我是不是得了失眠症了呢？Yes, exactly. You see, I have all the symptoms that you just mentioned. What shoul I do? Doctor, do you think I have insomnia?
E4It depends. The need for sleep varies from person to person. It also varies depending on the level of activity. In your case, I believe the main cause is just a change of lifestyle after retirement.36
M4我的确是很难适应现在的退休生活。可是，这个问题每天都越发严重了。那您能给我开点安眠药吗？我太久都没有好好得睡上一觉了。I do have problems adapting to retirement. But this condition is getting worse day  by day. Could you prescribe some sleeping pills for me? I haven’t had a proper night’s sleep for a long time.
E5Actually, at this stage, doing more exercise and being more active during the day could be a better solution for you. Also, worrying about loss of sleep can in turn lead to having even worse sleep.36
M5好吧，既然您说不是大问题，那我就放心了。那除了您刚说的，在睡前习惯方面，我有什么需要注意的吗？Alright, I’m very much relieved since you said it’s not a big deal. Apart from what you just mentioned, is there anything else I should pay attention to in terms of pre-sleep routine?
E6Well, yes. Doing things such as spending at least half an hour winding down, reading a paper or listening to some music can be quite helpful.26
M6那好吧。我会照您的话去做的。谢谢。Alright. I will follow your advice. Thank you.

entry.title: 探亲签证
entry.content:
Mrs. Lu is being interviewed by a member of the Migration Review Tribunal (MRT) regarding a rejected visa application.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1How are you, Mrs Lu? Good to see you today. Please have a seat.14
M1謝謝，您一定得幫我。 我帮我的弟弟申请了短期停留探访签证，但 是申请遭到了拒绝。我们现在很绝望。Thank you. Please help me. I helped my brother to apply for a short stay visitor’s visa. But our application was rejected and we’re really desperate.
E2Don’t be upset. Our job in the MRT is to review unfavourable decisions made by DIAC. However, in making its decisions, it must have regard to the relevant laws, and to government policy. Does that make sense?37
M2恩，我明白。我想我来对地方了。我认为移民局的拒签決定太不合 理了。我肯定，他们错了。Yes, I understand. I think I have come to the right place. I think DIAC’s decision to reject the visa is so unfair, and I am sure they have made a mistake.
E3I can’t say anything about that until I get the details. Now, this matter relates to the refusal to grant a Short Stay visitor’s Visa. I know you wanted to sponsor your brother to come to Australia for a three-month  period. Why was that?44
M3是這樣，我母親跟我們一起住在澳洲，她一直有糖尿病，去年開始 又有了一系列的併發症，半年前還發作過一次心臟病，身體越來越 差了。她很想再見一次我的弟弟，所以我弟弟需要一个签证。Well… my mother is living with us in Australia. She has diabetes and she’s started to suffer a series of complications since last year. Her health has been getting worse since her heart attack 6 months ago. But she really wants to see my brother again, so my brother needs a visa.
E4According to the decision, they were not satisfied that your brother would leave Australia when his visa expired. The reason is firstly, that your brother has never travelled overseas before; and that he had limited incentives to return home. Why do you think the decision is incorrect?47
M4我弟弟在中國經營著一家書店， 有自己 的 房產 ， 有妻子和一個孩 子。我們已經在申請里提交了銀行證明，顯示我弟弟有超過 70 萬人 民幣的银行存款。My brother owns a bookshop and has his own property in China. He has a wife and a child. We did provide the bank statement with our application, showing that his bank savings are over 700 thousand RMB.
E5That makes sense. Looking through the file, I can see that you provided very detailed documentation with your brother’s application. And now you have provided me with a letter that states you will accept the imposition of a security bond for your brother, if necessary.45
M5是的，先生。我們是 好公民，並沒有任何不良行為。澳洲移民局爲 什麽這麼冷酷？我只是想让我的弟弟来这里探望生病的母亲而已。Yes, Sir. We are good citizens here with no record of bad behaviour. Why is DIAC so cold-hearted? We just want to bring my brother here to visit my mother as she is sick.
E6You are absolutely right. It is quite possible that the MRT will set aside the decision of DIAC.18

entry.title: 新洲工作
entry.content:
Mr ZHONG is a newly arrived migrant, he has recently found a new job opportunity. Before accepting the new job, he goes to NSW Office of Industrial Relations to seek further information about working in NSW. Ms Jones, an IR officer meets Mr ZHONG at her office.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1我最近在找工作。有一个雇主要求我去面试，在我去之前，我想咨询您几个问题。I’ve been looking for jobs recently. One employer asked me to attend an interview. But before I go, I would like to ask you a few questions.
M1Congratulations！I will try to provide you with all the information that you might need. Before you start work you need to know the conditions you will be working and exactly what you will be expected to do in the role. Your employer should give you this information in a letter of offer.54
E2这封聘用书都具体都应该包含那些内容呢？我知道的有，我的工资，工作时间，我的职责等等。 但是我希望聘用书里面能包括公司的政策，因为它能反映用人单位的雇佣理念。我还希聘用书能列出一些对我有益处的职工培训项目。What exactly should be included in the letter of offer? I only know there should be my salary, working hours and duties. But I hope it also includes company’s policies which normally reflect their employment philosophies, as well as some useful training programs that I may find beneficial.
M2You are right. The letter of offer should include the employer’s name and contact details, your duties, salary, working hours, your employment conditions and whether you are to be employed as a casual, full-time or part-time employee.37
E3原来有这么多的细节信息呀。我本来想着特别简单， 就是我上班，雇主给钱。却不知道还有这么多不同的工作形式。Oh so there is actually that much detailed information. I thought it would be just as simple as I work and get paid. But I didn’t know there are so many different types of jobs.
M3Please remember to ask your employer what employment you will be working under and whether you are covered by a state or federal award.24
E4我明白了，每个细节都必须搞清楚。如果雇主给我书面聘用书，我应该收到后立刻签署相关的文件吗？I see. I need to go through every detail. If the employer sends me an offer in writing, should I sign those documents as soon as they are provided?
M4As part of the process of accepting a new job, you may be asked to sign a document agreeing to particular working conditions. This could be called a contract or an agreement. If this happens, make sure you read it very carefully first. Don’t feel pressured to sign straight away, especially if it doesn’t suit you.56
E5如果雇主没有给我书面的聘用书，我应该怎么办？我应该和他要吗？有的雇主嫌麻烦，不愿意出书面的聘用书。What should I do if the employer is reluctant to present me with a written offer Should I ask for it? Some employers simply can’t be bothered to issue an offer letter.
M5A verbal agreement is also a legal contract. One of the best things you can do is to note down everything the employer has told you about the job and your working conditions. Even though you have written it yourself, this can be used as evidence if there is any future dispute about your pay or conditions.57
E6我听说这边主要分两种工人，一种是合同制工人，一种是雇佣制工人。 而且雇员有全职工作， 半职工作， 还有临时工？如果雇主要求我做免费试工，我该怎么办？I heard that there are two types of workers, under which I can either work as a contractor or employee and that I can work full time, part time or casually. What if they ask me to do an unpaid trial?
M6Yes, you are right. It is important for all workers to know whether they are employees or contractors. Workers can be employed on a full-time, part-time or casual basis. It is against the law for prospective employers to make you do an unpaid work trial, even if it is just for a few hours.54

entry.title: 煤气泄漏
entry.content:
his is a dialogue between a woman discussing some problems regarding her living arrangements with a case manager at the Department of Family and Community Services.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon. How is everything going? You moved into the unit in Murray St almost three months ago now. Have you settled in all right?25
M1是这样的，其实还没有。有些问题，我得跟你反映一下。Well, no actually, there are a few problems I need to talk to you about.
E2Oh, really? I’m surprised to hear that. You were so pleased when you were able to get that unit, because it’s in such a convenient location for you. What’s the matter?31
M2嗯，还是存在不少问题的。但主要让我担心的是，厨房里好像总是弥漫着一 股让人头晕的煤气味。我恐怕是有煤气泄漏。Well, there are a few things…but my main concern is that there seems to always be a faint smell of gas in the kitchen, and I’m worried that there might be a leak.
E3Oh! Well yes, if that’s the case, you’re quite right, it does need to be fixed immediately. I’ll ring the gas company right now. Will you be home for the rest of the afternoon?34
M3这里结束之后，三点钟我就得到学校去接我的儿子放学。然后我们就直接回 家了。一般三点半之前就能到家。Well, after I leave here I have to pick up my son from school at 3 o’clock, but then we’ll be going straight home. We usually get home before 3.30.
E4Good. When it’s a suspected leak, they usually get someone to come and investigate it within a couple of hours. But you’ll need to be there to let them in.30
M4噢，对啊，我知道。那没有关系。如果能解决这个问题，我就放心多了。现 在我可不可以再反应一下其他的问题？Oh yes, I understand. That will be fine. It will be a great relief to have that fixed. Is it all right if I mention the other problems now?
E5Of course, go ahead.4
M5嗯，是关于 6 号房的那家人，就住在我们楼上。他们太吵了。夜里，我试过 让他们把电视机的声音关小一点，但从来都没有用。我们根本睡不了觉！Well, it’s about the people in unit 6, directly above us. They’re very noisy. I tried asking them to turn the TV down at night, but it hasn’t helped. We can’t get any sleep!
E6Ah, yes, some of the other tenants have complained about them too. I’ll send them a warning letter. If they don’t keep the noise down, let us know and we’ll follow it up.33
M6那就麻烦你了，谢谢。Please do. Thank you!

entry.title: 为女贷款
entry.content:
Mrs. Shan is speaking to a Legal Aid officer about helping her daughter financially.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mrs. Shan. Sorry to have kept you waiting. The receptionist wasn’t very specific when she mentioned what you’re here for today. Am I correct in saying that it has something to do with taking out a loan?39
M1是的，是这样的。我的女儿最近和女婿在 Burwood 买了一套房子，借了不 少钱。// 前不久她和我打电话时说她失业了， 女婿一个人打工还房贷还 要养家很辛苦。我想用我在 Kensington 的房子抵押贷款，来帮他们一把。Yes, you’re right. My daughter and her husband bought a property in Burwood recently. They borrowed a lot of money for that.// She called me not long ago, saying she has just lost her job, so her husband is now struggling to make the loan repayments and support the family all by himself. In order to help them out, I want to use my property in Kensington to get a mortgage.
E2I understand that it’s very difficult to watch your daughter struggle financially, but you also need to protect your own interests and make sure that whatever is asked of you is realistic. With that in mind, how would you describe your own financial situation?44
M2我的经济状况？一般吧。退休以后我就靠养老金生活，省点用基本能够凑 合。不过 Kensington 的房子是我自己的，房贷也已经还清。我女儿说如果 现在拿去抵押贷款的话可以借到一笔不小的数目。My financial situation? It’s pretty average. I’ve been living off the pension since retiring. I have no problem getting by if I save a bit of money. But the property in Kensington is mine and the mortgage has been paid off. My daughter said that if I use it to get a mortgage I would be able to borrow a lot of money.
E3I think I am getting to understand the whole story. Are you saying that, your daughter was the one who suggested the loan, is that correct?26
M3是的，虽然她和她丈夫的个人信誉评价都很好，但由于房贷的关系，各大 银行都不愿意再借钱给他们，所以不得不求我帮忙。Yes. She and her husband both have very good creditworthiness, but no bank wants to lend them money due to their current mortgage. So they had to turn to me for help.
E4Whilst I can empathise with your situation, I would still like to highlight that you will be the one who bears most of the risk.25
M4我明白，但是最近房价涨得这么厉害，如果银行因为他们还不起房贷而回 收他们的房子，以后再要买房就更困难了。I know, but the housing prices are soaring. It would be difficult for them to buy another property in the future, if the bank took back their house away just because they couldn’t afford the loan.
E5But if you take out a loan to help your daughter, you will be relying on her to make the repayments, as your pension is only enough for you to get by. If she can’t pay, you might lose your house.41
M5可是她毕竟是我唯一的女儿， 我不帮她谁帮她呢？我这次来也是她的提 议，因为我们都想不出更好的办法。您对此有什么建议吗？或许我应该把 房子的产权转一部分给她？But she is my only daughter. If I don’t help her, who else will? Coming to see you was also her suggestion, as neither of us could come up with a better idea. What’s your advice? Should I transfer part of my property title to her?
E6That would still carry the same risk as if you take out the loan yourself. You can consider taking out a reverse mortgage, where you only pay back the loan when you die or sell your home. But that might leave too little equity in the property to pay for your health care or to leave as an inheritance to your daughter.62
M6反向抵押贷款？我从没听说过。麻烦您给我一些关于这种贷款的信息，我 得回家和我女儿讨论一下。A reverse mortgage? I have never heard of it. Could you please give me more information about this kind of loan? I’d like to discuss it with my daughter when I get home.

entry.title: 花粉病症
entry.content:
Mr. Lee (L), a Mandarin speaking patient, is speaking to his GP (G) about his flu-like symptoms.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1醫生，我覺得好难受呀。Hello doctor. I am not feeling very well.
M1Good morning Mr. Lee. You actually don’t look very good. I can see that you have blood-shot eyes and your face is swelling.23
E2我近來一直打喷嚏、流鼻涕。起初我以为是流感，所以就去买了些治流感的药片。但吃了药之后却并没有好转。Recently, I’ve been sneezing a lot and have had a runny nose. I initially thought that it was the flu, so I went to buy some flu tablets. But I didn’t get any better after taking the medication.
M2Right, it doesn’t look like a cold or the flu to me. While I’m taking your temperature, could you please tell me whether you have itchy or watery eyes?29
E3有呀。白天我的眼睛感觉更难受。除此之外，我胃口不错，睡眠也没问题。并无大碍。医生，我这是怎么了？我可真是受不了了。Yes, I do. My eyes get worse during the day. Other than this, I have a good appetite and can sleep well. There’s nothing major. Doctor, what is my problem? I really can’t stand this anymore.
M3I see. What you have now is an allergy that is commonly known as ‘hay fever’. It is triggered by an allergic reaction to pollen in the air, usually in spring and summer.33
E4哦，是这样。难怪我那天在后院修割草坪之后，觉得更严重了。这病严重吗？Oh, I see. No wonder I felt worse the other day after I mowed the grass in the backyard. Is this a very serious illness?
M4Mr. Lee, don’t worry. I can assure you that your condition is not unique. Hay fever is very common in Australia – about 3.2 million Australians suffer from chronic hay fever.30
E5你可不可以给我开点药呢？我想尽快康复。尽管你说了这病不要紧，可我还是觉得我很难集中精神。Can you give me some medicine? I want to recover from this as soon as possible. Even though you just mentioned that this is not a serious problem, I find it very hard to concentrate.
M5You don’t need any prescription for the medication. You can simply go to any chemist and buy some nasal spray or tablets over the counter to alleviate your symptoms.29
E6醫生，那你能否给我一些如何预防花粉症的信息呢？我不想再得这种病了。Doctor, could you please give me some information on preventing hay fever? I don’t want to suffer from this anymore.
M6Sure, there are some leaflets on preventing hay fever in the reception. You can take a copy with you when you leave.22

entry.title: 蛀牙手术
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between a general dentist Mr Bryant and a patient, Ms Gao, regarding her toothache caused by horizontal tooth impaction.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Ms Gao. What brings you here today?9
M1医生，早上好，我牙齿的 X 光的结果出来了吗? 上次检查，您说我是长智齿 了。我每天都牙疼得吃不下、睡不香，生活一团糟。Good morning, doctor. Have you received my dental X-ray results? Last time, you told me that I have a wisdom tooth coming out. I cannot eat or sleep well due to the pain. My life is just a mess!
E2I have examined your X-ray report, I can see that you do have one wisdom tooth at the back left corner of your mouth. It seems that it is causing the gum around the tooth to be infected, and adjacent teeth to decay also. We have to treat this matter seriously, otherwise you might end up with severe complications.59
M2您指的是哪些并发症？您认为我现在的牙周炎是由长智齿引起的么 ? 我 近来已经格外注意保持口腔卫生了，但似乎也没什么帮助。What kind of complications are you referring to? Do you think my current periodontitis was caused by this? I have been paying particular attention to keeping my mouth clean lately but it seems it hasn’t been much help.
E3The most common complications include erosion cavities, infections, crowding and cysts. Your wisdom tooth has stalled halfway, which makes brushing and flossing of it extremely difficult. Food and bacteria have built up around the neighbouring molars, causing decay and gum infection. I can imagine the enormous pain you are experiencing.50
M3是这样， 那麻烦您再给我开点抗生素和止痛药吧！上次的药， 我都已经用完 了。我每天都疼得受不了，而且越来越严重。怎么才能彻底让它不痛呢？That’s true. I have finished all the antibiotics and pain killers that you prescribed for me last time. Could you give me some more? This pain is unbearable and worsening each day. How can I completely get rid of this pain?
E4Unfortunately, although mouth infections such as periodontitis caused by wisdom teeth can be treated with antibiotics, the infection will re-occur unless the wisdom teeth are removed.26
M4一定要拔吗？虽然我身边的很多朋友都拔了，但我听说拔牙也可能引发许多后 期的问题，比如说牙齿渗血、出血，牙槽干燥，甚至神经损伤。如果真的是这 样，那听起来可太吓人了。Do I have to? Many of my friends have had their wisdom teeth extracted, but I was told that it could cause many post-extraction problems. For example, bleeding and oozing, dry sockets or even nerve injury. It sounds so terrible if that is true.
E5But I’m afraid it is the best option. As you can see from the X-ray, the emerging tooth is angled 90 degrees sideways, and growing into the roots of the second molar, which is generally known as horizontal tooth impaction. We will have to remove it.46
M5看来是别无选择了。你看我的下牙是不是都被挤歪了？我是不是还要戴牙套？ 我小的时候戴过好几年，但是总被人嘲笑，我可不想再经历一次了。那手术时 会不会很疼？It seems I have no other choice. Have you noticed that my lower teeth are crooked? Do you think I need to get braces? I wore them for years when I was little, and I was often laughed at because of that. I don’t want to go there again. Will I suffer a lot of pain in the surgery?
E6You will be under local anaesthesia during the surgery so you won’t feel any pain during the procedure. We can talk about orthodontic treatment after the surgery.27
M6那我就照您说的做吧。请您给我安排个手术时间。谢谢。Ok, then I will follow your instructions. Please schedule a time for my surgery. Thank you.

entry.title: 养老咨询
entry.content:
Mrs Hong, a mandarin speaking woman is consulting with Thomas, the manager of an aged care centre.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好！谢谢你抽出时间来跟我面谈。我今天来是想要了解一下你们这个养老院的一些大致情况。Hello, thank you for taking the time to see me. I’m here today to get some general information about your aged care centre.
M1Good morning! Thank you very much for showing your interest in our centre. Please have a seat. My name is Thomas, I’m more than happy to help you.28
E2是这样的。我已经 75 岁了。我打算从家里搬出来，因为我的儿子和儿媳妇不再愿意和我住在一起了，说是没有隐私了。我想了解一下你们这里不同房间的价格。Well, I’m 75 and I am planning to move out of my home, because my son and his wife don’t like living with me anymore. They said they need more privacy. I’d like to know the rates for different types of rooms here?
M2Sure. Actually it is a very wise decision to choose us as we have various types of rooms which come with a variety of rates so you can choose the one that best suits your needs. The most popular one is a studio.43
E3哦，我了解了。那么你们这里通常都是住着什么样的人呢？我希望我的左邻右舍都是很好心又乐于助人的人。人老了，就图个心情好，要是遇到一些不好的邻居，那就麻烦了。Oh, I see. So generally speaking, what kind of people are living here? I ‘d like to have some kind and warm-hearted neighbours. When you’re old, being happy is all one cares about. If my neighbours happen to be unfriendly, that would cause trouble.
M3Don’t worry. Our centre has been among the top ten aged care centres for five years in a row, and we are as careful in choosing our members as they are in choosing us. If there is any dispute, you can always refer it to our dispute mediation committee to have it solved fairly.54
E4听起来还不错，你们还有专门的调解委员会。那么我想问问你们对客户在健康方面的要求有什么特别设计的一些设施吗？Sounds great! So you have this particular mediation committee here. Also I’d like to know, do you have any specially designed facilities and support for members with special health needs?
M4Oh yes, we take our members’ health issues into consideration. We have a carer team to help people who have Alzheimer’s and dementia, and all the rooms are specifically designed for seniors, and include things such as automatic lighting systems and heating systems. Our members’ specific health requirements are seen as very important to us.55
E5我有健忘症，还有高血压，而且你也看到了，我是用轮椅的。你们这里有针对我这样情况的设施和护理人员吗？I have amnesia and hypertension. And as you can tell, I am a wheel chair user.Do you have any facilities or carers to help people in my kind of situation?
M5Amnesia and hypertension are common among seniors, and when we first designed this centre, we took this into consideration. We have a team of 16 experienced nurses to provide professional care. Also, our rooms are all purpose built, which means they are all wheel-chair accessible.45
E6这很好。那么关于心理方面，你们有相关的专业人员吗？你知道，老年人很容易感到孤单，被社会隔离等，这些负面的心理倾向往往会引发一些生理上的问题。That’s good. What about the mental health services? Do you have any professionals? You know seniors often feel lonely and socially isolated. These negative psychological tendencies often lead to mental problems
M6I understand your concern. We have our own counselling service centre and activity centre. Our professional counsellors are more than happy to help each of our members. Also, social activities such as shopping and seeing movies are organised on a monthly basis.42

entry.title: WE 短文写作
entry.content:

Argument


Introduction

The controversial topic with respect to __ 主题 __ never fails to attract public attention. There has been a heated debate on whether __ 论题 __ . As far as I am concerned, I tend to be an advocate/opponent (支持/反对) regarding this issue. My explanation will be illustrated in the following part of this essay.

Body (1)

正方论点: It is reasonable / evidently for me to believe that __ 论题 __ is of great importance due to the fact that __ 正方论点 __ . It can be seen that __ 现象 __ , which indicates that __ 本质 __ . As a result / Hence / Therefore, __ 重述论点 __ .

Body (2)

反方论点: In contrast, there are quite a few to hold the opinion that it is of less value to __ 反方论点 __ , (Opposite Point).

拆台: This argument does not hold water at all since / (However, this argument is not convincing because they) __ 诋毁 __ ,

吹牛: There is a finding conducted BY the researchers at the University of Oxford has figured out that __ 编数据 __ .

Conclusion

In a nutshell, I may safely reach the conclusion that __ 正方论点 __ based on the examples and results of the research.


Advantage & Disadvantage


Introduction

These days, there is an open debate on both pros and cons of __ 主题 __ . So many believe that __ 缺点 __ , while others may claim that it has some beneficial effects. From my perspective, __ 主题 __ have both positive and negative aspects, and I will explain my point of view from both sides in the following essay.

Body(1)

On one hand, __ 主题 __ are advantageous because __ 优点 __ . To be specific, __ 阐述优点 __ For example, __ 编故事__ , which makes __ 编数据 __ . As a result, the benefits of 主题 to __ 编的故事 __ is quite obvious.

Body(2)

On the other hand, those who argue against the notion claim that __ 缺点 __ . Specifically, although __ 阐述缺点 __ . For instance, __ 编故事__ . In brief, __ 主题 __ + __ 缺点 __

Conclusion

In Conclusion, since __ 主题 __ can both __ 优点 __ + __ 缺点 __ , there are both advantages and disadvantages of __ 主题 __ .

entry.title: PTE 首考回忆
entry.content:
考试时间： 9:00AM/05/Jun/2020,
考试地点： Melbourne RMIT English Worldwide

考场容纳20人， 键盘、耳机、显示器都很新, 配置和目前·RMIT·学校电脑一致， 另外印度人讲话声音很大， 导致答口语的时候很慌。


RA:

壹: Japanese Tea Ceremony 机经原题

        
        
The Japanese tea ceremony / is_a ritual tour influenced by Buddhism / in which green tea is prepared_and served / to a small group_of guests / in_a peaceful setting. The ceremony can take_as long as four hours / and there are many traditional gestures / that both the server and the guest must perform.

RS: 
NO.都是高频
1The lecture theatre one is located on the ground floor of the Pack Building.高频
2The office opens on Wednesday and Thursday.高频
3The university supplies a number of scholarship for (这一句说的是who have spesific goal) qualified students.高频
4In this library, the reserve collection of books can be borrowed for up to three hours高频
5Unfortunately, the two most interesting economic electives clash on my time table高频

DI:
壹: S&P/ASX 200 (数字完全一样，标题更详细) 机经题
贰: Earnings and Unemployment Rates by Educational Attainment 机经原题
叁: The Graduation Laboratory (图片更详细， 有电梯，有男女厕所) 机原题
肆: How Houseflies Work - Life Cycle (这道题是蚊子，不是苍蝇) 机经题

RL:
壹: The Fourth Dimensions 第四维
        
            
        
        
原文: 
Well, there’ s a number of ways to think about what dimensions are. I hope we all know where three dimensions are, which you can say are left, right; forward, backward; up, down. And if you think about it, three . we say there are three dimensions of space. And sometimes we need three coordinates to locate some objects in space. So, you can say longitude, latitude and altitude. So if there were more dimensions, you would need more coordinates. Now of course for whatever reason we are not physiologically designed to observe those dimensions, but that doesn’t mean they don’t exist. One way of thinking about it is, Maybe the best way of thinking about it is the way that someone named Edwin Abbott did it in the late 19th century in a book called flatland. And he said suppose there were two dimensional creatures living in a two dimensional universe. They would have the same trouble conceptualizing three dimensions that we have when we try to conceptualize more than three, such as four. And so, he asked questions like, “What would observers in this two dimensional universe see, say, if a three dimensional object like a sphere passed through the universe?” And what this flatland universe would see would be a series of disks that grow in size and then decreased in size. In the same way that we can certainly think about a two dimensional world inside a three dimensional world, it could be that we observe three dimensions but really there are more. And if a hyper sphere say a four dimensional sphere passed through our universe, we would see a series of spheres that grew in size and then decreased in size. The fact that we don’t observe those extra dimensions doesn’t mean they don’t exist. And they are hard to conceptualize. They certainly are hard to visualize. But we can think about them mathematically and conceptually without too much trouble.
参考答案:
NO.
1我们身处于四维空间。We are living in a four-dimension space.
2四维包括了经度、纬度、海拔和时间。Four dimensions include longitude, altitude, latitude, and time.
3我们可以用这四个维度来表示任何时间任何地点的事情。We can use the four dimensions to describe anything in space at any time.
4如果你想描述一件事件，你需要提供纬度、经度、时间和日期。If you want to describe an event, you need altitude, longitude, time and date.

贰: Survey on Media 网上调查
        
            
        
        
原文:
Let’s say if I’m asking which source do you often use to get information. Newspaper? Radio? And the survey shows 62% of the people chose internet. You might be thinking I am going to say, how important the internet is, or how quickly it has changed the world for a few years. But what if I tell you this survey is conducted on the website global and mail.com? Our answer will be different. Because the people who did this survey on a website must be frequent users of internet. This sample is a biased sample. So we have to pay attention to how a survey is conducted.
参考答案:
NO.
1如果我问你们喜欢从哪些渠道获取信息，报纸？收音机？电视？Let’s say if I’m asking which source do you often use to get information. Newspaper? Radio? TV?
2调查结果显示有62%的人选择网络。And the survey shows 62% of the people chose internet.
3你们可能以为我要说，网络的重要性， 或是网络短短几年快速地改变了我们的 世界。You might be thinking I am going to say, how important the internet is, or how quickly it has changed the world for a few year.
4但如果我告诉你们，这个调查是在网站globalandmail.com上做的呢？But what if I tell you this survey is conducted on the website globalandmail.com?
5我们的结论就不一样了。因为网站上回 答问题的人一定是经常使用网络的，这 个样本是一个偏性样本，不具有代表性。Our answer will be different. Because the people who did this survey on a website must be frequent users of internet. This sample is a biased sample.
6所以我们要注意调查问卷的操作过程。So we have to pay attention to how a survey is conducted.

ASQ:
NO.问:答:都是高频
1What do you throw underwater to keep ships staying on rivers or oceans without drifting away?Anchor高频
2If you want to study the human brain and behavior, what should you be?Psychologist高频
3How often does a biennial convention take place?Every two years高频
4What is one half of 100%？50%高频
5What do the following belong to: roses, daisies, tulips, +待定 etc?Flowers回忆

SWT:
壹: Moving from City back to Countryside 搬回农村
机经原题
I knew it was a good idea because I had been there before. Born and reared on a farm I had been seduced for a few years by the idea of being a big shot that lived and worked in a city rather than only going for the day to wave at the buses.
True, I was familiar with some of the minor disadvantages of country living such as an iffy private water supply sometimes infiltrated by a range of flora and fauna (including, on one memorable occasion, a dead lamb), the absence of central heating in farm houses and cottages, and a single track farm road easily blocked by snow, broken down machinery or escaped livestock.
But there were many advantages as I told Liz back in the mid Seventies. Town born and bred, eight months pregnant and exchanging a warm, substantial Corstorphine terrace for a windswept farm cottage on a much lower income, persuading her that country had it over town might have been difficult.
参考答案：
While living in the country has some minor disadvantages including water supply and the absence of central heating, the author still thinks it is a good idea to move back because he had been there before, but persuading Liz back to country is hard because she would not exchange a warm terrace for a windswept farm cottage on a much lower income.

贰: Frog amber 青蛙琥珀
机经原题
A miner in the state of Chiapas found a tiny tree frog that has been preserved in amber for 25 million years, a researcher said.If authenticated, the preserved frog would be the first of its kind found in Mexico, according to David Grimaldi, a biologist and curator at the American Museum of Natural History, who was not involved in the find.
The chunk of amber containing the frog, less than half an inch long, was uncovered by a miner in Mexico’s southern Chiapas state in 2005 and was bought by a private collector, who loaned it to scientists for study.A few other preserved frogs have been found in chunks of amber — a stone formed by ancient tree sap — mostly in the Dominican Republic. Like those, the frog found in Chiapas appears to be of the genus Craugastor, whose descendants still inhabit the region, said biologist Gerardo Carbot of the Chiapas Natural History and Ecology Institute. Carbot announced the discovery this week.
The scientist said the frog lived about 25 million years ago, based on the geological strata where the amber was found. Carbot would like to extract a sample from the frog’s remains in hopes of finding DNA that could identify the particular species but doubts the owner would let him drill into the stone.
参考答案：
While a miner in Mexico found a tiny tree frog preserved in amber for 25 million years, the preserved frog would be the first of its kind found in Mexico, and a few other preserved frogs have been found in chunks of amber, so Carbot would like to extract a sample from the frog’s remains to find DNA that could identify the particular species but doubts the owner would let him drill into the stone.

WE:
壹: 研究气候的某方面
机经原题
You are given climate change as the field of study. Which area would you prefer? Explain why you picked this up the particular area of your study, and give an example in the area you pick.

贰: 一道新体不在机经
新题
大概问 humanity 是否被 （[重要/核心?]） Professional([??产业])Industry - such as Medical 所 怎么样（[一个E开头的]）

RO:
081Monash Student Ne Tan 真题
1Mechanical engineering student Ne Tan is spending the first semester of this year studying at the University of California, Berkeley as part of the Monash Abroad program.
2Ne, an international student from Shanghai, China, began her Monash journey at Monash College in October 2006.
3There she completed a diploma that enabled her to enter Monash University as a secondyear student.
4Now in her third year of study, the Monash Abroad program will see her complete four units of study in the US before returning to Australia in May 2009.

FIB(W&R)
壹: Australian Women Novelist
真题
In the literary world, it was an accepted assumption that the 1970s was a time of unprecedented growth in homegrown Australian fiction. And everybody was reading and talking about books by young Australian women. But it was not until recently that a researcher was able to measure just how many novels were published in that decade, and she found that there had been a decline in novels by Australian writers overall, but confirmed an increase in women’s novels. It is this sort of research - testing ideas about literary history - that is becoming possible with the spread of ‘Digital Humanities.’ The intersection of Humanities and digital technologies is opening up opportunities in the fields of literature, linguistics, history and language that [were not possible] without computational methods and digitized resources to bring information together in an accessible way. Transcription software is being developed for turning scans of books and documents into text, as the field of digital humanities really takes off.


贰: Energy Expenditure & Active Video Games [Version 1] 
回忆
EE (energy expenditure) and AVG (active video games) ……The sedentary video games cannot meet the minimum exercise intensities…… sedentary behaviors …
… time spent engaged in activities…… Playing AVGs increases energy expenditure, but can’t be a replacement of exercise….
叁: Good Schools Guide
真题
A big rise in state schools rated among the best institutions in the country is revealed in the latest edition of the Good Schools Guide. Middle-class parents facing financial pressures in the downturn are increasingly looking beyond the private sector to educate their children. The 23-year-old Good Schools Guide – a popular reference book for fee-paying families set on the best private school – has increased the number of state schools in this year’s edition to 251, pushing the figure to more than a quarter of its 1,000 entries for the first time. Explaining why the guide has more than doubled the number of schools it features outside the private sector in only five years, Sue Fieldman, regional editor, told the Financial Times: “The parents we speak to want more information on the state sector and the best it has to offer.


SST
壹: Drug Advertisements 药品广告 
05/Jun/202025/Dec/2019
The amount of money drug companies spend on TV ads has doubled recently, and studies show that commercials work. Although information in the ads was technically accurate, the tone was misleading. The ads portrayed that the character’s life was out of control before taking the drug but magically regained complete control after taking the prescriptions, without mentioning lifestyle changes could help. Buying prescription medications is not like buying soaps.
贰: Cognition 认知
回忆
Brain (Pace不是speed) We can process information quickly in childhood. Cognition is not about speed but is about accuracy.  (& 5000 Year ancestor…) According to the information we received, the resources we used are as limited as gas to cars. However, the situation will be different if gas is unlimited for us.

WFD
NO.
1The connect is to define the combination of math and philopyh.新增06/Jun29/May
2University Scholar target students who have specific goals回忆06/Jun
3The nation achieved prosperity by opening its ports/exports for trade.高频21/May06/Jun

entry.title: SST 录音总结
entry.content:
Summarize Spoken Test .

共 2-3 题



高频


001. Sound Receptors 声音接收器

高频预测:06/2020

准确原文：

You’ve got sound receptors in your ear, and they are beautiful. We’re not going to talk about them at any length, but there’s little flappy, these little spiky things going along in your ear and they can translate vibrational energy coming from your ear, hurting your eardrum, being translated into a vibration into the fluid in your ear into a physical motion of these little receptors there into an electrical motion, into an electrical signal that goes into your ear. So, all of that, all of that’s pretty impressive stuff. We are not going to talk about the details of it, but I invite some of you who want to learn more about this, particularly MIT students who would find receptors quite remarkable kinds of devices.

参考答案：

Sound receptors are small devices that are flappy and spiky in the ears. These receptors can translate vibrational energy coming from your ear, which is hurting your eardrum, into a vibration into the fluid, then this physical motion of receptors will be translated into electrical motion and electrical signals that go into your ear. MIT students are invited to learn more about sound receptors, who would find sound receptors remarkable. [2019.11.20 更新]



002. British Environmental Law 英国环境法

Before we understand international environmental laws, we should understand the domestic regulations in the UK first. Introduced by Charles II, the first environmental law in the UK was ineffective due to the lack of enforcement despite showing a good intention. During the Industrial Revolution, some industrialists used the Adam Smith’s model to maximize the benefit, which led to the need for statutory controls on the discharge of pollutants.



005. A Woman Novelist 女小说家

高频预测:06/2020

准确原文：

I have been writing non-fiction for years, and secretly wanting to be a novelist. When I first started writing at the age of 30, it was with the intention of writing fiction, but I took a little detour for 10 or 12 years, and write non-fiction which I absolutely have no regret about at all. I think it’s exactly the right thing for me to do, but there’s this dream tucked away inside of me to do this.

Now I remember reading something that Eudora Welty wrote, who is a great novelist from Mississippi who had a big influence on me actually. She said, “no art ever came out of not risking your neck.” And I think she’s absolutely right. It felt that way to me at the time; it actually feels that way every time I sit down to write something.

Finally, in the early 90s, I took my deep breath and started writing fiction. It felt risky to me at the time to do that. And one of the very first things I wrote was, what I thought was going to be the first chapter of a novel, called “The Secret Life of Bees.” I wrote it in 1992, and it is actually essentially the first chapter of the novel as it is now.

参考答案：

The speaker wrote non-fiction for years but secretly wanted to be a novelist. Although she took a detour in writing non-fiction, she had no regret at all because she thinks it was the right thing to do. Later she was inspired by a female novelist and started to write fiction. Finally, she wrote the first chapter of her novel named “The Secret Life of Bees.”



007. Citizenship curriculum 公民教育

高频预测:06/2020

准确原文：

Last month I published, alongside my annual report, a subject report on the development of citizenship in schools. The report celebrates the success of some schools in implementing the citizenship curriculum. It praises those schools where there has been substantial developments in the subject and which now go a long way towards fulfilling national curriculum requirements. In the report, we are critical of schools which have not taken citizenship seriously, either through reluctance or lack of capacity to make appropriate provision in the curriculum.

Citizenship is marginalized in the curriculum in one-fifth of schools. It is less well established in the curriculum than other subjects, and less well taught and some critics have seized on this as a reason for wanting to step back from supporting it.

Yet, the progress made to date by the more committed schools suggests that the reasons for introducing citizenship are both worthwhile and can be fulfilled, given the time and resources. Indeed, those reasons are given added weight by national and global events of the past few months. While not claiming too much, citizenship can address core skills, attitudes, and values that young people need to consider as they come to terms with a changing world.

The main problems standing in the way of the implementation of citizenship continue to be: the lack of commitment on the part of many school leaders; an insufficient amount of initial and

in-service training provision to ensure that every school can call upon teachers with subject expertise; and its uncertain place in the curriculum.

参考答案：

While some schools have successfully implemented the citizenship curriculum, some schools failed to take the citizenship curriculum seriously due to the lack of commitment from the leaders, insufficient teacher training and its uncertain place in the curriculum. From the experience of those more committed schools, introducing citizenship is both worthwhile and can be fulfilled, because citizenship can address core skills, attitudes and values among young people in a changing world.



009. Biology, DNA & RNA 生物与DNA

高频预测:06/2020

准确原文：

Now, the study of biology is responsible for some of the most profound insights that humans have, about the world around them. So, take a look at these four panoramas. In the upper left, you see some bacteria this happen to be equal line, you obviously see a butterfly, a flower, a dolphin. If you see that at the outer space, just looks these different forms and structures. You have no idea that they were all related to one to another. So one of the most profound thing that biology told us is that all life on earth is exceptionally related similar to one to another. So, for

2. British Environmental Law 英国环境法

准确原文：

Before we consider international environmental law and climate change we need to consider domestic legislation, as it is within the sovereign states that international law is put into practice. This reflects the environmentalists’ maxim, ‘think globally, act locally.’

United Kingdom legislative control over the impacts of mans’ activity on the environment is not new. As long ago as the reign of Charles II the main concern was the production of smoke from the burning of ‘sea coal. Almost all areas of trade and industry were subject to very detailed legislative controls at that time, although some were governed by ‘self-regulation’ in the form of guilds, which regulated both supply and methods of production. However, the measures implemented were mostly ineffective because then, as now, the specifying of legal duties and standards without providing any appropriate enforcement merely indicated good intentions but

were of little practical effect.

The next stage was prompted by the Industrial Revolution with the urbanization of society and its profound effects on the environment. Local industrialists used the Adam Smith model to maximize their economic benefit, but this was to the detriment of the local environment with the operation of ‘Gresham’s Law’ that is, the bad drives out the good.

Those industrialists who were concerned for either the health of their employees or the local environment faced higher costs than their competitors. The result was the need for increasingly comprehensive statutory controls on the discharge of pollutants into various receiving media.

参考答案：

Before we understand international environmental laws, we should understand the domestic legislations in the UK first. Introduced by Charles II, the first environmental law in the UK was ineffective due to the lack of enforcement despite showing good intention. During the Industrial Revolution, some industrialists used Adam Smith’s model to maximize the benefit, which led to the need for statutory controls on the discharge of pollutants.

6. A Mother’s Student Loan 学生贷款

「萤火虫教研与自习室高分学员共同确认」

准确原文：

I’m 43 years old, and I owe tens of thousands of dollars in student loans. Oh sure, I knew the loans were piling up as I went through school. But with one loan coming from here, another from there, I had no idea of the rockslide that was building.

Fifteen years later, I still experience moments of sheer horror regarding my family’s financial situation. My monthly student loan payment is more than triple my car payment.

OK, so without my college degree, I would not have been able to get my current job. For that I’m grateful, but at what cost?

My loans have been accruing at a rate of 10 percent, and now they have burgeoned to — well, I’m English major, you do the math. I don’t think they’ll ever get paid off. We’re in debt way past our eyeballs, and there’s no hope in sight.

I’m being kept in class — a financial class of graduates whose only hope for attending college meant borrowing money from the government. Because of our mounting credit card debt and monthly payments that far exceed our family’s income; my kids will also join the class of citizens who can’t rely on their parents for college support. Do I wish I’d chosen another educational route? You bet. Perhaps trade school — I’ve thought that being a plumber might not be such a bad gig.

But if your job aspirations require a four-year degree, take my advice and choose a college you can afford, both during and after graduation. Take a realistic look at your anticipated income and factor in priorities that don’t carry a price — like a spouse and children you might want to have someday.

I was overconfident that my student-loan debt would pale in comparison to the lucrative writing

career I’d enjoy after graduation. Now I’m paying for that decision — in more ways than I’d ever imagined.

参考答案：

A 43 years old woman is still paying her student loan fifteen years after she graduated from college, where she got her college degree and without which she would not have her current job. Due to her financial burden, her children cannot rely on her for a college education. And she advised students to choose the college they can afford and take a realistic look at their anticipated income.

14. Vitamin D 维他命 D [V2：紫外线防晒霜]

准确原文：

The body also manufactures vitamin D from cholesterol, through a process triggered by the action of sunlight on skin, hence its nickname, “the sunshine vitamin.” Yet some people do not make enough vitamin D from the sun, among them, people who have a darker skin tone, who are overweight, who are older, and who cover up when they are in the sun. Correctly applied sunscreen reduces our ability to absorb vitamin D by more than 90 percent. And not all sunlight is created equal: The sun’s ultraviolet B (UVB) rays—the so-called “tanning” rays, and the rays that trigger the skin to produce vitamin D are stronger near the equator and weaker at higher latitudes. So in the fall and winter, people who live at higher latitudes (in the northern U.S. and Europe, for example) can’t make much of any vitamin D from the sun.

The body can manufacture vitamin D from cholesterol through sunlight. But people with a darker skin tone, who are overweight, older or wearing sunscreen cannot make enough vitamin D. The UVB rays in sunlight can trigger the skin to produce vitamin D, which are stronger near the equator and weaker at higher latitudes. Thus, people who live at higher latitudes can’t make much vitamin D from the sun.
